name: Aadland For Congress
subject: You're invited to a Brew-Pub Party
body:
Join Erik in Golden this Wednesday
| | | |
---
| Hello Peter,
You are invited to a Brew-Pub hosted Meet & Greet Petition Party in Golden this Wednesday!
Erik will speak for a bit, then spend time meeting you, answering questions and talking about the upcoming campaign for CD7.
If you are a resident of CD7 (we can help find out if you're unsure) please plan to sign the petition to get Erik onto the primary ballot.
Also, you can learn how to help the campaign while enjoying Over Yonder Brewing's fine craft beers.
---
| |
---
|
# Wednesday January 19th at 7:00PM
---
|
---
|
---
If you are a resident of U.S. House District 7 (CD7) please plan to sign the petition
---
| |
---
|
---

❌ Democrats have failed the economy.
❌ They've failed with inflation.
❌ They've failed with Afghanistan.
❌ They've failed with COVID.
❌ They've failed with "Build Back Broke."

_Do I need to keep going?_

**They know the ship is sinking and their last resort is to pass election rigging schemes. name: NRSC HQ
subject: Survey: Are Democrats hypocrites?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
| Fellow patriot,

We’ve witnessed a lot of EMPTY talk by Democrat leader Chuck Schumer since the new year began.

**[Schumer realizes that his opportunity to make his mark on the Senate is fleeting and he’s desperate](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=143277e0d6d14c2584240b75a7d87c91&_e=I3uGq9OwsP7Kbv-VkCui7uAJREb9umBUp_2nE8nEj-XbuUIawqm52J0oEMV5Mu3Qte5rLroTWcX4Oq0eizbAaOs4sFVOonECTL0o7iWwnfxgAoTXSCbILrZenAPBvQ-pWjW0PxnEhAF5sptPJoj8H09Swu4dhB3ZYxVzskW-yjQUzFxFyrV_CjHxkO1YAvFCdKP-UeYLqtQ7xA0DVK3hwTa-umG4bkAUjy6lTI7swWRo2awFUEN7SKH655YKwFx2u5vz3taI4qRX3oktpd1rhI3jNkwnVNteqYOcNaB9yfUk8Bz3mL6Bckeg9PhtAwwAoTyj6teolCY27eO25BFETSKdvOkOo7cLzx8Jg5Ea4ZjO4l1C7gX_oTKeikldobMxn-y3-9GUt29UjxsBo4pWCk5gGgjuFJ-koXJz2e00UQ7GHVjXFU6eXIh7-Ul9nQOzppV-lP9M0Co9YnX8tEmE9FszGz1meFe9vd5hF5zJKTifPrjUlOhOIlI4nSecFZQd)** to preserve his tenuous majority by rewriting the rules in the middle of the. Seeing the prospect for Republicans to retake the majority in November, he wants to nuke the filibuster and federalize election law.

That’s a scary prospect. With a simple majority of DEM senators voting in the affirmative, Schumer could nationalize elections.

**We want to know:** Do you trust Schumer and DEMS to have your best interest in mind when they rewrite the rules of voting?

name: NRSC HQ
subject: Is Chuck Schumer a hypocrite?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
| Fellow patriot,

We’ve witnessed a lot of EMPTY talk by Democrat leader Chuck Schumer since the new year began.

**[Schumer realizes that his opportunity to make his mark on the Senate is fleeting and he’s desperate](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be60fa32ff8f4a8a94e45a06bff476f1&_e=j4JMIQM2JP8et7OhYrjW2fN59h_lZYZqCsSRwG8tF3sOX6UaVXPUkEFS_7tCg_X9A6iru6BrL19qjSz-eY6k_rt6ckATFxDg_e4NoMxcuhJ__VufgKKDtGjoHgkCrRaurB7vaR4iLxMr2dP-kUN8j7uv1ifV_h9VWKcYYI4vmVwBlg0ehjiyXsSiBHtyjjZL4Ccbb3W4PpCAHjjWfw4ibQgbL44few4E30io0YSicn2xS37EnGGQhLfwAfFpQvdX4ZxzTQ8tJQleixrYLBDPbjt_BAGyKycB3-IrSpJRVOk-XC1JZzF4-bPWmVUFDUwO3mK4s3wEONaRicoN_4eJyPYPJB0XoSAH4pF5lWUHR_nna0ENXD1SQeIvwopWCBb4e6u3Xy39KOBiDeYtcVEyIe19S03LfL6M7KqtRannsqNeHETagmLkfrzxVwBoTijOKi361-dejnHhZFStwBd6yoW4yvH4ymBJ_AYIQlvlS4o%3D)** to preserve his tenuous majority by rewriting the rules in the middle of the. Seeing the prospect for Republicans to retake the majority in November, he wants to nuke the filibuster and federalize election law.

That’s a scary prospect. With a simple majority of DEM senators voting in the affirmative, Schumer could nationalize elections.

**We want to know:** Do you trust Schumer and DEMS to have your best interest in mind when they rewrite the rules of voting?

[** DO YOU TRUST DEMS WITH VOTING REFORM
LET US KNOW ASAP->**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be60fa32ff8f4a8a94e45a06bff476f1&_e=j4JMIQM2JP8et7OhYrjW2fN59h_lZYZqCsSRwG8tF3sOX6UaVXPUkEFS_7tCg_X9A6iru6BrL19qjSz-eY6k_rt6ckATFxDg_e4NoMxcuhJ__VufgKKDtGjoHgkCrRaurB7vaR4iLxMr2dP-kUN8j7uv1ifV_h9VWKcYYI4vmVwBlg0ehjiyXsSiBHtyjjZL4Ccbb3W4PpCAHjjWfw4ibQgbL44few4E30io0YSicn2xS37EnGGQhLfwAfFpQvdX4ZxzTQ8tJQleixrYLBDPbgeLGxqKVqEGjBo4ps7BMm1h9vVJU93OFVcGIOXgyrSpJwsDqcAiLHtgRPW6WvtdvpsTzHBoqy7ffPpiX5RUMAu_iNmSt2AgO_Dpe18KIx9QeRtw307M_c79jV2kbcYgUWtDOgALhBgXU1wGKXC84-PFbJucavo8PWaHQBxC3waHdNGHj4WkxCnqztt-0C1tGXVIX4RwZIt7OrMgdUP4hX8%3D)

Please take 10 seconds to let us know today. **[It’s vital that we hear from you.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be60fa32ff8f4a8a94e45a06bff476f1&_e=j4JMIQM2JP8et7OhYrjW2fN59h_lZYZqCsSRwG8tF3sOX6UaVXPUkEFS_7tCg_X9A6iru6BrL19qjSz-eY6k_rt6ckATFxDg_e4NoMxcuhJ__VufgKKDtGjoHgkCrRaurB7vaR4iLxMr2dP-kUN8j7uv1ifV_h9VWKcYYI4vmVwBlg0ehjiyXsSiBHtyjjZL4Ccbb3W4PpCAHjjWfw4ibQgbL44few4E30io0YSicn2xS37EnGGQhLfwAfFpQvdX4ZxzTQ8tJQleixrYLBDPblVFZY3NGXeHYaFxUtvtC4SdJHIetcym5ljZ3R65J8iZxASFbmrbHq1nF3fp9fA554qVDXm2luZkRmcHY_Jv2bGErMup2WMHjxgB_Cn5uyDvxKTQVrwbUS8u03KZ0J01VBpOcuabohaHNxUBgsjPaZUCTWJs11to7GC4iIokF-gykpX6PBsJfzAmlToPvrFEBb9sXwHigvcehtK_mP3EMFw%3D) **

name: Greg Lopez for Governor
subject: Words of True Wisdom
body:
Celebrating Dr. Martin Luther King
| | | |

---

---
---
Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. taught us so many things about civil rights, courage, and our own nature. He once so brilliantly stated, "Darkness cannot drive out darkness: only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate: only love can do that." Then, he followed it up by being the light and love. Leaders can learn a lot from someone who made revolutionary statements and then lived up to his rhetoric.
Today we get to remember some of the wonderful gifts left to us by Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. There are so few who leave us with such great wisdom and guidance. Together, we can embody some of that wisdom here in Colorado.
It may seem dark for some of us, but Dr. King had an answer for that, too, when he stated, "But I know, somehow, that only when it is dark enough can you see the stars." We have a chance at a new day this November, to take this great wisdom and courage and apply it to our state government.
Let us stand together as one Colorado for all Colorado.

---
---
---
|
---
| | | | | | | Greg Lopez for Governor | P.O. Box 1413, Parker, CO 80134

name: Geoff Diehl
subject: Reminder: Join Me and Run for Office!
body:
2022 is the Time to Stand Up
| | | |
---
---
---
| Friends,
There's a runaway train of progressive ideas in our state. It's leading us far in the wrong direction, causing the cost of goods to rise and parents to be shut out of their children's education. It's time for candidates who care about the future of our state to stand up and run for office.
If you run in 2022 as a Republican for State Representative, State Senate, and municipal offices like City Council, Selectman, and School Committee, you'll be joining forces with the Massachusetts Republican Party and the Diehl for Governor campaign to run as a TEAM.
Now is the time! In 2010, in the mid-point of President Obama's first term, Massachusetts Republicans picked up 19 seats in the state Legislature, and elected Scott Brown as our U.S. Senator. We can repeat this success, and increase those numbers, in 2022. But I can't do it alone; I need your help.
Please join me and run for office. Attend one of the MassGOP's upcoming training sessions for candidates, and learn more about how to run successfully for office:

* Jan. 15 (Saturday), Leominster Eagles, 456 Litchfield St., 9:30 a.m.

* Jan. 17 (Monday), VFW Billerica, 12 Phiney St., 6:30 p.m.

* Jan. 22 (Saturday), Rhumb Line Bar & Restaurant, Gloucester, 9:30 a.m.

* Jan. 24 (Monday), Fall River VFW, 6:30 p.m.

* Jan. 29 (Saturday), Monument Beach Sportsmens Club, Buzzards Bay, 9:30 a.m.

* Jan. 31 (Monday), Italian American Vets WW of Milford, 6:30 p.m.

* Feb. 7 (Monday), Grace Baptist Church, Attleboro, 6:30 p.m.

* Feb. 15 (Tuesday), Watertown Marine Corps League, 6:30 p.m.

* Feb. 22 (Tuesday), Disabled American Veterans Hall, Braintree, 6:30 p.m.

* Feb. 28 (Monday), Worcester Marine Corps League, 6:30 p.m.

name: Dr. Bob Lorinser
subject: Friends, MLKJr's legacy in Michigan reminds us voting rights must be protected
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE FRIENDS OF DR
body:
Friends , did you know Dr. King first gave his "I Have a Dream" speech right here, in our state? **Dr. King first gave his "I Have a Dream" speech in Michigan** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760167/325818435/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=) **_June 23, 1963 is an important date in Michigan history. It's the day Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. delivered his famous speech that would ring through the annals of time._** [CONTRIBUTE FOR VOTING RIGHTS](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760168/325818436/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760169/325818437/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=) [ON-CLICK IMMEDIATE $19.63 DONATION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760170/325818438/1277889215?express_lane=true&amount=19.63&refcode=express_lane_19.63&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=) DETROIT, Mich. – Dr. King is most revered for his role in the March on Washington in 1963, where he delivered his “I Have a Dream”, but it wasn’t the first time he had spoken those famous four words. Two months before King spoke in front of the Lincoln Memorial on August 28, 1963, he gave another impassioned speech at the Walk to Freedom in Detroit on June 23. The Walk to Freedom was the largest civil rights demonstration to date at the time and was called “one of the most wonderful things that has happened in America” by King himself. Michigan's Walk to Freedom commemorated 20 years since the Detroit race riot of 1943 when over two dozen people were killed and many more injured. King was joined by Rev. C.L. Franklin, the father of Aretha Franklin, and the Mayor of the city Jerome Cavanagh. Over 125,000 people demonstrated with Dr. King down Woodward Avenue to Cobo Arena (TCF Center). The Walk to Freedom's main purpose "was to speak out against segregation and the brutality that met civil rights activists in the South while at the same time addressing concerns of African Americans in the urban North: inequality in hiring practices, wages, education, and housing." [ | | |
---|---|--- ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760171/325818439/-803653937?v=cZbvdMQGitE&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=&emci=db628609-8b77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=2016077b-8e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15167427)

Another notable connection to Michigan – During the tragic day of Dr. King's assassination, Dr. William Anderson of Detroit was on the phone with Coretta Scott King when it happened.

“She used to call me Andy,” Anderson said.

“I was at home in Detroit, sitting with my wife and kids, and the message came on the air, that Martin had been assassinated in Memphis by an unknown assailant. I was on the phone, talking to Coretta. She said, ‘Wait a minute, Andy — something has happened to Martin.'

"She never came back to the phone.”
|
---

**_Dr. King's legacy reminds us of the sanctity of our right to vote – that without constant vigilance, democracy is still under threat. Voting rights must be protected. This is not a partisan issue. It's a civil rights issue._**

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760172/325818440/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=)
**I will work tirelessly to protect voting rights for all Americans**
WASHINGTON, D.C.. — Last year, the Senate could not advance important Voting Rights legislation. Three times in the past year my opponent, Jack Bergman, stood against voting rights, and never honored the will of the people in last November's election.

Senate Republicans blocked the John Lewis Voting Rights Act from advancing when the Senate took a procedural vote on whether to open debate on the legislation.

In October, the 'Freedom to Vote Act' was blocked from making its way for debate on the Senate floor. This was a compromised, scaled-back version of the**** For The People Act that passed the House without my opponent's vote.

* The Freedom to Vote Act would have:
* Automated voter registration through the DMV
* Declared Election Day a public holiday
* Allowed 15 days of early voting for federal elections
* Curtailed partisan gerrymandering
* Enforced stronger FEC campaign finance laws
* Required Super-PACs to report their donors

The bill would have protected our elections from interference, dark money, partisan gerrymandering, and voter suppression. It also included compromised provisions clarifying state voter ID laws.

Voter suppression benefits no one. I may disagree with a voter’s vote and be displeased with an election result. However, no one has a right to suppress votes or call our free and fair democratic election invalid. As we move forward to advance our union, we should ensure safe, secure, and fair elections are accessible to as many eligible voters as possible.

Additionally, as recently as Aug. 20 my opponent [**continues to stand behind his vote**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760173/325818441/1879348302?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=&emci=db628609-8b77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=2016077b-8e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15167427) against certifying the 2020 election, referencing Antrim County‘s alleged discrepancies. Arizona — a state in which my opponent objected to election results — conducted a GOP-led audit. It confirmed the vote was accurate. Because this result was politically inconvenient for my opponent, now he is calling the [**Big Lie a distraction.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760174/325818442/1116769814?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=&emci=db628609-8b77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=2016077b-8e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15167427)

My opponent twice tried to invalidate a free and fair election, attempts that have been proven over and over again to be unjustified and ill-intended.

A Republican-led / bipartisan Michigan Senate Oversight Committee found no evidence of voter fraud. There is no reputable evidence of significant voter or election fraud, as certified by all 50 states’ Republican and Democrat election committees. Challenges to the validity of the election are refuted, and many court challenges support the integrity of the 2020 election.

**Here are the facts:**

In 2016, our country elected Donald Trump in a fair and free election. In 2020 we elected Joe Biden. We are a democracy, and we need to allow the people’s will—and the votes they cast—to guide us.

As a nation, we should collectively want all eligible voters to vote. Unfortunately, on average, [**just over half of all eligible voters exercise this right**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760175/325818443/-748158625?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=&emci=db628609-8b77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=2016077b-8e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15167427). We must support any efforts to register eligible voters and remove any barriers for American citizens to exercise their rights.

We need to recognize and promote multiple methods of casting a ballot. In-person, mail-in, and absentee ballots are all valid and secure.

Despite the constant rhetoric coming from opponents to democracy, we have and will continue to have elections absent of widespread voter fraud, but continued investment in safeguarding our elections against any foreign influence is vital.

Gerrymandering to support one candidate over another is not who we are, and we must seek to end this practice. I support independent, non-partisan redistricting committees to evaluate maps and redraw them fairly and accurately. Maps should not give an unfair advantage to any party, Democratic or Republican.

Strengthening our governmental ethics and campaign finance rules to protect our democracy is necessary.

Our system is not perfect, and I support improvements to our election. The House has passed two bills that would protect our democracy and the right to vote, both would bring necessary improvements and enhancements to our system:

In March, the For The People Act, H.R. 1 passed the House. My opponent did not vote on the bill. The bill is designed to improve access, promote integrity, and ensure the security of our elections. **If I were in Congress as your Representative, I would have voted for this bill.**

On August 24, 2021, my opponent voted against the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act, H.R. 4. This bill aims to protect the right to vote and would strengthen the Voting Rights Act of 1965. It would make any voter restriction efforts more difficult for states to implement in the future. **If I were in Congress as your Representative, I would have voted for this bill.**

My opponent voted against certifying a fair election. He did not cast a vote on the For the People Act, and he voted no on the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act.

These are three positions that don’t reflect and represent our district, nor do they promote the will of the people. I fundamentally disagree with my opponent because our democracy is stronger when it is accessible to as many eligible voters as possible.

I'm humbly [**asking for your support**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760176/325818444/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=) in making this reality when honored to be a Representative for Northern Michigan and the UP.

Thank you for your consideration.

Yours,

-Dr. Bob

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40760177/325818445/1277889215?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE2MDc3Yi04ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bN6ZpBcLVY8b5K2bCSJT8tMFzgz-atEY1qUPC-0UHtc=)

Dr. King first gave his "I Have a Dream" speech in Michigan = June 23, 1963 is an important date in Michigan history. It's the day Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. delivered his famous speech that would ring through the annals of time. = = = DETROIT, Mich. – Dr. King is most revered for his role in the March on Washington in 1963, where he delivered his “I Have a Dream”, but it wasn’t the first time he had spoken those famous four words. Two months before King spoke in front of the Lincoln Memorial on August 28, 1963, he gave another impassioned speech at the Walk to Freedom in Detroit on June 23. The Walk to Freedom was the largest civil rights demonstration to date at the time and was called “one of the most wonderful things that has happened in America” by King himself. Michigan's Walk to Freedom commemorated 20 years since the Detroit race riot of 1943 when over two dozen people were killed and many more injured. King was joined by Rev. C.L. Franklin, the father of Aretha Franklin, and the Mayor of the city Jerome Cavanagh. Over 125,000 people demonstrated with Dr. King down Woodward Avenue to Cobo Arena (TCF Center). The Walk to Freedom's main purpose "was to speak out against segregation and the brutality that met civil rights activists in the South while at the same time addressing concerns of African Americans in the urban North: inequality in hiring practices, wages, education, and housing." Another notable connection to Michigan – During the tragic day of Dr. King's assassination, Dr. William Anderson of Detroit was on the phone with Coretta Scott King when it happened. “She used to call me Andy,” Anderson said. “I was at home in Detroit, sitting with my wife and kids, and the message came on the air, that Martin had been assassinated in Memphis by an unknown assailant. I was on the phone, talking to Coretta. She said, ‘Wait a minute, Andy — something has happened to Martin.' name: Andy Beshear
subject: Dr. King's vision
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Today we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and his vision to create a country committed to justice, equality, and fairness for all people. Peter,

Today we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and his vision to create a country committed to justice, equality, and fairness for all people.

In 1964, 10,000 Kentuckians marched alongside Dr. King in Frankfort, calling for an end to discrimination and segregation in our Commonwealth. That march helped lead to Kentucky signing the first state civil rights act in the South.

We've come a long way since then in the fight for civil rights, but there is still so much more to do. As Governor, I'm committed to building a more equitable and fair Commonwealth for every Kentuckian.

This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we as Kentuckians must come together and remember that we can overcome our divisions. We have much more in common than that which divides us.

Let us never lose sight of the work we must do to honor Dr. King's vision for a better future.

Thank you,

Andy

| PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
---
Andy Beshear for Governor
PO Box 4278
Louisville, KY 40204
United States
---
If you no longer wish to receive emails from us, you can [unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/4fa915fc-8775-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442/4230ef78-9777-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjMwZWY3OC05Nzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DlQL_vDxrSu8n8f_BbGw17A-FTFJOm4bKDrK7Uj-xzg=&id=102280162).
---

name: Victoria Virasingh
subject: Honoring Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Victoria for Congress
body:
[https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif] https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=220105_FR_newyearnewemail_LOGO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=220105_FR_newyearnewemail_LOGO] [https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif]
The late Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. left a legacy that amplified our country’s values. Many of us find inspiration in his speeches, journals, peaceful advocacy and his life as a public servant.
The life of a public servant focuses on making our communities stronger for everyone, no matter their background, and shows that a single person can make a difference. Through his life, he has motivated so many people over decades to serve their community too.
I feel privileged to be able to be your candidate for Virginia’s 8th Congressional District. Your support continues to be a source of great and infinite hope. I believe in the impact we can have when we work together. To paraphrase Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., " together, we spread the light of infinite hope throughout our community."
To honor Dr. King’s words today and every day, I will continue working to better our community with hope, empathy, and compassion.
Your support means everything. Thank you!
Victoria Virasingh
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/91626/images/Screen%20Shot%202021-11-24%20at%201.45.13%20PM.png]
Victoria Virasingh is the daughter of immigrants. She grew up housing insecure and watched her working parents struggle to pull their family out of poverty. But today, even those opportunities to work hard and live the American dream are becoming less and less attainable.
That's why Victoria is running for Congress in Virginia's 8th District -- to create equitable opportunities for those who have been left behind, to expand access for the store owner to the refugee, to support our struggling elders and students, and to lift up working families.
Victoria is a fearless progressive champion who will fight for bold policies to improve the daily lives of Virginians, and she's bringing the fresh new leadership that we need to move our state forward.
Pitch in to help elect Victoria Virasingh as the next voice of VA-08 >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=220105_FR_newyearnewemail_BIO]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=220105_FR_newyearnewemail_BIO]

name: Peter Franchot
subject: Honoring Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/FAWlogo_Blue%404x.png]Dear Peter,
Today, we honor the incredible life and legacy of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Nearly 60 years ago, Dr. King delivered his famous “I have a dream” speech during the March on Washington. On that day, he spoke these words:
“Now is the time to make real the promises of democracy. Now is the time to rise from the dark and desolate valley of segregation to the sunlit path of racial justice. Now is the time to lift our nation from the quicksands of racial injustice to the solid rock of brotherhood. Now is the time to make justice a reality for all of God's children.”
Dr. King’s words resonate just as powerfully now as they did so many years ago. We have much more work to do if we are to make real the promise of democracy. We must confront white supremacy and dismantle institutional racism in all its forms. We must level the playing field across areas like education, housing, and healthcare. [https://www.franchot.com/levelplayingfield] We must continue to reform our criminal justice system so we can restore trust between our law enforcement and communities of color. We simply must do more.
In 2022, we are once again confronted with the fierce urgency of now. Today and all days, may we honor Dr. King by continuing the important work of bending our universe ever closer towards justice.
Thank you,
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/Franchot%20Signature.png][https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/MLK_1024x512.png]
By Authority: Franchot Anderson-Walker for Maryland Slate; Thomas C. Brennan, Treasurer.
Friends of Peter Franchot
P.O. Box 6648
Annapolis, MD 21401
United States

name: Admiral Mike Franken
subject: Chuck Grassley is one of only two sitting senators who voted against making MLK Day a holiday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764281/325830484/301434390?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YWQwNTRjMy05ZDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RGEwZ9M9hYltYkLv7nKm_TwpCktdJ4KQUncvlBcsk94=&emci=d7a72f54-0e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8ad054c3-9d77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6371843)
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764282/325830485/301434390?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YWQwNTRjMy05ZDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RGEwZ9M9hYltYkLv7nKm_TwpCktdJ4KQUncvlBcsk94=&emci=d7a72f54-0e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8ad054c3-9d77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6371843)
Hello,
If my opponent Senator Chuck Grassley had his way back in 1983, we would not be making the connection of Dr. King’s message and today’s assault on voting rights in dozens of states. **Chuck Grassley is one of only two sitting senators who voted against then-President Reagan from making Martin Luther King, Jr. Day a federal holiday.**
Dr. King lost his life because he spoke out against hate and racial segregation. He demanded the equality guaranteed by the Constitution for all citizens and inspired a civil rights movement that make us a better nation. His work continues, but restricting voting rights, a regressive trend now favored by many State Legislatures, runs counter to Dr. King’s legacy.
Senator Grassley stands out having voted against Dr. King’s message to make every American’s voice heard equally. Once again, things have changed in America, yet Grassley wants to stay in office to push his 1950’s ideas on everyone for another six years. Whether it is the color of your skin, your economic status, where you live, or your language, rolling back access to voting is an affront to every American.
**The best way to carry the torch of Dr. King’s legacy is for Congress to pass key legislative bills, specifically the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act.** These measures will prevent discriminatory practices and policies and ensure that everyone has equal access to the ballot box. True patriotism is making it easier for everyone to vote.
We cannot allow lifetime politicians, like Senator Grassley, to undo the work of Dr. King. No one wants the Jim Crow 2.0-type laws the Republicans are pushing on entire sectors of society.
As a senator, I trust the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act will solidify Dr. King’s legacy.
Thank you for being a part of the team that _will_ defeat Chuck Grassley and turn Iowa blue this November,
Mike Franken, VADM, U.S. Navy (ret.)

clean_body: Hello, If my opponent Senator Chuck Grassley had his way back in 1983, we would not be making the connection of Dr. King’s message and today’s assault on voting rights in dozens of states. Chuck Grassley is one of only two sitting senators who voted against then-President Reagan from making Martin Luther King, Jr. Day a federal holiday. Dr. King lost his life because he spoke out against hate and racial segregation. He demanded the equality guaranteed by the Constitution for all citizens and inspired a civil rights movement that make us a better nation. His work continues, but restricting voting rights, a regressive trend now favored by many State Legislatures, runs counter to Dr. King’s legacy. Senator Grassley stands out having voted against Dr. King’s message to make every American’s voice heard equally. Once again, things have changed in America, yet Grassley wants to stay in office to push his 1950’s ideas on everyone for another six years. Whether it is the color of your skin, your economic status, where you live, or your language, rolling back access to voting is an affront to every American. The best way to carry the torch of Dr. King’s legacy is for Congress to pass key legislative bills, specifically the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act. These measures will prevent discriminatory practices and policies and ensure that everyone has equal access to the ballot box. True patriotism is making it easier for everyone to vote. We cannot allow lifetime politicians, like Senator Grassley, to undo the work of Dr. King. No one wants the Jim Crow 2.0-type laws the Republicans are pushing on entire sectors of society. As a senator, I trust the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act will solidify Dr. King’s legacy. Thank you for being a part of the team that will defeat Chuck Grassley and turn Iowa blue this November, Mike Franken, VADM, U.S. Navy (ret.) = Paid for by Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc PO Box 13401 Des Moines, IA 50310 United States

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: bending toward justice
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Van Hollen for Senate
body:
| |
---
This weekend we honor one of our greatest Americans, the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., who gave his life in the struggle to push our country to meet its founding promise of liberty and justice for all. Dr. King knew deeply and personally how difficult progress can be. But he also understood that, if we stay the course, progress is inevitable. His observation that "the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice” was a call to action in pursuit of our values. Dr. King believed that each of us has a responsibility to participate in our democracy, to confront injustice, and to persevere against the forces of fear, hate, and ignorance that want to take us backwards. He lived his beliefs — and we are so much the better for it. **Now it's our turn.** At this moment we face a broad-based assault of state laws designed to make it harder to vote, interfere with who counts our votes, and who will certify the outcome of our votes. And it's up to us to protect our democratic process. Even as we work to break the logjam in the Senate, change the undemocratic filibuster rule, and pass the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act, we must also prepare for grassroots action and involvement on all fronts of our 2022 elections — getting voters to the polls, working at polling places, and responding to this assault on our elections with nothing less than a tsunami of people power. We have to unite in action to achieve the America we believe in— where every man, woman, and child is equal under the law and in our society; where might does not mean right; and where empathy, truth, and integrity are the values we demand from each other and our officials — elected and unelected. Working together we can — and must — bend that arc further and faster. On this Martin Luther King Day let us recommit to that mission. Onwards, Chris

* * *

name: Val Demings
subject: What's on my mind today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
I wonder how Dr. King would feel today
| | |
---
Today, friend, my mind is on Selma and Montgomery.
Fifty-seven years ago, activists of all backgrounds marched by the thousands in Alabama – joined and led by Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the Southern Christian Leadership Conference.
There were three protests spanning 54 miles from Selma all the way to the state capitol in Montgomery. Black men and women were harassed. Arrested. Beaten. Some were even killed in cold blood. But still they persisted for 54 miles.
All that blood shed and skin bruised – the tears and the lives lost – we must never forget what it was all for: **Their right to vote.**
Just days later, after "Bloody Sunday" woke the world to the atrocities of state-sanctioned segregation in the South – change, at long last, was realized. On March 15, President Lyndon B. Johnson announced on national television that he would send Congress the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

Dr. King watched the news in the living room of his friend and fellow activist, Marie Foster. **Tears fell from his face as he wept. He wept at the promise, the guarantee, of something so simple yet so sacred.**
I wonder how Dr. King would feel today, friend – almost 57 years later - as Republican-led voter suppression bills tantamount to the laws he rallied against and gave his life to defeat are being filed and passed all across this country. _More than 440 voter suppression bills were introduced last year alone._
It was at the culmination of the Selma to Montgomery march that Dr. King spoke what are now some of his most famous words. He said, "I know you are asking today, 'How long will it take?'" He continued, "How long? Not long, because the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice."
Today, countless Americans are once again asking the same: _How long?_ How long will it take the Senate to act? To abandon an archaic procedural rule in favor of ending voter suppression and guaranteeing people's access to the polls. _How long?_
I firmly believe, with everything in my soul, that what Dr. King said is true: The arc of the moral universe _does_ bend toward justice. **So today, in celebration of all he stood for, I'm asking you to join me in raising your voice and standing up for the right to vote. [Please, sign my petition now to tell the Senate to abandon the filibuster and pass voting rights legislation. Our democracy is at stake.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764734/325832575/518785067?email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&code=jtk1508-su-nd&refcode=jtk1508-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxM2ZkNWE3MC05ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=y_Xy3lJ4NIASfcSjO6vG46g9ynqf1tQz3GPD-fXaVdA=&emci=0e71adff-5175-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=13fd5a70-9e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=20244100)**
In solidarity – and in honor of everything Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. stood for and fought for.

Val Demings
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764735/325832576/1804727444?code=jtk1508-su-nd&refcode=jtk1508-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxM2ZkNWE3MC05ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=y_Xy3lJ4NIASfcSjO6vG46g9ynqf1tQz3GPD-fXaVdA=) **Val Demings here:** I can’t thank you enough for fighting alongside me to flip Florida blue. Florida will determine whether Democrats keep our Senate majority – that’s why Republicans are pouring millions of dollars into this race to crush our movement _and_ keep Florida red _permanently_. Our grassroots campaign is off to a great start, but in a race as close as this one, I need your help to surge ahead and defend the Senate. I’m relying on grassroots supporters like you, donating to emails like this, to help me defeat Marco Rubio and stop Mitch McConnell from flipping the Senate. Will you donate today to help me flip Florida blue? **[Click here to donate now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764736/325832577/1804727444?code=jtk1508-su-nd&refcode=jtk1508-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxM2ZkNWE3MC05ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=y_Xy3lJ4NIASfcSjO6vG46g9ynqf1tQz3GPD-fXaVdA=)** If you’d like to purchase merchandise to show your support for this campaign, **[please click here to visit my campaign store.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40764737/325832578/-460395862?code=jtk1508-su-nd&refcode=jtk1508-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxM2ZkNWE3MC05ZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=y_Xy3lJ4NIASfcSjO6vG46g9ynqf1tQz3GPD-fXaVdA=&emci=0e71adff-5175-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=13fd5a70-9e77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=20244100)** Thank you for your continued support. clean_body: I wonder how Dr. King would feel today | | | Today, friend, my mind is on Selma and Montgomery. Fifty-seven years ago, activists of all backgrounds marched by the thousands in Alabama – joined and led by Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the Southern Christian Leadership Conference. There were three protests spanning 54 miles from Selma all the way to the state capitol in Montgomery. Black men and women were harassed. Arrested. Beaten. Some were even killed in cold blood. But still they persisted for 54 miles. All that blood shed and skin bruised – the tears and the lives lost – we must never forget what it was all for: Their right to vote. Just days later, after "Bloody Sunday" woke the world to the atrocities of state-sanctioned segregation in the South – change, at long last, was realized. On March 15, President Lyndon B. Johnson announced on national television that he would send Congress the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Dr. King watched the news in the living room of his friend and fellow activist, Marie Foster. Tears fell from his face as he wept. He wept at the promise, the guarantee, of something so simple yet so sacred. I wonder how Dr. King would feel today, friend – almost 57 years later - as Republican-led voter suppression bills tantamount to the laws he rallied against and gave his life to defeat are being filed and passed all across this country. More than 440 voter suppression bills were introduced last year alone. It was at the culmination of the Selma to Montgomery march that Dr. King spoke what are now some of his most famous words. He said, "I know you are asking today, 'How long will it take?'" He continued, "How long? Not long, because the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice." Today, countless Americans are once again asking the same: How long? How long will it take the Senate to act? To abandon an archaic procedural rule in favor of ending voter suppression and guaranteeing people's access to the polls. How long? I firmly believe, with everything in my soul, that what Dr. King said is true: The arc of the moral universe does bend toward justice. So today, in celebration of all he stood for, I'm asking you to join me in raising your voice and standing up for the right to vote. In solidarity – and in honor of everything Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. stood for and fought for. Val Demings | | = Val Demings here: I can’t thank you enough for fighting alongside me to flip Florida blue. Florida will determine whether Democrats keep our Senate majority – that’s why Republicans are pouring millions of dollars into this race to crush our movement and keep Florida red permanently. Our grassroots campaign is off to a great start, but in a race as close as this one, I need your help to surge ahead and defend the Senate. I’m relying on grassroots supporters like you, donating to emails like this, to help me defeat Marco Rubio and stop Mitch McConnell from flipping the Senate. Will you donate today to help me flip Florida blue? = If you’d like to purchase merchandise to show your support for this campaign, Thank you for your continued support. Together we’re going to protect voting rights, expand access to affordable health care and fully recover from this pandemic. = By providing your mobile phone number you consent to receive recurring updates from Val Demings for U.S. Senate, including by automated text message. Hi there,

Each year, on this day, we take time to honor the life of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and celebrate his legacy. I have always respected and looked up to Dr. King, not just because he was a revolutionary and an activist for our community, but also I saw myself in his love for his family.

Dr. King had four children, Yolanda Denise King, Martin Luther King III, Dexter Scott King, and Bernice Albertine King. I see them and think of my own children, Masonmarie, Kyrié, Darrius, Josiah, and Yael. From Washington to Selma to Montgomery, I know he kept his family in his thoughts –**because that's what we are all fighting for, a chance and a life for our future.**

As a hero of our Civil Rights Movement, he fought for our voices to not only be recognized, but to be heard -- regardless of the color of our skin. **But the rights he fought for are now quickly being stripped away piece by piece.**

This past year, we have seen states all across our country enact baseless and discriminatory voter restriction laws -- from unnecessary voter ID laws, to reducing access to early voting, to limiting polling locations, _especially in Black and Brown neighborhoods -_ we need to fight now, against the same white supremacy that Dr. King spent his life fighting against.

So let's do more than just reflect today. Last Thursday, the House passed the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act, which includes provisions to expand voting access, including vote-by-mail, eliminate racist voter roll purges, and make Election Day a federal holiday so we all can take the time to make our voices heard. **But the opposition is already stirring, and we once again face the possibility of losing these necessary protections.**

We need more voices in the room that are committed to making sure _all_ our voices can be heard – so that's why I'm ready to serve as your next Congresswoman.

**[Will you join my movement to ensure they hear us loud and clear in the halls of Congress?>>> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40765525/325834438/347518524?refcode=em22_220117_MLKDAY&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDQ5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2EyYmEyMC1hMDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4onCkiR_-XHaG1z9PA18XxlCW6k7NW_WssLX8znSBuM=)**

Thank you for honoring Dr. King with me today.

Angela

body:
Today we honor the enduring legacy of one of nation’s most profound leaders.
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40766461/325836644/-786800018?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTM5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk4OGFhNi1hMTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VY9HKBvkNZvwz5auOI7ncRaJ9SnhWATsfCO2Bmou-HQ=&emci=e9fbf0c1-9775-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=3c988aa6-a177-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=14488557)

Team, We pause today to celebrate the life of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. A fearless and passionate leader, Dr. King honed the power of community and love to challenge the status quo and push our nation forward. Dedicated to ensuring equal access to the American promise despite overwhelming adversity, King's hard work has been instrumental in altering the fabric of our nation, pushing us to embrace our differences as strengths and serving as the foundation for justice and tolerance. Dr. King's tremendous legacy should serve as inspiration for us all. His tireless service in the pursuit of a stronger, more unified nation rings true today, and as we build on his legacy, we must do so in the spirit of equality, justice, and fairness. Thank you, and Happy Martin Luther King Day Chelsea Clark | | | Democrat Chelsea Clark is a small business owner, a city councilwoman, a sixth-generation farmer, and a single mom. She's running to be Ohio's Secretary of State because the constitutional right to vote is under attack. Chelsea will fight to make sure that we all have access to vote, that all votes are counted, and that our elections are safe and secure.

name: Team U.T.C.
subject: Celebrate Martin Luther King Day by protecting his legacy.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Unite the Country, unitethecountry
body:
Unite the Country [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/EA/EA005/1/78987/images/utc-web-logo.png]
Peter —

When Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. stood on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial on August 28, 1963, he spoke truth to power.

He called for an end to racism, poverty, and injustice. And fought with countless men and women for racial equality, freedom, and democracy.

Dr. King’s message rings more true today than ever before. And It’s up to us to finish his work.

The way we honor him — today and every day — is not by simply lifting Dr. King’s legacy with words but by action. The fight for racial equality and economic justice is a long one. And together, we can bring about real change.

Thank you,

Team Unite the Country
Unite the Country was formed in 2019 to support Joe Biden’s candidacy for President. After a resounding victory in 2020, our goal now is to communicate to the American electorate how President Biden’s agenda will both advance broad and desperately needed policy changes and help to heal our fractured political environment.
Unite the Country is proud to continue to support President Biden’s bold agenda and vision for America, and we will support candidates for public office who have similar foresight and courage.

name: Team ODP
subject: How we're honoring Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s legacy in 2022
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| Ohio Democrats,

Martin Luther King Jr. Day is not just a day to share the brilliant things that Dr. King said in his speeches or to think back on the work he did during the civil rights movement.

Today serves as a day to volunteer in your community, a time to reflect on how far our country has come in the fight for equality, and a chance to recommit to fighting for a better future for all.

Dr. King once said, "Give us the vote." It seems like a simple statement, but a statement that carries huge meaning today. As we honor Dr. King's legacy today, we must recommit to the fight to ensure our voices are heard -- especially the voices of disenfranchised voters. We must fight to ensure every eligible voter has equal access to the polls. And we must fight to ensure our congressional maps are nonpartisan and fair.

That's how we're committed to honoring Dr. King's legacy at the Ohio Democratic Party in 2022 and beyond.

Thanks,

Team ODP

---

name: Alisha Shelton
subject: Celebrating Dr. King’s Legacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHELTON FOR CONGRESS
body:
As an American and a Black woman, I am grateful every day for Dr. King’s work and legacy.

| Peter,

Today we celebrate the life and legacy of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

As someone committed to fighting for justice and equity for all people, especially Black Americans, I have always been inspired by Dr. King’s “I Have A Dream” speech. I am also deeply aware of the fact that without Dr. King’s leadership, I, as a Black woman, would likely not be able to exercise my right to vote, let alone run for office.

However, many who honor Dr. King today are only familiar with his work through this speech and do not understand the scope and importance of his work. Those who defend insurrectionists, work against voting rights, and do not understand why we say Black Lives Matter fail to grasp that if Dr. King were still with us, they would almost certainly oppose him.

As an American and a Black woman, I am grateful every day for Dr. King’s work and legacy. May we all share our truths, our words, in the exact same way, having enough faith to trust the direction they are to go.

Thank you,
Alisha Shelton
---

name: Letitia James
subject: Over 440 voter suppression bills
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James for NY
body:
| | |
---
Peter: Today, we honor the legacy of a man who spent his life fighting for racial equality and equal access to the ballot.
**Now, more than 50 years after Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s death, we continue his fight for voting rights.** While we’ve made significant progress to protect one of our most sacred rights we have as Americans, we still have a long, trying road ahead of us.
That’s why we wanted to share this voting rights petition to ensure that we have enough champions for freedom to carry out Dr. King’s legacy. **I need 5,000 signatures before midnight tonight, so let me know:**** [_Will you sign my petition and stand with me to defend voting rights for every American?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40766777/325837793/549295702?code=jtk309-su-nd&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fjamesforny%3Frefcode%3Djtk309-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNWUxODBhMC1hMjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CGXLNNPXNLYV89F-5juYeZkuw15YbyFI2coK3jQFACQ=&emci=40a5d17c-6375-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=e5e180a0-a277-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=25369504)**

Our fight to keep Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s vision alive has never been more essential. While members of the GOP flood Twitter today with promises to uphold King’s legacy, many are simultaneously attempting to undo his hard-fought progress on voting rights and racial equality.
In 2021 alone, the GOP introduced _**over 440 voter suppression bills**_ across the country – and many of these bills inordinately impact communities of color. More than that, GOP lawmakers are intentionally misrepresenting King’s words for their own political agendas.
**But Peter, I can’t defend the right to vote and make Dr. King’s vision a reality by myself.**** [_Will you add your name to my petition before midnight tonight to help me protect and uphold voting rights for all?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40766781/325837797/549295702?code=jtk309-su-nd&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fjamesforny%3Frefcode%3Djtk309-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNWUxODBhMC1hMjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CGXLNNPXNLYV89F-5juYeZkuw15YbyFI2coK3jQFACQ=&emci=40a5d17c-6375-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=e5e180a0-a277-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=25369504)**

Now, more than 50 years after Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s death, we continue his fight for voting rights. While we’ve made significant progress to protect one of our most sacred rights we have as Americans, we still have a long, trying road ahead of us. That’s why we wanted to share this voting rights petition to ensure that we have enough champions for freedom to carry out Dr. King’s legacy. I need 5,000 signatures before midnight tonight, so let me know: | | Our fight to keep Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s vision alive has never been more essential. While members of the GOP flood Twitter today with promises to uphold King’s legacy, many are simultaneously attempting to undo his hard-fought progress on voting rights and racial equality. In 2021 alone, the GOP introduced over 440 voter suppression bills across the country – and many of these bills inordinately impact communities of color. More than that, GOP lawmakers are intentionally misrepresenting King’s words for their own political agendas. But Peter, I can’t defend the right to vote and make Dr. King’s vision a reality by myself. | | Thank you for continuing this important fight with me. name: Chris Deluzio
subject: Honoring the legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Deluzio for Congress
body:
Hello Derek,

Today and every day, we honor the legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and his fight for freedom, liberation, and justice for all.

Martin Luther King’s commitment to social change was unwavering and fearless in his pursuit of equality. Whether it was organizing boycotts against segregated busing, supporting striking sanitation workers, demanding fair housing alongside public housing tenants — the civil rights leader stood with our most vulnerable communities.

We must continue the fight for freedom and racial justice – and do all we can to protect voting rights. Civil rights remain under siege, and there’s a lot of work ahead of us to combat the threats to our democracy, including right here in Pennsylvania.

We must confront vote suppression head-on by passing legislation that secures the right to vote — like the Freedom to Vote Act and The John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act to honor Dr. King’s legacy. He led the fight against racial discrimination in voting, and we have a responsibility to carry on this work — work that I’ve been proud to take on as a voting rights lawyer.

That’s what our movement is all about — coming together in service of a greater common good. I believe that every Pennsylvanian deserves a safe workplace, affordable healthcare, clean air and water, quality education, and more. But to do that, we must all have a voice at the ballot box.

— Chris Deluzio

Chris Deluzio is a voting rights lawyer and Iraq war veteran running for Congress in Pennsylvania's 17th District. He'll be a champion for our unions, fight to guarantee good healthcare for every American, take on corporate power, and advocate for bold action to protect and strengthen our democracy. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, and Democrats have to hold it in order to keep their House majority in 2022. Please chip in to help Chris build a strong campaign that can win against the Republican machine.

Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/del-email-jan2022?refcode=fl_email_na_20220117MLKday_CD_90da_xx_pt]

[https://www.facebook.com/ChrisforPA]
[https://twitter.com/ChrisForPA]
[https://chrisforpa.com]

Paid for by Chris Deluzio for Congress
Chris Deluzio for Congress
PO Box 16210
Pittsburgh, PA 15242
United States

name: Charles Graham
subject: This isn’t the future Dr. King envisioned
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Charles Graham for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40768724/325847405/-325589832?refcode=gra_headerlogo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NjE4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYWQ5NmUyMC1hNjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w4nie816xwRvezpXit1jZmJTFq23KnGKa7vX5Ssn13s=)

| | Folks, When Dr. Martin Luther King gave his famous _I Have a Dream_ speech in 1963, I doubt he envisioned that decades later a twice-impeached former President would incite a violent riot at our nation’s Capitol in an effort to halt the peaceful transition of power. And when Dr. King endured brutality and jail time for simply wanting a better future for his daughters and other folks of color, I doubt he thought that decades later, Republicans would be trying to ban teaching about the very struggles that Dr. King and so many others endured during the 1960s civil rights movement. But folks, that’s the reality of the times we’re living in – Republicans across the nation, at every level of government, are stoking culture wars and resorting to “us” vs. “them” tactics to consolidate power. And they’re even trying to ban teaching about racism altogether. Republicans are diligently trying to ban the teaching of Critical Race Theory in schools – the idea that racism is a structural issue embedded in institutions and policies, rather than an individual issue of prejudice. Republicans are arguing that kids need not be taught about racism and racist systems – that they’re not “ready” for it. But if children of color in the 1960s had no choice but to be “ready” for the hate and violence they endured at recently integrated schools, I think kids of today are ready to learn about it. As a former educator and someone who staunchly opposed the North Carolina GOP’s anti-Critical Race Theory bill in the Assembly, I cannot stand for this erasure of history. The North Carolina bill and so many others are based on fear-mongering, but it is the communities like mine that will pay the price. The true history of our struggles will never be known. To put it simply, folks, Republicans are not heeding Dr. King’s teachings, and this is not the future Dr. King envisioned for us – his dream of unity and mutual respect has sadly not come true. I of course did not know Dr. King, but if I had to guess, I believe he wouldn’t want us to give up on his dream. Although it is taking much longer to come to fruition, there are still good people out there fighting for equality – fighting for Dr. King’s dream. That’s why I’m running for Congress – because human dignity is a human right, and I sure haven’t forgotten that fact. And I’ll continue to fight for it. Today, we honor Dr. King and all of his accomplishments and teachings. Tomorrow, we get back to work trying to make his dream a reality – by striving towards a more perfect union. In solidarity,
Charles
---

name: Mondaire Jones
subject: To truly honor Dr. King, we must pass voting rights legislation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_
body:
#

Friend,
Today, we celebrate the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Right now, millions of folks throughout the country are volunteering in their communities in honor of Dr. King and his commitment to service.
We should take special care to remember Dr. King’s leadership in the fight for federal voting rights. He advocated for a more just country by securing the fundamental right of Black citizens to vote.
Nearly 54 years after Dr. King’s death, our democracy faces its greatest test since Jim Crow, and the fight for voting rights lives on. As Republicans desperately try to seize power through voter suppression and election subversion, they have introduced over 400 voter suppression bills in state houses across the country. They’re working overtime to disenfranchise millions of Black and brown voters - unless we stop them.
That’s why, last weekend, I was honored to join Dr. King’s family in Arizona where we marched together in support of the fundamental right to vote. As I stood alongside his family, it was clear that the best way to truly honor Dr. King’s legacy is for Congress to pass voting rights legislation once and for all.
Standing together, we called on the Senate to reform the filibuster and pass the _Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act_ without delay.
When it came to fighting for multiracial democracy, Dr. King never gave up. And neither will we.
Yours in the Struggle,
Mondaire
* * *

body:
Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his life fighting for civil rights. In a time of deep division and oppression, the civil rights movement he took part in strengthened our democracy and advanced the right to vote for all people. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40768794/325847532/-2023352048?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkZDk2ZTIwLWE2NzctZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkNDQ0MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jxfwbbHvaIwbpTdWAA7NSo7KDt4-hJitE7QTKDCaEAI=&emci=9d6b302c-6e75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=9dd96e20-a677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=16335001) Team,

Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his life fighting for civil rights. In a time of deep division and oppression, the civil rights movement he took part in strengthened our democracy and advanced the right to vote for all people.

But last year, on January 6, extremists like Steve Bannon, Marjorie Taylor Greene, Donald Trump, and the Treason Caucus attempted to erase millions of votes in a free and fair election. **Their attempt to subvert our democracy was a direct attack on the rights and values that Dr. King fought for and the legacy he left behind.**

This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we must honor his memory by standing up for the right of the people to be heard. As Dr. King said, "Change does not roll in on the wheels of inevitability." We can't let insurrectionists erase the civil rights that leaders like Dr. King fought for **and we can't be complacent in the face of lies.**

**[So today, will you recommit to the fight for our civil rights by helping us defeat the Treason Caucus and hold them accountable in the upcoming elections? Join our team and take a stand for our democracy.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40768797/325847535/83737857?ms=em22_220114_MLK_adv_gq_act___NAPAC_ettx&refcode=em22_220114_MLK_adv_gq_act___NAPAC_ettx&source=em22_220114_MLK_adv_gq_act___NAPAC_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220114_MLK_adv_gq_act___NAPAC_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=eOU2zplHW50HCGbgAGBqOxqSTiJuDnh6La70g5dxbVsqg3kggmD5P6dQ8YNlX%2BYbSIRqmx%2BOB4rW6NEhjhiottRqnk3CCWEgzGjboZqWEKmytfh1e1Ro6mo3ppd%2BnjEhY1KC6nN6xlwjyHiXl1w0AQ%3D%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkZDk2ZTIwLWE2NzctZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkNDQ0MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jxfwbbHvaIwbpTdWAA7NSo7KDt4-hJitE7QTKDCaEAI=&emci=9d6b302c-6e75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=9dd96e20-a677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=16335001)**

Thank you,

Team Never Again

name: Hendrell Remus
subject: A Dangerous Unselfishness
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

“Let us develop a kind of dangerous unselfishness.”

Peter, these words are from Dr. Martin Luther King Jr’s final speech in Memphis on April 3, 1968 over fifty years ago. It would be his final speech before his assassination the following day. He was speaking to sanitation workers who were striking for fair working conditions.

In this speech, he emphasized the need for justice through action — nonviolent protest, community demonstrations, and the power of withholding labor. These words ring just as clearly today. We have seen a resurgence in the labor movement over the past year, with workers demanding fair wages and benefits in the wake of rampant wealth inequality. We have seen an incredible uprising of Black Lives Matter protesters calling for true equity in our criminal justice system.

We have come a long way since Martin Luther King Jr’s final speech in Memphis, but it’s clear that we still have a long way to go.

So today, we must look at Martin Luther King Jr.’s great legacy as a call to action. At the federal level, we must pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act to enshrine and expand upon the sacred right to vote. We must pass the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act to begin the long journey of holding our system accountable to all people. And here in Tennessee, we must fight to expand healthcare to all Tennesseans, protect and expand the rights of workers, and fully fund our public schools in every zip code.

As Martin Luther King Jr. once said, “Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.” This is a truth that we must remember in everything we do as Tennessee Democrats. We must hold steadfast in our fight to win Tennessee back for the people, and not the special interests that are currently holding it captive through Republican control.

None of the progress that we’ve made so far would have been possible without you — thank you. We’ve come a long way this past year in building an organization that can scale to the level needed to take our state back, but we still have far to go.

On this important day of reflection, we recommit our fight to form a more perfect union. As Tennessee Democrats, we will be fearless in our pursuit of justice and equity so that we can create an America that truly represents all of us.

Solidarity,

Hendrell Remus
he/him
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

https://www.facebook.com/tndem [https://www.facebook.com/tndem]
https://twitter.com/tndp [https://twitter.com/tndp]
https://www.instagram.com/tndems [https://www.instagram.com/tndems]

CAROL V. ABNEY, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.

name: Eric Lynn
subject: Honoring MLK
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Lynn for Congress
body:
We need to do more [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770385/325854681/-1183402875?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzY4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDMxODYzNy1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UmgDFgBYeQUm2tZXB6H3PcAjvs1OMLiCa_fO3sEDyKU=&emci=be709e6b-7975-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=20318637-a877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1139417) Dear friends, Today is Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, and although we honor his memory today, we should constantly recognize the ways that Dr. King reshaped our nation for the better. As I march in the parade honoring Dr. King today, I am mindful of the work we still have to do. While Dr. King’s message can be summarized by “a call for equality,” he stood for so much more. **We can only have equality if we have equity of opportunity.** We can only create opportunities for everyone - regardless of race, gender, sexual orientation, and zip code - if we identify and address the ways that our systems needs to work for all. **At the core of many of these issues is fair representation in government, and unfortunately, voting rights face brazen attacks at the local, state, and national levels.** Last week, President Biden called to waive the filibuster so that the Senate can pass an absolutely essential voting rights bill, and this is necessary if we hope to protect and elevate the voices of disenfranchised Americans. [**We cannot possibly have equality while many people are discouraged or prevented from participating in our democracy. If you agree, please click here to sign our petition calling for the U.S. Senate to take action!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770387/325854682/886331486?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzY4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDMxODYzNy1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UmgDFgBYeQUm2tZXB6H3PcAjvs1OMLiCa_fO3sEDyKU=&emci=be709e6b-7975-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=20318637-a877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1139417) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770390/325854685/-1183402875?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzY4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDMxODYzNy1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UmgDFgBYeQUm2tZXB6H3PcAjvs1OMLiCa_fO3sEDyKU=&emci=be709e6b-7975-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=20318637-a877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1139417) The best way that we can celebrate and respect Dr. King is by fighting for legislation that will protect voting rights and create opportunities for all Americans, and I’m committed to that cause. **Today’s holiday aims to honor Dr. King’s life, but tomorrow, let’s work to honor Dr. King’s vision.** Thank you, Eric Lynn | | clean_body: We need to do more Dear friends, Today is Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, and although we honor his memory today, we should constantly recognize the ways that Dr. King reshaped our nation for the better. As I march in the parade honoring Dr. King today, I am mindful of the work we still have to do. While Dr. King’s message can be summarized by “a call for equality,” he stood for so much more. We can only have equality if we have equity of opportunity. We can only create opportunities for everyone - regardless of race, gender, sexual orientation, and zip code - if we identify and address the ways that our systems needs to work for all. At the core of many of these issues is fair representation in government, and unfortunately, voting rights face brazen attacks at the local, state, and national levels. Last week, President Biden called to waive the filibuster so that the Senate can pass an absolutely essential voting rights bill, and this is necessary if we hope to protect and elevate the voices of disenfranchised Americans. The best way that we can celebrate and respect Dr. King is by fighting for legislation that will protect voting rights and create opportunities for all Americans, and I’m committed to that cause. Today’s holiday aims to honor Dr. King’s life, but tomorrow, let’s work to honor Dr. King’s vision. Thank you, Eric Lynn | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Eric Lynn for Congress Eric Lynn for Congress PO Box 16991 St. Petersburg, FL 33733 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Malcolm Kenyatta
subject: The only normalcy we will settle for
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Malcolm PAC
body:
We cannot go back to what was normal before this pandemic
#

Friend,
Occasions like today force us to ask big questions. We are over two years into a pandemic that exposed gaping holes in America's safety net and in our conscience.
A lot of folks think it's time to throw our hands in the air and move on with our lives. They want to get back to normal.
But the basic questions Dr. King forced us to ask ourselves were these:
_How should we treat one another? What should our government work on? And who should our government work for?_
And if we ask ourselves those questions today — as we all should — our answer must be that we cannot go back to what was normal before this pandemic.
On March 25, 1965, the last day of the march to Selma, Dr. King told us exactly what to say when folks start talking about going back to what's normal when he said:
_"The only normalcy that we will settle for is the normalcy that recognizes the dignity and worth of all of God’s children."_
Normalcy for some people means paying lip service to Dr. King's legacy and then forgetting about it every other day of the year.
But today is a day for us to stop and recalibrate ourselves around his dream.
And his dream wasn't only about racial justice — it was also about addressing now the issues that we lack the political will to confront.
My hope every MLK Jr. Day is that we garner the political will to not just go back to normal after the day is over but to remember what normalcy really should mean.
That's something we can't limit to one day. It's something we must remember 365 days a year.
Thank you,
Malcolm

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Dr. King's legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
| | |
---
Friends – As our democracy faces partisan attacks, I find myself reflecting more and more on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s legacy. Dr. King spent his life fighting for a more perfect union, and his unshakeable commitment to non-violent action has inspired generations of Americans. **Today, more than ever before, we must recommit ourselves to the example set by Dr. King as we continue to strengthen racial justice in America.** While we as a country have made great strides, there is still much work to be done to ensure that all people are fully included in our society, our economy, and our democracy. Last month, I delivered a speech on the Senate floor calling for a simple majority vote on legislation related to fundamental issues of our democracy. The Senate must take action to protect the right to vote, and to ensure free, fair, and impartially administered elections. **[If you agree, please join me in this fight by adding your name in support of protecting our democracy and the right to vote.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770438/325854849/2003055011?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjI4MjkyZi1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fMBL3lp0HJvZnxQoIFH1eOYAXGceQcRVyL1LyGsVmWo=&emci=7a015285-ff76-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8f28292f-a877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=5452893)** Across the country, we are seeing partisan politicians attempt to throw out the votes and silence the voices of those with whom they disagree. These efforts threaten the integrity of our election system – and they are disproportionately impacting communities of color. As we continue our work toward creating a more perfect union, Dr. King's legacy shapes our continued efforts. The fight to save our democracy cannot wait. We must pass legislation to protect our most fundamental right. **[Please add your name today if you support protecting our democracy and defending the right to vote.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770439/325854850/2003055011?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjI4MjkyZi1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fMBL3lp0HJvZnxQoIFH1eOYAXGceQcRVyL1LyGsVmWo=&emci=7a015285-ff76-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8f28292f-a877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=5452893)** Thank you for being a part of my team. I'm so grateful for your support. With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan
