name: Aramis Ayala
subject: Peter, we can take MLK’s real message of love and JUSTICE to DC!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Aramis Ayala for Congress
body:
I have no doubt that if Dr. King was alive today, he would be on the front lines, boldly fighting for equality and justice in what we call “our criminal justice system” and beyond.

| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770637/325855522/-1888922884?refcode=abd_em_fr_2021.12.21_bX_logo_x__f1_s1_c1__header&amount=25&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2022.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDhhOTZhMC1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7AidR1vOGXTvTYLT9O23snbz2MaXtrpsxZbxp1HGtw=)
---
**“True peace is not merely the absence of tension; it is the presence of justice.” - Martin Luther King, Jr.** Peter,

Today America is celebrating Martin Luther King Day -- but you and I both know the parades and speeches don’t begin to convey the full scope of his vision. Dr. King proclaimed that the presence of justice will only be felt when we liberate our Black and brown brothers and sisters from a system that brings pain to our communities. His “I Have A Dream” speech, reminds us “ _**We can never be satisfied as long as the Negro is the victim of the unspeakable horrors of police brutality”**_ and in “The Trumpet of Conscience” points to systemic inequality as the underlying culprit: _**“Day in and day out he violates welfare laws to deprive the poor of their meager allotments; he flagrantly violates building codes and regulations; his police make a mockery of law; he violates laws on equal employment and education and the provisions of civil services.”**_

I have no doubt that if Dr. King was alive today, he would be on the front lines, boldly fighting for equality and justice in what we call “our criminal justice system” and beyond. It lies with us to take his vision and use it to reach the promised land he spoke of. After all, _**“Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor; it must be demanded by the oppressed."**_

**[In Congress, I will fulfill Dr. King’s call for the “presence of justice,” but I won’t be able to do it without your help. Will you donate to my campaign today for the promise of a better future?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770638/325855523/-1888922884?refcode=ABD_EM_FR_2022.01.17_b1_MLK_X_F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=5&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2022.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDhhOTZhMC1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7AidR1vOGXTvTYLT9O23snbz2MaXtrpsxZbxp1HGtw=)** | [GIVE $5 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770639/325855524/-1888922884?refcode=ABD_EM_FR_2022.01.17_b1_MLK_X_F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=5&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2022.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDhhOTZhMC1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7AidR1vOGXTvTYLT9O23snbz2MaXtrpsxZbxp1HGtw=)

Peter, that means we must take our demands to Washington and refuse to back down. **When I am elected to Congress, you can count on me to take on the status quo and fight for accountability because you’ve seen me do it before.** My commitment to dismantling the systems that perpetuate inequality can be seen in my unrelenting fight against the GOP to end the death penalty in Florida, successful programs that kept non-violent young people from prison, and direct interventions to prevent immigrants from being deported without due process. I made sure the people of Florida know who they’re hiring by publishing a historic Brady List of questionable police. I expanded services for sex trafficking victims and survivors of abuse. Rather than starting from scratch when we take our fight for bold justice reform to Washington, we will be building on these proven successes. From there we will repair the cracks in the foundations of equality by securing economic stability for hardworking families and students, expanding healthcare for all, backing clean energy and water, and answering Dr. King’s challenge _**“to restore community, to resist injustice, and to meet the needs of my brothers.”**_

Like Dr. King, I believe that when people of goodwill come together to speak out and act on their basic convictions, deep and lasting change is possible. Please join me today in honoring the true and complete essence of who Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. was. Let us not only speak profoundly, but also act decisively.

**[With your help, we can go to Congress and make Martin Luther King, Jr’s dream of justice reform and equality realities. So if you can, I would greatly appreciate it if you would donate $5 to my campaign today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770645/325855530/-1888922884?refcode=ABD_EM_FR_2022.01.17_b1_MLK_X_F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=5&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2022.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDhhOTZhMC1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7AidR1vOGXTvTYLT9O23snbz2MaXtrpsxZbxp1HGtw=)** [GIVE $5 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40770646/325855535/-1888922884?refcode=ABD_EM_FR_2022.01.17_b1_MLK_X_F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=5&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2022.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDhhOTZhMC1hODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7AidR1vOGXTvTYLT9O23snbz2MaXtrpsxZbxp1HGtw=)

Aramis Ayala is an award-winning advocate for justice reform and human rights. As Florida’s first Black State Attorney, she has devoted more than two decades to public service and building safer, healthier communities. Aramis’s unflinching commitment to justice earned national headlines when she went head-to-head against former governor Rick Scott to oppose the death penalty. An educator, cancer survivor, and mother of two, Aramis is running for Congress to deliver a bold, forward-focused vision that restores public trust and guarantees an equitable future for all of us.

clean_body: I have no doubt that if Dr. King was alive today, he would be on the front lines, boldly fighting for equality and justice in what we call “our criminal justice system” and beyond. | = “True peace is not merely the absence of tension; it is the presence of justice.” - Martin Luther King, Jr. Peter, Today America is celebrating Martin Luther King Day -- but you and I both know the parades and speeches don’t begin to convey the full scope of his vision. Dr. King proclaimed that the presence of justice will only be felt when we liberate our Black and brown brothers and sisters from a system that brings pain to our communities. His “I Have A Dream” speech, reminds us “ We can never be satisfied as long as the Negro is the victim of the unspeakable horrors of police brutality” and in “The Trumpet of Conscience” points to systemic inequality as the underlying culprit: “Day in and day out he violates welfare laws to deprive the poor of their meager allotments; he flagrantly violates building codes and regulations; his police make a mockery of law; he violates laws on equal employment and education and the provisions of civil services.” I have no doubt that if Dr. King was alive today, he would be on the front lines, boldly fighting for equality and justice in what we call “our criminal justice system” and beyond. It lies with us to take his vision and use it to reach the promised land he spoke of. After all, “Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor; it must be demanded by the oppressed." = | = = = = = = Peter, that means we must take our demands to Washington and refuse to back down. When I am elected to Congress, you can count on me to take on the status quo and fight for accountability because you’ve seen me do it before. My commitment to dismantling the systems that perpetuate inequality can be seen in my unrelenting fight against the GOP to end the death penalty in Florida, successful programs that kept non-violent young people from prison, and direct interventions to prevent immigrants from being deported without due process. I made sure the people of Florida know who they’re hiring by publishing a historic Brady List of questionable police. I expanded services for sex trafficking victims and survivors of abuse. Rather than starting from scratch when we take our fight for bold justice reform to Washington, we will be building on these proven successes. From there we will repair the cracks in the foundations of equality by securing economic stability for hardworking families and students, expanding healthcare for all, backing clean energy and water, and answering Dr. King’s challenge “to restore community, to resist injustice, and to meet the needs of my brothers.” Like Dr. King, I believe that when people of goodwill come together to speak out and act on their basic convictions, deep and lasting change is possible. Please join me today in honoring the true and complete essence of who Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. was. name: Charlie Crist
subject: Celebrating the legacy of Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for and approved by Charlie Crist, Democrat for Governor
body:
|
---

| | |
---
Dear friend,

This weekend, we celebrate the life and legacy of one of America’s greatest patriots – Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

In the face of injustice and bigotry, Dr. King saw what our country could be: a place where equal opportunity was accessible to all - a place where one is judged by the content of their character rather than the color of their skin. Thanks to Dr. King and the movement he inspired, we are closer to that America than ever before.

But in recent years, it has become clear that powerful forces in our country do not want us to reach that goal. Republican-led state legislatures are attempting to tear down one of Dr. King’s most important victories for equality – the right to vote.

Republicans like Ron DeSantis are trying to take us back to the days of Jim Crow, imposing poll taxes for returning citizens and making it harder for people from minority communities to cast their ballot. They know that when all citizens have equal access to the ballot box, they have the power to hold bad leaders accountable – and they don’t like it.

The threats to voting rights are real and dangerous – but we can and must overcome them. And Dr. King gave us the roadmap to do it. We must make our voices heard in every way we can, especially at the ballot box.

Dr. King believed that the vote is one of the most powerful tools we have against injustice. This weekend, I am reminded that our right to vote should never be taken for granted – we must fight for it. And as long as I breathe, I won’t stop fighting.

God bless,
Charlie

---

| Paid for and approved by Charlie Crist, Democrat for Governor
---
Charlie Crist for Governor
P.O. Box 1547
St. Petersburg, FL 33731
United States

name: Jasmine
subject: Remembering Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jasmine Beach-Ferrara for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40771036/325857716/459488397?refcode=jas_headerlogo&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmJjY2JhMS1hOTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=a1kquQ0PBbzUKZIm-NlHb88vmpPvAEENe3sFNDINKHQ=)

| | Peter, Today, we honor the legacy of civil rights leader and activist Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Dr. King's was a prophetic voice at a time of great crisis in our country. He connected the dots between racism, poverty, violence, and war. He connected the American struggle to ways that people have struggled across the globe and from the earliest days, insisting that we understand that connectivity as both an act of humility and humanity. His message sometimes gets diluted into bromides and slogans. But at the core, he was reckoning – as a minister, an activist, and a political leader – with fundamental questions about love, power, freedom, and justice. Part of what is so powerful to me about Dr. King’s work is that he insisted we base our choices, actions, and language in love – this was non-negotiable as both a moral and strategic imperative. There was no way to move forward without love. We also live in a time of American crisis. In this crisis, we see the dangers of extremism and fundamentalism that have their grips in political power structures and too readily invoke violence. We see another surge in a pandemic that has exhausted all of us and taken far too many lives. We see the ticking clock of a climate crisis that must be treated as the emergency it is. It is a time when all of us must ask what we are prepared to do for each other, for our country, for our world. In such times, we do well to remember those who have come before us – the untold numbers of people who have faced a crisis and had to make a choice about what to do. Do we act with violence or love? Do we flee, or do we show up? In these moments, there are clarion voices that have helped people mobilize around our better angels and highest ideals. Dr. King was one such voice, and there were many others – Bayard Rustin, John Lewis, and Fannie Lou Hamer to name a few. Their message can guide us now, as surely as it guided people decades ago. To be of service. To stand on the side of love. To show up again and again in the work and struggle of creating the country we all need America to be. In a typical year, our family goes to the MLK Day March here in Asheville. This year, it’s being held virtually. As we’ve thought about how we can help our kids understand what this day means, we know that it will be about connecting the dots for them between different experiences and how the intention of this day is something we should carry with us each day. Today, and every day, our fight for justice continues. In solidarity, Jasmine

clean_body: | = | | Peter, Today, we honor the legacy of civil rights leader and activist Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Dr. King's was a prophetic voice at a time of great crisis in our country. He connected the dots between racism, poverty, violence, and war. He connected the American struggle to ways that people have struggled across the globe and from the earliest days, insisting that we understand that connectivity as both an act of humility and humanity. His message sometimes gets diluted into bromides and slogans. But at the core, he was reckoning – as a minister, an activist, and a political leader – with fundamental questions about love, power, freedom, and justice. Part of what is so powerful to me about Dr. King’s work is that he insisted we base our choices, actions, and language in love – this was non-negotiable as both a moral and strategic imperative. There was no way to move forward without love. We also live in a time of American crisis. In this crisis, we see the dangers of extremism and fundamentalism that have their grips in political power structures and too readily invoke violence. We see another surge in a pandemic that has exhausted all of us and taken far too many lives. We see the ticking clock of a climate crisis that must be treated as the emergency it is. It is a time when all of us must ask what we are prepared to do for each other, for our country, for our world. In such times, we do well to remember those who have come before us – the untold numbers of people who have faced a crisis and had to make a choice about what to do. Do we act with violence or love? Do we flee, or do we show up? In these moments, there are clarion voices that have helped people mobilize around our better angels and highest ideals. Dr. King was one such voice, and there were many others – Bayard Rustin, John Lewis, and Fannie Lou Hamer to name a few. Their message can guide us now, as surely as it guided people decades ago. To be of service. To stand on the side of love. To show up again and again in the work and struggle of creating the country we all need America to be. In a typical year, our family goes to the MLK Day March here in Asheville. This year, it’s being held virtually. As we’ve thought about how we can help our kids understand what this day means, we know that it will be about connecting the dots for them between different experiences and how the intention of this day is something we should carry with us each day. Today, and every day, our fight for justice continues. In solidarity, Jasmine | | We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, . If you’d like to remove yourself from our list, please click . name: John B. King Jr.
subject: More than his legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John King for Governor, Eliza Leighton, Treasurer
body:
This MLK day, I invite you to take a moment to acknowledge our nation’s history in all its complexity.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40771125/325857843/-1613255896?refcode=BLU-KING-20220117-1-EM&&&&amount=25&refcode2=f0bccba1-a977-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGJjY2JhMS1hOTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=degRAOTf5NxtPTMcp9yI8p24NIGWHq_cNmTiULh6Z1c=)
---
| Peter, _“The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.”_
— Martin Luther King Jr. Of all Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s famous speeches and quotes, this has always particularly resonated with me. I am struck by how eloquently this quote captures the ongoing and arduous journey toward a more just future, and how we can only realize this future if we work together and hold ourselves accountable for our past. **I also find this quote particularly fitting in 2022, as we look back on the 50+ years since Dr. King first spoke these words and recognize we still have a ways to go.**

As we reflect on Dr. King’s legacy and our continued march toward justice, it is imperative we not only celebrate the progress the civil rights movement made possible but also grapple with America’s structural inequities and our responsibility to break down those systemic barriers in order to increase opportunity for all.

This MLK Day, I invite you to take a moment to acknowledge our nation’s history in all its complexity — the ugliness and the glory— and reject the “it’s-all-better-now” narrative which frames Dr. King’s legacy as a completed moment in history. Instead, **we must consider how we can commit ourselves to honoring his legacy by living up to the opportunity to do good.**

While we as a nation have made significant progress since the era of Jim Crow — institutional racism and inequality still shape our present. Black Americans face police brutality, less access to opportunities in schools, higher unemployment rates, unfair voting restrictions, and more at disproportionately higher rates than white Americans. We must address fundamental structures of inequality and, collectively, strive to make better policy choices.

**We must find our own role in the relay race progression of history, carry forth the baton that Dr. King and so many others have passed on to us, and prepare to hand it on to the next generation.**

Today, I will be joining the MLK community peace gathering in Prince George's County, and I hope each of you will do whatever you can in your communities to channel Dr. King’s legacy.

Thank you,

John B. King Jr.

name: Gillian Battino
subject: Honoring Dr. King’s Legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Battino for U
body:
We must dedicate ourselves to continue the fight for justice that Dr. King devoted himself to. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40771301/325858659/464806879?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTQzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjFjYmUwOS1hYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u_o3ySqAq_yxFldfNSykY5ZFpx91Jo9sbNdBQvfoizs=&emci=7f2262df-7675-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8b1cbe09-aa77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=25515813) | Team, Today, as we celebrate the work and life of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr, so much of Dr. King’s legacy is still being developed, challenged, and pushed forward today. Reflecting on the important contributions that Dr. King made in our fight for equality and justice, we are reminded of the work we still have yet to do. In the Senate today, we continue to see archaic and antiquated procedures, such as the filibuster, implemented to block racial justice legislation. Across the nation, GOP-controlled state legislatures continue to pass restrictive measures that make it more difficult for Black, Indigenous, and People of Color voters to cast a ballot. We must dedicate ourselves to continue the fight for justice that Dr. King devoted himself to. **In honor of his legacy, I’m fully committed to working as a U.S. Senator to abolish the filibuster, pass legislation to protect the right to vote for Black and brown voters nationwide, and continue to work towards the vision of equality that Dr. King so strongly believed in. ** Yours in progress, Dr. Gillian Battino [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40771302/325858660/-945847804?refcode=E13490-20220117-MLKDay-SB-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTQzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjFjYmUwOS1hYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u_o3ySqAq_yxFldfNSykY5ZFpx91Jo9sbNdBQvfoizs=)

clean_body: We must dedicate ourselves to continue the fight for justice that Dr. King devoted himself to. | | Team, Today, as we celebrate the work and life of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr, so much of Dr. King’s legacy is still being developed, challenged, and pushed forward today. Reflecting on the important contributions that Dr. King made in our fight for equality and justice, we are reminded of the work we still have yet to do. In the Senate today, we continue to see archaic and antiquated procedures, such as the filibuster, implemented to block racial justice legislation. Across the nation, GOP-controlled state legislatures continue to pass restrictive measures that make it more difficult for Black, Indigenous, and People of Color voters to cast a ballot. We must dedicate ourselves to continue the fight for justice that Dr. King devoted himself to. In honor of his legacy, I’m fully committed to working as a U.S. Senator to abolish the filibuster, pass legislation to protect the right to vote for Black and brown voters nationwide, and continue to work towards the vision of equality that Dr. King so strongly believed in. Yours in progress, Dr. Gillian Battino = It’s time for a new kind of leadership in Wisconsin. Gillian built her career on integrity, advocacy, and action, and she’s ready to put that skill set to work in the U.S. Senate! name: Brad Pfaff
subject: Martin Luther King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pfaff for Congress This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
Let’s remember Dr. King’s commitment to voting rights on this MLK Day.

| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40771473/325859467/-1995343361?refcode=abd_em_fr_2021.10.04_bX_logo_x__f1_s1_c1__header&amount=25&utm_campaign=abd_em_fr_2021.01.17_b1_mlk_x_f1_s1_c1__ac&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ngp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYzliNDU1My1hYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pdHdAkXF-r4bV-Rc0uWHYffqiy64fHdhVKbTY4J7Khs=)
---
Friends,

Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day, and I will be spending the day in service, and reflecting on his commitment to improving this country.

Dr. King was instrumental in advancing the voting rights movement throughout the South and the entire country. His brave, nonviolent persistence helped bring the Voting Rights Act to Congress nearly 60 years ago.

His hard work, along with the work of others in the Civil Rights Movement, increased access to the ballot box for African Americans and other marginalized groups.

Now, our voting rights are again under attack. When we win in November, I promise to honor Dr. King’s legacy by supporting the John Lewis Voting Rights Act in the House.

Please join me today in reflecting on Dr. King’s legacy and furthering his work for our country.

Brad
| | Paid for by Pfaff for Congress This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com
Pfaff for Congress
PO Box 3606
La Crosse, WI 54602
United States

name: Patrick Clark, Keystone Progress
subject: Keep fighting for the promises of democracy.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
Dear Friend,

"Now is the time to make real the promises of democracy."

These words by the great Martin Luther King Jr. keep us focused and inspired to fight for voting rights on this day dedicated to his legacy, and every single day of the year. At Keystone Progress, we are using this as a mantra in continuing to fight for our democracy.

This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we are mobilizing in opposition to two pending, terrible and harmful proposed amendments to the Pennsylvania Constitution: H.B. 38, which allows for gerrymandering in judicial elections, and H.B. 2207, which, instead of reforming the redistricting process, creates a sham "citizen's commission" to keep power in the hands of the majority party in each chamber of the
General Assembly.

We need to fight this kind of trickery, these kinds of cruel laws that keep voters silenced. We need to raise up and support the will of the people.

Help us keep democracy alive by visiting our online advocacy page TODAY!

Email your state lawmakers to tell them they must reject H.B. 38 and H.B. 2207 and other similar amendments and laws that hurt Pennsylvanians. You can also write to your Congresspeople — that includes Sen. Toomey — to tell them they MUST stand up for our voting rights in Congress!

ACT NOW! [ https://keystoneprogressaction.com/mlk-day/]

Thank you for standing with us in the light, following the brilliance and vision of Martin Luther King Jr.

Pat Clark
Keystone Progress

P.S. If you can, please also donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-progressives-in-pa?refcode=mlk] to help us preserve our voting rights here in PA. We need to fight now so that this never happens again.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-progressives-in-pa?refcode=mlk [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-progressives-in-pa?refcode=mlk]

https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/ [https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/]

Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www.kpef.info. Not affiliated with any candidate or candidate's committees.

Keystone Progress
354 S Atlantic Ave
Pittsburgh, PA 15224

name: Mark Holland
subject: Bend the Arc
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Holland for Kansas
body:
Mark Holland is running for US Senate because Kansas and America deserve a strong, courageous leader who leads with faith, speaks the truth, and is willing to stand up to extremists across the political spectrum. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/7c4a46d7-ba74-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442/1e43cbd5-ac77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZTQzY2JkNS1hYzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=25ebO2LCM3TY-S3QUz7carAbrxNI3GLbGfDC83cl5Jw=&id=103697760)
---
| Dear friends,

Reverend Doctor Martin Luther King, Jr. has always been an inspiration to me. I love to hear my parents talk of meeting him when he preached at their church in Chicago. When my college applications called for an essay on a role model, I wrote about MLK. I remember when this became a national holiday—a day off work, and a day on for justice. I have been proud to walk through the awe-inspiring monument for him and his work in Washington DC.

My favorite quotation of Dr. King is, “the arc of the moral universe is long, and it bends toward justice.” Friends, we are called to be arc benders. The work that Dr. King and his entire generation did for voting rights is under attack. There is chronic gerrymandering to water down voices. And now, Republican legislators across the country are attacking access to the polls. All of this is fueled by the chronic lie that Donald Trump won the 2020 election—he did not.

Let us be clear, the Republicans are targeting election integrity ONLY in urban areas with a high percentage of minority voters. This is wrong. We know it. We must act.

I am running for US Senate because Jerry Moran is supporting voter suppression across the country with his unwillingness to speak out. Justice does not come from silence. Change comes from bold, clear voices who have the courage to stand up for what is right.

Thank you,
Mark

---

**Meet Mark:** Reverend Mark Holland is a fourth generation Kansan and a third generation United Methodist Pastor. He has dedicated his life to the service of others, as a father, pastor, community leader, elected official, and non-profit leader. Mark is the co-founder and executive director of Mainstream UMC, a global advocacy organization dedicated to greater inclusion in the church. He has served the Church in Kansas for over 25 years, most recently as the Senior Pastor of Trinity Community Church in Kansas City, Kansas for 19 years.

clean_body: Mark Holland is running for US Senate because Kansas and America deserve a strong, courageous leader who leads with faith, speaks the truth, and is willing to stand up to extremists across the political spectrum. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | Dear friends, Reverend Doctor Martin Luther King, Jr. has always been an inspiration to me. I love to hear my parents talk of meeting him when he preached at their church in Chicago. When my college applications called for an essay on a role model, I wrote about MLK. I remember when this became a national holiday—a day off work, and a day on for justice. I have been proud to walk through the awe-inspiring monument for him and his work in Washington DC. My favorite quotation of Dr. King is, “the arc of the moral universe is long, and it bends toward justice.” Friends, we are called to be arc benders. The work that Dr. King and his entire generation did for voting rights is under attack. There is chronic gerrymandering to water down voices. And now, Republican legislators across the country are attacking access to the polls. All of this is fueled by the chronic lie that Donald Trump won the 2020 election—he did not. Let us be clear, the Republicans are targeting election integrity ONLY in urban areas with a high percentage of minority voters. This is wrong. We know it. We must act. I am running for US Senate because Jerry Moran is supporting voter suppression across the country with his unwillingness to speak out. Justice does not come from silence. Change comes from bold, clear voices who have the courage to stand up for what is right. Thank you, Mark | | | = Meet Mark: Reverend Mark Holland is a fourth generation Kansan and a third generation United Methodist Pastor. He has dedicated his life to the service of others, as a father, pastor, community leader, elected official, and non-profit leader. Mark is the co-founder and executive director of Mainstream UMC, a global advocacy organization dedicated to greater inclusion in the church. He has served the Church in Kansas for over 25 years, most recently as the Senior Pastor of Trinity Community Church in Kansas City, Kansas for 19 years. ** Paid for by Holland for Kansas Holland for Kansas PO Box 171236 Kansas City, KS 66117 United States

name: Susie Lee
subject: Remembering Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Susie Lee for Congress
body:
| | Folks — On this Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we remember one of the greatest civil rights leaders in our nation’s history. Dr. King’s voice inspired thousands to reject racism and march towards a more equal and equitable society. Dr. King’s mission was an American one at its core. He stood for equal opportunities for public education, equal justice in the court of law, and equal access to the ballot box. These rights ought to be protected for every American citizen, no questions asked. The legacy that Dr. King left behind has inspired generations of activists and leaders to keep up the good fight because while we’ve made progress, **there’s still work left to be done.** Today, let’s reflect on the work that Dr. King had accomplished and recommit to doing everything we can to make our country one where civil rights prevail in the face of discrimination. Just as it was back in the days of Dr. King, the time is always right to do what is right. Together in unity, Susie Lee

name: Ann Roe
subject: We owe it to Dr. King
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANN ROE FOR CONGRESS Ann Roe for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772552/325866379/-766995749?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zTj7rYDwkpoVgXZcDb1-zoyTnjIvPoiauvhW2OaQ1nY=&emci=c4b1d198-5875-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=6d068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15639879)

Hi Friend,

This year we have a chance to make Martin Luther King Jr. Day _more_ than just another day off. I can think of no better way to honor the late civil rights leader than by demanding action on federal voting rights legislation.

I am in agreement with Bernice King when she says: **"Instead of taking the King holiday off, they [Congress] should make it a day ON to pass the Voting Rights Act."**

We remember Dr. King for various reasons: he reflects the best Americans can be when we come together to fight for change, but most of all we remember him for dedicating his life to fighting for the freedoms of everyone in our country, _especially_ the right to vote.

The best way we can honor his memory is through **_action._**

We are calling on the Senate to pass the **Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act** , which would expand voting opportunities and increase campaign finance transparency, while also restoring key provisions in the landmark 1965 Voting Rights Act that the Supreme Court weakened in 2013.

There is no better time than _now_. And it's time we use our voices to push for action from our lawmakers. **[Please, join me in demanding that Congress truly honors Dr. King's legacy, and passes the Voting Rights Legislation Act>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772556/325866386/843454514?ms=em22_220117_mlkday&refcode=em22_220117_mlkday&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zTj7rYDwkpoVgXZcDb1-zoyTnjIvPoiauvhW2OaQ1nY=&emci=c4b1d198-5875-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=6d068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15639879)**

It is time to change Senate filibuster rules. For decades, the filibuster has been used for decades to kill civil rights legislation. It is a Jim Crow relic and the longer it's in place, the longer it will be abused. Our processes must evolve with our democracy.

Anything that allows for the continuation of restrictive voting laws has to go. This is not a reflection of the America we want to build together.

**[Will you join me in calling on lawmakers in Congress to fulfill Dr. King's message by passing the Voting Rights Legislation Act to expand and protect access to the ballot?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772560/325866389/843454514?ms=em22_220117_mlkday&refcode=em22_220117_mlkday&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zTj7rYDwkpoVgXZcDb1-zoyTnjIvPoiauvhW2OaQ1nY=&emci=c4b1d198-5875-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=6d068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15639879)**

clean_body: | Hi Friend, This year we have a chance to make Martin Luther King Jr. Day more than just another day off. I can think of no better way to honor the late civil rights leader than by demanding action on federal voting rights legislation. I am in agreement with Bernice King when she says: "Instead of taking the King holiday off, they [Congress] should make it a day ON to pass the Voting Rights Act." We remember Dr. King for various reasons: he reflects the best Americans can be when we come together to fight for change, but most of all we remember him for dedicating his life to fighting for the freedoms of everyone in our country, especially the right to vote. The best way we can honor his memory is through action. We are calling on the Senate to pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act , which would expand voting opportunities and increase campaign finance transparency, while also restoring key provisions in the landmark 1965 Voting Rights Act that the Supreme Court weakened in 2013. There is no better time than now. And it's time we use our voices to push for action from our lawmakers. It is time to change Senate filibuster rules. For decades, the filibuster has been used for decades to kill civil rights legislation. It is a Jim Crow relic and the longer it's in place, the longer it will be abused. Our processes must evolve with our democracy. Anything that allows for the continuation of restrictive voting laws has to go. This is not a reflection of the America we want to build together. We owe it to the legacy of Dr. King to continue to fight for every American's right to vote and their right to have their vote counted. – Ann Roe = = *

name: Ben Samuels
subject: Remembering Dr. King
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BEN SAMUELS FOR CONGRESS
body:
Friends,
When I think about the state of the world today, I worry that in a few years our democracy will no longer exist. State legislators all over the country are positioning themselves to overturn election results, disenfranchise voters, and gerrymander their way to victory.
Martin Luther King Junior symbolizes the long struggle for civil rights in this country. King spent his life promoting radical love, compassionate leadership, nonviolent resistance, and the empowerment of the electorate.
At a moment of increased divisiveness, electoral denialism, and regressive legislation, King's message is more important than ever. King said, “Love is the only force capable of transforming an enemy into a friend.” Today, I invite you to recommit yourself to equality, democracy, and justice.
In solidarity,
Ben Samuels
Ben Samuels is a businessperson, an economic policy expert, and a bipartisan problem-solver running for Congress in Missouri’s 2nd Congressional District to bring jobs and opportunities back to the St. Louis area.
Support Ben's campaign for Congress by making a donation today.

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: Continuing Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Today we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. – a civil rights leader dedicated to liberty and justice for all Americans. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772584/325866499/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=222f-43nufwdK1IdGhw5KixBtwJDxoRDYChIcu5rS4s=&emci=32e32645-8a75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=70068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

Today we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. – a civil rights leader dedicated to liberty and justice for all Americans.

Nearly 60 years ago, Dr. King led the March on Washington calling for civil rights and voting rights protections. This march helped lead to the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which outlawed discrimination at the ballot box. In the nearly six decades since, we've made even more progress towards equity and justice. But change has come slowly and our work is far from over.

Last year, attempts to make it harder for some Americans to vote reached levels unseen since the Voting Rights Act was signed into law. These laws were designed by the Heritage Foundation to advantage Republicans, and were introduced – and many passed – by Republicans in state legislatures across the country. Common themes in these 400 bills include provisions to decrease the number of polling locations, restrictions on ways people can vote and when they can vote, and even laws that would allow Secretaries of State or state legislatures to throw out and change the results of an election. These bills disproportionately impact Black Americans and young Americans.

**And these efforts to suppress voting rights and undermine election results are nothing less than a threat to our democracy.**

That's why in Congress I'm fighting back against any attempts to suppress or invalidate votes. Last year, I was proud to help pass the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act through the House to restore key provisions of the Voting Rights. And just last week, I joined other Democrats in the House to help pass a set of voting rights bills to ensure equal access to the ballot box for all Americans and to ensure every vote counts. But we still need the Senate to act.

**[On this Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day, will you recommit to the fight to protect the right to vote for _all_ Americans? Add your name if you agree it's time to stand up to voter suppression.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772585/325866505/-712343568?ms=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=FzSS%2FAocX5naLYythmqAJf%2BfWruBfZiQwaGWsvR8agIielzEHq7s5L%2B9XaRjp7hP52td8qn2wGMDQBpBJGdQthDhGWFeYRyuI4mTqKl2cV9GwmNxeaAZPFIIO1aYsYwQuDnFZmJGtAJ9NHhSUIdGRSJbJH6jjL3VUOap0cyI7r8%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=222f-43nufwdK1IdGhw5KixBtwJDxoRDYChIcu5rS4s=&emci=32e32645-8a75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=70068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15820338) ** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40772586/325866506/-712343568?ms=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220117_mlkday_adv_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=FzSS%2FAocX5naLYythmqAJf%2BfWruBfZiQwaGWsvR8agIielzEHq7s5L%2B9XaRjp7hP52td8qn2wGMDQBpBJGdQthDhGWFeYRyuI4mTqKl2cV9GwmNxeaAZPFIIO1aYsYwQuDnFZmJGtAJ9NHhSUIdGRSJbJH6jjL3VUOap0cyI7r8%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MDA2ODU4NC1hZTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=222f-43nufwdK1IdGhw5KixBtwJDxoRDYChIcu5rS4s=&emci=32e32645-8a75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=70068584-ae77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15820338)

Kim

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: honoring Dr. King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
It is our responsibility to find in ourselves his courage

| Team,

Today, we commemorate the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Through his tireless work, Dr. King forced our country to confront painful truths about injustice and inequality. This struggle persists today, as Black Americans and people of color continue to face hatred and systemic racism – in housing, employment, the criminal justice system, voter suppression efforts, and more.

In a time of rising racism, anti-Semitism, Islamophobia, xenophobia, and white nationalism, it is our responsibility to find in ourselves his courage and continually find ways to protect and build on his legacy, today and every day.

These issues are especially salient as we work to pass meaningful and much-needed voting rights legislation to strengthen and protect our democracy. I was proud to work shoulder to shoulder with my colleagues in the House last week to pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act, to ensure that all Americans have an equal voice in our democracy.

May Dr. King’s work inspire us all to continue fighting for his vision of social, racial, and economic justice for all.

In solidarity,
Sara
---

name: Seth Magaziner
subject: Dr. King's legacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SETH MAGAZINER FOR GOVERNOR
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40773371/325870888/-1862136002?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzEwYjlkNi1iMDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UugNFRFxaH94-zuV9OppqfaN-Ccwhb9fwphFbQrL2r4=&emci=75886476-aa77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=c710b9d6-b077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15384964)
Peter,

Today on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we take time to honor the legacy of a man who dedicated his life to fighting for justice, equality, and freedom for all.

One of Dr. King’s many great achievements was passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Last year, the US Supreme Court gutted the protections of the Voting Rights Act and since then, we've seen new voter suppression laws across the country that disproportionately impact Black Americans.

It's up to us to protect the voting rights that Dr. King fought for and to ensure that everyone can freely exercise their right to vote. That‘s why Congress must pass federal legislation to protect the right to vote.

We still have a long way to go to achieve the dream that Dr. King envisioned, but I believe we can bend the arc of the moral universe towards justice and build a better future for the next generation.

Today I encourage you to reflect on the work of Dr. King and think about ways we can all work together to build a state and country with economic opportunity, freedom, and justice for all.

Thank you,

Seth

name: Fairness Project
subject: Remembering Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40773419/325871213/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjYyODI0Yy1iMTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LnaI1M2JFoq155hZ6BY1pctKjrF5ojJYUVAv-CNU01g=&emci=bd977639-7a75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=9662824c-b177-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=561582) | Friends — Here at Fairness Project, we are honoring Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. today, and that means remembering his political demand for the _total_ enfranchisement of Black Americans across the country. A demand which includes unimpeded access to elections, non-discriminatory employment practices, and economic empowerment. We believe the potential to make real progress on this issue exists everywhere and we’re prepared to do the work to turn that potential into policy. Dr. King and his allies laid out the vital framework. Now, it’s up to our generation to make good on those old hopes and promises. Today, and every day, we reflect on Dr. King’s long, impactful career of social justice activism, and think about how our work can aspire to meet his urgent political demand for fairness. — Fairness Project
---

clean_body: | | Friends — Here at Fairness Project, we are honoring Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. today, and that means remembering his political demand for the total enfranchisement of Black Americans across the country. A demand which includes unimpeded access to elections, non-discriminatory employment practices, and economic empowerment. We believe the potential to make real progress on this issue exists everywhere and we’re prepared to do the work to turn that potential into policy. Dr. King and his allies laid out the vital framework. Now, it’s up to our generation to make good on those old hopes and promises. Today, and every day, we reflect on Dr. King’s long, impactful career of social justice activism, and think about how our work can aspire to meet his urgent political demand for fairness. — Fairness Project = Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 23 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. Contributions will not necessarily be used to support activity in any one state. You can request to get only the most important emails from our team , or you can . name: Mark Walker
subject: Honoring MLK Jr. today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Walker4NC
body:
| | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5dffe9cf8697fc0a3a89ac0c84d83476/35903/7c9cebb47a204add/94fae3f2597d4903c3d6b838967d376c/35903)
---
Peter, Today, we celebrate a man who changed the course of history.: Martin Luther King Jr. Dr. King's legacy has echoed on in our shared commitment to unity, particularly during these difficult times.

In the words of Dr. King: "We may have all come on different ships, but we're in the same boat now." So many Americans are hurting these days, and even with the good work done by Dr. King and others towards breaking down barriers of inequality, many of our communities in North Carolina are still suffering. Together, we must work hard to make North Carolina and our nation a better place for all Americans.

One of the things I am proudest of during my time in Congress is I have always been able to find a way to build bridges. Whether it's in our local community or on a national level. I helped direct more funds to Historically Black Colleges and Universities, and I introduced the Prison to Prosperity Act to help lift barriers to opportunity across North Carolina. America became a beacon for the world and a land of opportunity like no other only through unity and perseverance. We can still work together for an even stronger and more united future.
---
In remembrance,
Mark Walker
---

name: Team Fair Fight Action
subject: The best way to honor Dr. King’s legacy? Pass voting rights NOW.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
Black and brown voters in Georgia and across the country are looking to Senators to stand on the right side of history by passing the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.

Fair Fight |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40773487/325871503/1793776944?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNDFkODMzMy1iMTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=we5zk3X059fQaBPDR7WUK4NB2_zNQQX-cBQGrM3ValI=&emci=5594b74c-b077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=b41d8333-b177-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1037277)

| | Friend, Dr. King dedicated his life to ensuring that all eligible Americans—whatever our color, background, or zipcode—have equal freedom to vote and equal say in the decisions that impact our lives. So today, as we reflect on the contributions of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., we must also ask our United States Senators one simple question: Will your legacy protect or deny our freedom to vote? **In this moment, there is no greater way to honor Dr. King’s legacy than by passing the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.** Black and brown voters in Georgia and across the country are looking to Senators to stand on the right side of history by delivering federal legislation to protect our freedom to vote. As Dr. King wrote in his 1963 Letter from Birmingham Jail: “ _I think the tragedy is that we have a Congress with a Senate that has a minority of misguided senators who will use the filibuster to keep the majority of people from even voting_.” Sadly, Dr. King’s words ring just as true today as when first written almost 60 years ago. **Failure to deliver the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act will put senators on the wrong side of history**. Our democracy hangs in the balance. **Anti-voter Republican lawmakers in states across the country are hard at work attempting to silence the voices of countless voters of color**. In 2021 alone, **34 laws** in **19 different states** were enacted that restrict access to the ballot box. The **Senate must act now to pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act** so that every vote counts and we all have a say in our future. Every senator who fails to rise up and protect our freedom to vote will contribute to the irreversible fall of American democracy. Their legacies, and our legacy as a country, are on the line. **The country is watching**. **History is watching**. Senators have the power—and the responsibility—to lead. We cannot allow the voices of millions of voters to be silenced. The Senate must be restored. **Call your Senators today and every day this week at 833-345-2552 to demand they honor the legacies of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and Congressman John Lewis by passing the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act—immediately. ** Thank you for doing your part to honor Dr. King by joining us in this fight. In Solidarity, — Team Fair Fight Action
|
---|---|---

|
---

subject: Remember & Reflect
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
Friends, Today is a day to remember the bright and inspiring legacy of the Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., and ensure his legacy lives on as we work towards a more just society. I am continuously inspired by the perseverance of this great leader and man of faith – a champion of the people whose hope for this country's future was never diminished. In these divided times, let's reflect on the thoughts of Dr. King and use his wisdom to bring together an America impacted by so much that is pulling us apart. I hope that we can use this day to remember the greatness and humility of this inspiring leader. Thank you, Tom O'Halleran
---

name: Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Action for the week of January 17th: Dems are still going nuclear!
body:
| |
---
Chuck Schumer recessed the Senate at the end of last week, even though he really didn’t want to do that. This means that he will restart the Democrats’ efforts to eliminate the filibuster and take over our elections this week, beginning today, Tuesday.

While Sens. Manchin and Sinema both confirmed last week that they will NOT vote to change or weaken the filibuster, it’s never over until it’s over in politics – and even then, we must always remain vigilant.

Therefore, please make sure to once again use our AlignAct platform to email, call, and target Senators on social media this week. Depending on what happens, we may have an updated call to action later in the week, but for now, all of us need to repeat the actions from last week – because **_THEY ARE WORKING!_**

**Note: You can use each campaign as many times as needed! If you completed them last week, do them again this week!**
** **
If you should encounter problems today, please stop trying to use the system and just call the targeted Senators listed in each campaign. Then, email us and let us know the problems you had and we will pass them on to the AlignAct team.****
** **
**The priority is to call Sens. Manchin and Sinema first, and then call your own Senators as well. The message is simply, “Save the Senate. Do not change the filibuster.” Please be polite when you call, particularly to Manchin and Sinema’s offices – tell them THANK YOU!**
** **
**Let us sum up what the Democrats are trying to do. Democrats want to take over the Senate, to take over elections, to take over the country – forever. Don’t. Let. Them.**
|
---
[](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.)
This campaign targets Sens. Manchin and Sinema, encouraging them to stay strong in their support of the filibuster.
[TAKE ACTION](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.)
|
---
[Inflation has risen 7% over the past year](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.-7EQCb4TckJ31PGtjxfdg), the U.S. Department of Labor[ announced](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.) last week, marking the highest increase in 40 years. Unleaded gasoline, up 50.8%, led the [Consumer Price Index](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._Z0y6f-TOkVWjj8Q), and can be tied to new regulations the Biden administration has imposed on oil and gas producers. Other leading inflation indicators included used cars and trucks, up 37%; and hotel stays, up 27%. Groceries increased by 6.5% overall with meats, poultry, fish and eggs rising by 12.5%.

Democrats were alone in voting for Biden’s inflation-stoking $1.9 trillion spending package last year. And as a clear signal of just how far out of touch the administration remains, Biden’s chief economic advisor last week touted an additional $2 trillion in spending through the Build Back Socialist scheme as the[ antidote](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._sGNN-SV20wdOhAB7Svg) to runaway inflation.

Bad economic news for Americans also spells political bad news for Biden. The president’s approval rating has sunk to an[ all-time low of 33%,](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.-nWcaceJiCow) according to a Quinnipiac poll released last week. Biden’s freefall is of increasing concern to Democrats; whispers of[ Hillary in 2024](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/BUtpVV9eyR3U27ukq44oHg) are proliferating and Democrat activist[ Stacey Abrams snubbed an invitation](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/7/eyJhaSI6NDkxMDk0ODksImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRIiwicnEiOiIwMi1iMjIwMTctNzU2ODEwMDQ0NDRhNDcyNmE4YTBlNGE4M2E2NDY4MjQiLCJwaCI6bnVsbCwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiOCIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vdHdpdHRlci5jb20vTllUbmlja2Mvc3RhdHVzLzE0ODA2Njk3MjcwNTE1NzkzOTQ_cz0yMCZtaWQ9NDQ4MjUmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRIn0/SvBxsQUPIfCw7A-ySRA3NQ) from the president to appear with him during an Atlanta appearance last week.
|
---
[](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.)
This campaign targets several Democrat Senators who signed a 2017 letter defending and supporting the filibuster.
[TAKE ACTION](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._OnyW4A)
|
---
Surprise! [Roughly half of the 104,000 illegal immigrants](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.-r9YK3ly7Aj5Qx5P3bg) placed in a new Homeland Security program relying on the honor system are “missing.” Under terms of the “Notice to Report” program, migrants are permitted to travel to the U.S. destination of their choice. Upon arrival, migrants are required to report to ICE, after which a deportation hearing would be held. The NTR program was initiated in March 2021 with the intent to reduce overcrowding in detention facilities.

A shocking new[ DHS report](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.) reveals that more than 50,000 migrants entrusted to self-report over a six-month span last year failed to check in with ICE, and have, in effect, disappeared into the American interior.

The Biden administration’s NTR program reverses Trump administration policy under which migrants seeking entry into the United States were required to check in with an ICE office at the border, and then remain in Mexico for the duration of their immigration proceedings. Not only does Biden policy permit migrants to cross the border, but for the duration of their proceedings, migrants are provided housing, food stamps and Medicaid benefits, courtesy of the American taxpayer.
|
---
[](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/DTfXG9cb8J33hnYgy1kYNg)
This campaign allows you to contact your own Senators, urging them both to defend the filibuster and to vote no on the election takeover bills.
[TAKE ACTION](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/C68bgcFAkDRsL8K4ABpm-Q)
|
---
[(NSBA) claim in internal emails](http://lnk.teapartypatriots._bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/XfR1AG4n0fP78qBNiQA7ZQ) that Education Secretary, Miguel Cardona, requested a letter from the organization about the threat to school boards.

The letter the NSBA sent to President Biden compared concerned parents to terrorists and asked the government to intervene. The Department of Justice responded to this letter by asking the FBI to help local officials monitor parents.

Cardona has denied these claims, but the American people deserve to know the truth. If Cardona caused the NSBA letter, it is another example of the president targeting his political opponents. Any opposition to critical race theory or other indoctrination efforts is now labeled as domestic terrorism.
|
---
[CONTRIBUTE](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/7/eyJhaSI6NDkxMDk0ODksImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRIiwicnEiOiIwMi1iMjIwMTctNzU2ODEwMDQ0NDRhNDcyNmE4YTBlNGE4M2E2NDY4MjQiLCJwaCI6bnVsbCwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMTYiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy50ZWFwYXJ0eXBhdHJpb3RzLm9yZy9kb25hdGU_bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/5QNPD4b9rH4YrkakhEmYLg)
|
---
[](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/7/eyJhaSI6NDkxMDk0ODksImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRIiwicnEiOiIwMi1iMjIwMTctNzU2ODEwMDQ0NDRhNDcyNmE4YTBlNGE4M2E2NDY4MjQiLCJwaCI6bnVsbCwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMTciLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy50ZWFwYXJ0eXBhdHJpb3RzLm9yZy8_bWlkPTQ0ODI1JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUSJ9/Z-KTvWICnp0XZfnHD27ksg)

name: Nina Turner
subject: [WATCH] Legacy of MLK Roundtable with me, Sen. Bernie Sanders, Dr. Cornel West, and Michael Render (Killer Mike)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina Turner for Us
body:
Hello Somebody!
In 2016, I participated in a roundtable discussion on the legacy of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., alongside Sen. Bernie Sanders, Dr. Cornel West, and my good friend Michael Render (Killer Mike).
It’s a powerful discussion, and one that is very much still relevant today. We talk about Dr. King’s life, his work with the poor people’s movement, and the legacy of his activism — from ending racial & economic injustice to fighting militarism and corporate greed.
**[Watch the Legacy of MLK roundtable with me, Senator Bernie Sanders, Dr. Cornel West, and Michael Render (Killer Mike).](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40774021/325874289/-803653937?v=befhumio-wo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWEwYjAyMS1iMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pELKXm6WXAd7HntNBkq83ePg7XllJG_TY1Yp98MBW7w=&emci=c178ac0e-e076-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=65a0b021-b377-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12102799)**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40774026/325874293/-803653937?v=befhumio-wo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWEwYjAyMS1iMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pELKXm6WXAd7HntNBkq83ePg7XllJG_TY1Yp98MBW7w=&emci=c178ac0e-e076-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=65a0b021-b377-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12102799)
One thing we get at in the discussion that I think is important to emphasize is that while Dr. King was a great man and humanitarian, he was not an isolated icon. He was part of a movement and a tradition of organizing and resistance that stretches back hundreds of years and continues to this day.
He understood that change never starts with the powerful, coming from the top on down. It always comes from the bottom on up, when a group of people are prepared to come together and fight for justice.
**[Please watch the Legacy of MLK roundtable with me, Senator Bernie Sanders, Dr. Cornel West, and Michael Render (Killer Mike). Today especially, we all have a lot to learn from Dr. King’s life and legacy of activism.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40774027/325874298/-803653937?v=befhumio-wo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWEwYjAyMS1iMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pELKXm6WXAd7HntNBkq83ePg7XllJG_TY1Yp98MBW7w=&emci=c178ac0e-e076-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=65a0b021-b377-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12102799)**
Thank you,
Nina Turner

* * *
Paid for by Nina Turner for Us

Cleveland, OH 44101
United States This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Jameson Ellis
subject: I had a brain fart on this podcast...
body:
but it's a side of me you didn't know existed.

| | | | |
---
| | Good morning Peter,

I was recently featured on a podcast called Hank's Think Tank, and it was raw and uncut.

We talked about my reason for running, the border crisis, national defense, voter confidence, my personal voting record, and... brain farts.

Yeah, I had one or two...

But it was a great discussion, nonetheless.

Tap below to check it out and see a side of me you may not have known existed.

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Remembering Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#]Today, we remember Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the Civil Rights Movement.

His groundbreaking work in the 1950s and 60s shaped America into the country we know today. During this time of national uncertainty, I’m thinking about his dream for this nation.

Dr. King told us what America could be, and I believe in his dream. I’m determined to make his dream, and the dreams of millions of Americans, a reality.

We are diverse. We are dynamic. We are hopeful for the future. But we still have work to do to make this country equal and just for all of us.

So, today, please take a moment with me to remember the work of Dr. King and others like Congressman John Lewis who fought tirelessly for our country.

Tomorrow, please join me in continuing to carry their torch forward, so that our best days are always ahead of us.

Yours,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: Dr. Kathleen Harder
subject: Shocking and inhumane
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kathleen Harder for Congress
body:
Peter,
Today we honor the life, work, and dreams of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., a man motivated by love and the ideals that every being has the innate right to life, freedom, and dignity. When speaking in front of the Medical Committee on Human Rights in March of 1966, Dr. King declared:
http://www.drkathleenharder.com/ [http://www.drkathleenharder.com/]
Dr. King dedicated his life to bettering this country and bringing us closer to the light of equality in every facet of our society. He believed in it so strongly that he lost his life for it. That’s why it’s important today that we honor him by doing something kind for someone else or helping folks in need.
Dr. King believed that we could build a better world through empathy, compassion, and kindness. So as you go about your day today, think about how you can better live with those values so that we can all strive towards the world Dr. King envisioned together.
Thank you so much.
- Dr. Kathleen Harder

| | Friends, As Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. said, “Everybody can be great – because _anybody_ can serve.” Today and every day, we honor those who stand up and serve in the face of injustice and remember Dr. King’s powerful legacy. His vision for a true democracy has never been more needed. We still have work ahead of us to achieve equality for every American, to promote better jobs and higher wages, and to truly know justice and know peace. May we choose to answer Dr. King’s call to action every day, so that together, we may realize his dream for future generations. In solidarity, Elaine Luria

name: NextGen America
subject: This MLK Day, we’re urging the Senate to pass voting rights legislation.
body:
| | Friend, **On this day, we honor the legacy of Martin Luther King Jr.** He dedicated his life to bringing this country forward to a brighter, more equitable future. Today, we’re encouraged to follow his example and step up to serve our communities. However, with voting rights under attack in this country, we can’t celebrate this day in good faith without standing up for our democracy. The filibuster is an obsolete relic of the Jim Crow era that Republicans are using to block critical legislation that would make voting safe and accessible ahead of the midterm elections. **In tribute to Dr. King, Majority Leader Chuck Schumer has introduced a plan to streamline the passage of the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.** This new bill combines key provisions of previous voting rights legislation that would create national standards to ensure we can safely, freely, and equally cast our ballots, as well as unequivocally strike down deliberate barriers to voting that target voters of color. **Tomorrow, all 50 Senate Democrats must find the courage, as Dr. King did, to fight for our fundamental right to vote.** They must exercise their majority, bypass the filibuster, and pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act. We have momentum on our side, but all 50 Senate Democrats must unify and act fast. [**Send a message: Tell your senators to bypass the filibuster so we can pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40774381/325877493/-1986195355?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI5MWFlNS1iNjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ruzI7wBlgB3Jf5CHuU-VWmqkc2VTY2Pyq8JnCE4fllU=&emci=34640f10-8c75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=22b91ae5-b677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6787172) This holiday comes a week after President Biden finally endorsed Senate rules reforms to fight back against relentless Republican obstruction. This could be a turning point in our efforts to safeguard the right to vote and make the promise of democracy real for us all — if Senate Democrats act. **No more empty promises in pursuit of the dream for a more equal America. Now’s the time to deliver on federal voting rights legislation.** [**Write to your senators: Abolish the filibuster and expand voting rights by passing the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40774383/325877495/742688000?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI5MWFlNS1iNjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ruzI7wBlgB3Jf5CHuU-VWmqkc2VTY2Pyq8JnCE4fllU=&emci=34640f10-8c75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=22b91ae5-b677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6787172) | [Send message](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40774385/325877497/-823395941?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI5MWFlNS1iNjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ruzI7wBlgB3Jf5CHuU-VWmqkc2VTY2Pyq8JnCE4fllU=&emci=34640f10-8c75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=22b91ae5-b677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6787172 "CTA Button")

name: Shontel Brown
subject: On this MLK Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
We’ve got a lot to commemorate and even more to protect.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40774417/325877562/1378773197?refcode=EM_FR_220117MLK_N_SB_90DO_3_G_N_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGI5MWFlNS1iNjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9vUxOYrwWp1hG-URV4ACDicjcKcSEZZvivJZInkVGxY=) Peter,

Reverend Martin Luther King Jr. was nothing short of a trailblazer for civil rights and justice for Black Americans. He and so many other civil rights leaders are why we have our voting rights today.

Now, those voting rights that civil rights leaders fought so hard for are under attack. We must do everything to protect them. We need the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act.

So as we commemorate Martin Luther King Jr. and the accomplishments of the Civil Rights Movement, let’s look forward to how we’ll protect our fundamental right to vote today and continue to bend the arc toward justice!

Thank you,

Shontel Brown |
As a Cuyahoga County Council member and Chairwoman of the Cuyahoga County Democratic Party, Shontel Brown has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. Now, as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District, she will fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and equity and justice for all. **Will you join her and be the change we need?**

clean_body: We’ve got a lot to commemorate and even more to protect. = Peter, Reverend Martin Luther King Jr. was nothing short of a trailblazer for civil rights and justice for Black Americans. He and so many other civil rights leaders are why we have our voting rights today. Now, those voting rights that civil rights leaders fought so hard for are under attack. We must do everything to protect them. We need the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act. So as we commemorate Martin Luther King Jr. and the accomplishments of the Civil Rights Movement, let’s look forward to how we’ll protect our fundamental right to vote today and continue to bend the arc toward justice! Thank you, Shontel Brown | As a Cuyahoga County Council member and Chairwoman of the Cuyahoga County Democratic Party, Shontel Brown has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. Now, as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District, she will fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and equity and justice for all. Will you join her and be the change we need? = Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress Shontel Brown for Congress PO Box 221232 Beachwood, OH 44122 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

body:
We need you there | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/43664422ad5d/do-you-recognize-our-state?e=33b11688c1)
---
| |
---
| | Peter,

Do you recognize what Minnesota has become? Decades of far-left leadership has left us with a severe criminality problem, endless lockdowns and mandates, skyrocketing taxes and inflation, and a mass exodus of friends and families who have been here for years.

If we don't turn this around quickly, we're not going to have a state to leave to our kids. At the top of the chaos and destruction is Tim Walz, who has at best been SILENT on the issues that matter most to Minnesotans. We need YOU to [**attend precinct caucus**](https://DrScottJensen.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0a6593cc14b86d0dcb3944b28&id=868fe9b413&e=33b11688c1)[**es on February 1st**](https://DrScottJensen.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0a6593cc14b86d0dcb3944b28&id=aa1a5644cf&e=33b11688c1) and become a delegate, so we can pave the way to victory in November!

We need a candidate and campaign that can galvanize conservatives and independents throughout Minnesota. **That's what we're doing.** We are running on restoring SAFETY on our streets, supporting and funding STUDENTS not BROKEN institutions, returning to SCIENCE (instead of political science), and securing our elections. **That message is resonating across the state!**
---
|
---
| **Our campaign is the most equipped campaign to beat Tim Walz.**
* We continuously turn out hundreds to our rallies and events, getting new people engaged who've NEVER participated before.
* We smashed small-dollar fundraising records for an off-cycle election year, relying on moms and dads and farmers and teachers and dishwashers, who are begging for a better future!
* We are already making the case in places like Minneapolis and St. Paul, to voters who've never voted Republican before. It's working because we're working!
* We consistently have the highest favorables among Republicans and Independents in the polls.
* We are not running a campaign like any other politician. The definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over again while expecting different results. We're talking to new voters, going places where Republicans haven't gone before, and MAKING THE CASE that this conservative movement is for ALL Minnesotans.
* We continue to take slings and arrows from the Left! The DFL almost exclusively attacks me \--- and my medical license is now under investigation for a FIFTH time because we threaten the medical and political establishment. **Democrats know who the biggest threat is against their agenda: our campaign.**

---
| Now we need you to get off the sidelines! Get engaged and get out to your caucus on February 1st! You can find where your caucus location is by going to [our website](https://DrScottJensen.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0a6593cc14b86d0dcb3944b28&id=754c4d26f7&e=33b11688c1) or the [Secretary of State's site ](https://DrScottJensen.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0a6593cc14b86d0dcb3944b28&id=6bb534d182&e=33b11688c1)directly. If we're going to change Minnesota for the better, it requires your participation!

**Let's win and turn our state around![See you at precinct caucuses](https://DrScottJensen.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0a6593cc14b86d0dcb3944b28&id=57d4a66e2e&e=33b11688c1)!**

In Freedom,
Dr. Scott Jensen
Republican Candidate for Governor - Minnesota
