name: Casey DeSantis
subject: Democrats' Disgraceful Attack
body:
| | | | [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001uvJNF6VPwWc1kCDe-AzQakWI4fzXiCWPRYiEqvzIyEWTNhG17WdAyi8URzQeDiHm1NmeXQ7f-Ym6kB12NUeJ9ALyDXRqyhHkK7On3-GQRPFbjmREJWtmNxyoDEc4BQLQUFWAx2NDnmhAWUXbbAw7yP4UV-PTFP1IuNkH2NO-9W7u3CQWbSiJmF7J_q6p8qg2&c=wvO90BJwQhoL4x2lLhDt0kRYWLNKBlQ0t6yQKSn1HqDVYB2fP5AF5w==&ch=lyH-gUcZ4tm3bSCEmsdRbLIsGeEf4HCLPWBLEHUoUGdd929XIYebGg==)
---
| Peter,
My husband and our governor, Ron DeSantis, has been a fearless leader for Florida. Governor DeSantis is a dedicated father and loving husband. I get to see his selfless leadership up close, and he inspires me daily. Every single day he takes on liberal elites, well-funded special interests, and the corporate media to protect your rights. In our family, we understand the special opportunity we have to fight for you. It is an honor. Our governor has been so successful exposing the failed policies of the Biden-Harris-Fauci administration, Democrats are now desperate. They will stop at nothing to distract from their failed policies. When they can't win on the issues, all they can do is resort to their tactics of baseless attacks and mudslinging. Just this weekend, Florida Democrats compared Governor DeSantis to Hitler. This comparison is repulsive, offensive, and minimizes Hitler's evil crimes against humanity. But one thing this claim shows is the true feelings of our opponents and the uphill battle we are facing in 2022. While the opposition continues slinging arrows, our governor keeps forging the path ahead for Florida's bright future. He will continue fighting to make sure our children receive a high-quality, in-person education and to keep our communities safe. He will make sure our businesses stay open, our workers have the right to earn a livelihood free from government mandates, and our economy continues roaring. Our opponents' smears reek of desperation, but luckily, there is no stopping our governor - America's Governor - from doing the work because it's the right thing to do. Ron DeSantis is a great father, a great husband, a great man, and a great governor for Florida. He loves his family, loves the people of Florida, and fights like hell to protect our freedom and liberty. And I am damn proud to be alongside him for the journey. Thank you for your support of our mission. God bless you and God bless the great state of Florida. Sincerely, Casey DeSantis
---

name: Tom Nelson
subject: Today we honor Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin
body:
# [#]Today, we honor the life, service, and work of Martin Luther King Jr. – his determination to right injustices and fight for equality should continue to inspire and motivate us all. The ongoing fight for criminal justice reform, the struggle to pass voting rights protections, and the disproportionate impact COVID has had in minority communities all show us how far we have to go to realizing MLK’s vision.

His commitment to his faith inspired my own, and I try each day to lead with my heart as he did. As a retired Lutheran Pastor, my father did the same.

Today, join me in reflecting on what every one of us can do to continue his mission of achieving racial justice in our country – whether that is through volunteering, donating, or another act of service.

We cannot allow divisions based on racism, hate, and violence. As a country, we must learn to celebrate our differences and work to overcome the racial disparities that still plague Wisconsin and the nation.

Martin Luther King Jr. envisioned a world in which his children would be judged not by the color of their skin but by the content of their character. I am pledging to honor Dr. King’s legacy by doing all I can to help achieve his vision of equality for this country, and I hope you will do the same.

– Tom Nelson
VOLUNTEER » [https://go.nelsonforwi.com/a/pledge-to-volunteer]
Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. clean_body: [#]Today, we honor the life, service, and work of Martin Luther King Jr. – his determination to right injustices and fight for equality should continue to inspire and motivate us all. The ongoing fight for criminal justice reform, the struggle to pass voting rights protections, and the disproportionate impact COVID has had in minority communities all show us how far we have to go to realizing MLK’s vision. His commitment to his faith inspired my own, and I try each day to lead with my heart as he did. As a retired Lutheran Pastor, my father did the same. Today, join me in reflecting on what every one of us can do to continue his mission of achieving racial justice in our country – whether that is through volunteering, donating, or another act of service. We cannot allow divisions based on racism, hate, and violence. As a country, we must learn to celebrate our differences and work to overcome the racial disparities that still plague Wisconsin and the nation. Martin Luther King Jr. envisioned a world in which his children would be judged not by the color of their skin but by the content of their character. I am pledging to honor Dr. King’s legacy by doing all I can to help achieve his vision of equality for this country, and I hope you will do the same. – Tom Nelson VOLUNTEER » [] Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. [] [] [] [] [] Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin

subject: Honoring the Life & Legacy
body:
Happy MLK Day

| | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40775570/325883198/1300596418?220117_CLTV_MLK_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBlNDFhYTVhLWJiNzctZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkNDQ0MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=nniWQV_WVQp3IDT14aeEOfcjwxgbHx-wFbcKZwta8go=)
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40775571/325883199/1300596418?220117_CLTV_MLK_IMG1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBlNDFhYTVhLWJiNzctZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkNDQ0MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=nniWQV_WVQp3IDT14aeEOfcjwxgbHx-wFbcKZwta8go=) Dear Peter,

60 years ago, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. inspired generations from all different races to come fight for the greater good of our country. It’s a fight we are still fighting today.

He paved the way for us to use our voices, our resources, and our power to come together and stand up for what we believe in. **Equality for all.**

This is the same message we are trying to promote today – equality for victims of injustice and social disadvantage. It’s something Barrier To Entry PAC is working tirelessly to still change.

But when we look back, we recognize that we are still so far away from uniting our country to understand his message. That’s why young people, progressive leaders, and those who have been marginalized are taking it upon themselves to help restore the message of MLK and continue his service for equality.

None of us will give up.

That is why today is important – we are reminded that service to others is a selfless act and that the arc of justice bends towards freedom.

We celebrate him in that spirit.

Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day,
****Barrier To Entry PAC****

name: Rushern Baker
subject: Dr. King
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2]

Friend,
The future that the late Dr. King dreamed of so dearly is attainable, but we’re not there yet. From the violence during the Jan 6 th attack, to consistent efforts suppressing voting rights and undermining our elections – it is clear that those who wish to divide us have not yet waived a white flag.
In honoring the work and vision of Martin Luther King Jr. we must all remain committed to the fight for equity and inclusion in this country. Make no mistake – we’ve come a long way – but the road ahead must be of action if we are to succeed. May this blessed cause be one that persists on ALL days of the year! Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day!
Thank you,
http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2 [http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2]
Rushern L. Baker, III
https://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2]

clean_body: [] Friend, The future that the late Dr. King dreamed of so dearly is attainable, but we’re not there yet. From the violence during the Jan 6 th attack, to consistent efforts suppressing voting rights and undermining our elections – it is clear that those who wish to divide us have not yet waived a white flag. In honoring the work and vision of Martin Luther King Jr. we must all remain committed to the fight for equity and inclusion in this country. Make no mistake – we’ve come a long way – but the road ahead must be of action if we are to succeed. May this blessed cause be one that persists on ALL days of the year! Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day! Thank you, [] Rushern L. name: Kevin Roberts, Ph.D.
subject: Sign the pledge to stop the unlawful COVID-19 vaccine mandate
body:
Please allow me to introduce myself as the new President of The Heritage Foundation.
[View this message in your browser](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z6ij2oqb0c8fvim3tonks6rrbbjcfsch8j8vm5j8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm0or7c9h238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kkpq2d9ieuqfbdbie4rrcdljkorrbdt469r31d5jekrq9onicbp30s366dh76t5iccqr2ppjmlj60t5km2o72s3n6gor8sj66lj70pjjccprddbnmv970sj2cpob4pjgsipm6pti69j3applm1jr4tr66jgb4pj0s2qm6o70mkoj3d9hmlp2bs)
| | | [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z8k1h5po0bgabvr40h7a0f9hb3r61ffpepc58dno_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm0or7c9h2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow Patriot, Please allow me to introduce myself as the new President of The Heritage Foundation. I’m writing to you today with an urgent need. With the threat of the Biden administration’s reckless and unlawful Covid-19 vaccine mandate, my work to defend your conservative values has been cut out for me from the very moment I arrived at Heritage. **But the good news is, I’m leading a team that fights back whenever your freedom is threatened.** I’m extremely proud to share with you that Heritage has filed a federal lawsuit challenging the Biden administration’s unlawful mandate. Because we recognize this vaccine mandate as exactly what it is: a dangerous level of government overreach attempting to strip your foundational liberty to make your own personal medical decisions.

At Heritage, we are focused on returning power to the people. We are working to preserve and defend your individual liberties guaranteed in U.S. Constitution. We are standing rock solid in defense of the very values that make America great. We are fighting this government power grab and protecting the rights and freedoms of all Americans. But we’re looking for the support of proud American citizens like you. And today, I need to know that you are standing beside us as we fight back. [**That’s why I’m asking you to sign our Pledge to Stop the Unlawful Vaccine Mandate.**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z56ahd9csblkvg7l47d7c50nanhprqvm7i2gkkdg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm0or7c9h2bs) It’s urgent that we know you are with us. I would be honored to see your name added to the pledge.
**You can sign the pledge right now using this link:**[**https://secured.heritage.org/vaccine-mandate-pledge/**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zaai641mmogqe77g2uaja1l0p6kpcvudni2ehb30_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm0or7c9h2bs) And you are more than welcome to share it with everyone you know. If we want to stop this violation of your freedom and privacy, we need as many people as possible to stand up against a mandate the Department of Labor has no authority to impose. **Please don’t delay. Be sure to add your name to the pledge right now.** Sincerely yours, Kevin D. Roberts, Ph.D.
President, The Heritage Foundation
---
| | The Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington D.C. 20002
---

name: Mike Ortega
subject: Remembering Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ortega For Congress
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40775822/325885421/-1122995658?refcode=fl_email_pt_20220117MLKday_MO_full_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YzMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T773j-sm4zIKqTaAPHrluAWGWwbZtWbEdgYyH45jVio=)

Friend,
When I think about my decision to run to represent District 46, I think about all the people who inspired me to act in the face of injustice. There’s no better example than Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. He’s an example to me, our community and the entire world.
Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. inspired millions to take direct action for the civil and human rights of Black people and people of color. He showed us just how important people-powered movements are and how powerful we are when we act together. He led hundreds of thousands of people across the country to the March on Washington to protest segregation and racial injustice.

It’s because of his leadership we made progress and passed legislation like the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and The Voting Rights Act of 1965. His legacy will always live on and inspire the activism work I do, because the fight for equality still continues today.
I’m running to represent District 46 because we all deserve equal access to education, healthcare, and a clean environment. These things should not be determined by your race, wealth or zip code. **The hope for a better America was Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.’s dream and through this campaign, I hope to honor it.**
Friend, we are trying to build a movement across all communities in District 46. I hope you’ll join me in this fight. Sign the pledge below if you’re with me and want to get more involved in this campaign.
[Sign the pledge](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40775823/325885433/-1556387543?refcode=fl_email_pt_20220117MLKday_MO_full_xx_btn&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YzMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T773j-sm4zIKqTaAPHrluAWGWwbZtWbEdgYyH45jVio=&emci=1a179d00-8775-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=6c317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12523946)

clean_body: | | = Friend, When I think about my decision to run to represent District 46, I think about all the people who inspired me to act in the face of injustice. There’s no better example than Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. He’s an example to me, our community and the entire world. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. inspired millions to take direct action for the civil and human rights of Black people and people of color. He showed us just how important people-powered movements are and how powerful we are when we act together. He led hundreds of thousands of people across the country to the March on Washington to protest segregation and racial injustice. It’s because of his leadership we made progress and passed legislation like the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and The Voting Rights Act of 1965. His legacy will always live on and inspire the activism work I do, because the fight for equality still continues today. I’m running to represent District 46 because we all deserve equal access to education, healthcare, and a clean environment. These things should not be determined by your race, wealth or zip code. The hope for a better America was Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.’s dream and through this campaign, I hope to honor it. Friend, we are trying to build a movement across all communities in District 46. I hope you’ll join me in this fight. Sign the pledge below if you’re with me and want to get more involved in this campaign. In Solidarity/En Solidaridad Mike | Our campaign is fueled by everyday people like you chipping in whatever they can. Can you give $25 or whatever you can right now to help us stay on track on our campaign costs? = | = | Paid for by Ortega For Congress Ortega For Congress PO Box 1271 Anaheim, CA 92815 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Tina Kotek
subject: Voting rights honor the legacy of Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek P
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40775893/325885667/69228752?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ceEfG5IiF008aWPPU-xBk1qQnAJDGvVRF7feaRMi3Y0=&emci=295f27de-6675-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=6a317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=15353729)
---
Peter,

Today is a day to honor the life and legacy of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. He dedicated his life to the fight for justice, and one of the ways for us to to celebrate his legacy is through honoring one of his greatest calls to action.

Dr. King fought to expand voting rights. We owe it to future generations to continue to fight for Americans' right to vote and to have their voices heard at the ballot. His efforts helped bring about unparalleled progress for equal rights in our nation – including the historic Voting Rights Act of 1965. But in recent years we have seen an increase in voter suppression efforts by states across the country.

Just last year, 19 states across the country passed bills that would make it harder to vote. It is important now, more than ever, that we continue the work to protect our rights at the ballot.

We need national, comprehensive voting rights legislation to allow every single person that wants to make their voice heard be able to do so.

While other state legislatures work to limit voter participation, Oregon continues to advance in the opposite direction. As Speaker of the House, I supported the **Voter Pre-Registration Act** , which allowed for voter pre-registration at age 16, and the landmark **Motor Voter Act** , which expanded access to our democracy by making sure that every eligible Oregonian gets a ballot. As your Governor, you will be able to count on me to protect the right to vote in Oregon and to do what I can to help other states have the same access to the ballot..

**[With your support, we can accomplish so much. Can you donate $5 to support our commitment to protect voting rights?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40775897/325885677/1650183785?refcode=em22_220117_mlk&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ceEfG5IiF008aWPPU-xBk1qQnAJDGvVRF7feaRMi3Y0=)** [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40775900/325885688/1650183785?refcode=em22_220117_mlk&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ceEfG5IiF008aWPPU-xBk1qQnAJDGvVRF7feaRMi3Y0=) Thank you,
Tina

name: Conservative Direct
subject: Trump Destroys Biden, Says He ‘Humiliated Our Nation'
body:
\-- Donald Trump hasn’t lost his touch. Not only can he still attract a crowd — 15,000 supporters attended his rally in Arizona Saturday night — but he also pulled no punches during his fiery speech. “I love Arizona. We had a tremendous victory in Arizona that was taken away. … We had a rigged election. … [The media] refuse to talk about it. ‘The Big Lie’ is a lot of bull****. That’s what it is.” Last year, a forensic audit of the election in Maricopa County, Ariz. was conducted and found that the number of ballots impacted by irregularities was more than 5 times Biden’s state-certified margin of victory. Despite this, the media falsely claimed the audit proved Joe Biden was the legitimate winner of the state. Trump also said that Joe Biden “has utterly humiliated our nation,” and that our allies no longer respect us and our enemies no longer fear us. In a speech delivered on Thursday, Biden had trouble putting together a coherent sentence. Trump, in contrast, was strong and confident, and projected power. He promised that a “red wave” was coming “and it’s going to sweep across this country.” Trump also blasted Biden’s incompetence in handling COVID-19. “We gave Biden every tool he could want. … He’s incapable.” Biden inherited three COVID vaccines upon taking office, two of which were already approved for emergency use by the FDA. Biden’s failure to include therapeutics and testing as part of his COVID strategy has been widely criticized. Related: Sorry Libs, Americans Think Trump Did Better Fighting COVID Than Biden Trump had strong words for Arizona Governor Ducey, who is reportedly considering a run for U.S. Senate against Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.), as well. “He’s not going to get my endorsement,” Trump said. “Ducey has been a terrible, terrible representative of your state.” |

clean_body: \-- Donald Trump hasn’t lost his touch. Not only can he still attract a crowd — 15,000 supporters attended his rally in Arizona Saturday night — but he also pulled no punches during his fiery speech. “I love Arizona. We had a tremendous victory in Arizona that was taken away. … We had a rigged election. … [The media] refuse to talk about it. ‘The Big Lie’ is a lot of bull**. That’s what it is.” Last year, a forensic audit of the election in Maricopa County, Ariz. was conducted and found that the number of ballots impacted by irregularities was more than 5 times Biden’s state-certified margin of victory. Despite this, the media falsely claimed the audit proved Joe Biden was the legitimate winner of the state. Trump also said that Joe Biden “has utterly humiliated our nation,” and that our allies no longer respect us and our enemies no longer fear us. In a speech delivered on Thursday, Biden had trouble putting together a coherent sentence. Trump, in contrast, was strong and confident, and projected power. He promised that a “red wave” was coming “and it’s going to sweep across this country.” Trump also blasted Biden’s incompetence in handling COVID-19. “We gave Biden every tool he could want. … He’s incapable.” Biden inherited three COVID vaccines upon taking office, two of which were already approved for emergency use by the FDA. Biden’s failure to include therapeutics and testing as part of his COVID strategy has been widely criticized. Related: Sorry Libs, Americans Think Trump Did Better Fighting COVID Than Biden Trump had strong words for Arizona Governor Ducey, who is reportedly considering a run for U.S. Senate against Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.), as well. “He’s not going to get my endorsement,” Trump said. “Ducey has been a terrible, terrible representative of your state.” | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | ---|---|--- | | Dig Deeper... name: Kat Cammack
subject: Remembering Dr. King
body:
Dr. King once said, “as Americans, we unite around one another in the spirit of liberty; focusing on the inward character, and not the outward appearance.”
---
| | | | | | [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001SY573Lq90vpBdbUYe4SD-U91UQOIiTua50diEiS6hJrYiwU5bhdh1wYPgxUM4Ie6YCvL-vUKx-LH6tcvqG0Qq83y8xxYiUJOgTFDL8W2wvnJgRkDSS6xyETimRm2IvRCo1OnNVrJ1wdAiip4cxp4Ok0EUsCv2VvY&c=euN6q7K3bM2gO27HCZRY309mIwl8s7-hJHcwEizl93Yotb6gnZCNFg==&ch=S7PntXQgc1UE3X-yK5N1_cJxWk5w7fEwxwyf0OpNw1I_MEya-YloPA==)
---
| Friend,
Dr. King once said, "as Americans, we unite around one another in the spirit of liberty; focusing on the inward character, and not the outward appearance."
Not just today, but everyday, he inspires us to remember that even in times of deep disagreement, political divides, or tough times for our nation - what unites us are our shared values and principles that continue to make this the greatest nation in the world.
---

name: Matt West
subject: Uplift his words. Carry his legacy.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Matt West for Congress
body:
Matt West For Congress

Friend,

Today, we’re taking a moment to reflect on the life of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., who dedicated his life to working towards a just and equitable future for all Americans.

Dr. King believed that every American had the potential and responsibility to step up and work towards a world without racism, bigotry, or oppression.

In a time where voting rights are under attack, police brutality is rampant, and the coronavirus pandemic ravages the health and economic security of communities — especially communities of color — it couldn’t be more clear that we must do better.

In 2022, we need to recommit ourselves to upholding Dr. King’s legacy and fight back against politicians and extremists trying to undo over 50 years of progress.

The fight for racial and economic justice is a long one, but we must keep fighting. Together, we can build a better and more equitable world.

Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day,

Matt West

— — — —

Facebook → https://www.facebook.com/Matt-West-for-Congress-108181921638796
Twitter → https://twitter.com/mattdwest

name: Marco Rubio
subject: Please forgive me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for Senate
body:
Friend, please excuse how blunt I’m about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen... [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c612934%7c6767%7c6%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c1&wdmdk=SqOSyo0gRtxLRJMgtu3h9w&sysid=1)
Friend, please excuse how blunt I’m about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...
|
---
| | | Friend, please excuse how blunt I’m about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...

I’ve begged.
I’ve pleaded.
I’ve humbly asked.
I’ve even listed out the stakes - I’m the most vulnerable Republican Senator running for re-election, my race will determine control of the Senate.

_**All this - and you STILL haven’t stepped up.**_

Maybe you haven’t stepped up because you think Florida is a shoo-in but that couldn’t be further from the truth. Florida’s last Senate election went to a recount and Republicans BARELY won. My race is virtually tied, Friend, I’m depending on you...

I’ll ask one more time, can I count on you? I really **really** need this year to start off strong.

**[SEND DESPERATELY NEEDED HELP >>](https://secure.winred.com/marco-rubio-for-senate/marco-eoy-extended/?utm_campaign=20220114_WG-GAU.102400_marco-rubio_t1131418-564&utm_medium=emailp&utm_source=WG-GAU.102400&utm_content=20220114_please-forgive-me&utm_tc=GAU-20220117 "SEND DESPERATELY NEEDED

Thank you for your support,

Marco Rubio

If we let up and the Democrats win in Florida, they will KEEP control of the Senate and continue to force their dangerous agenda through Congress.

It is up to us to stop this, Friend. I need each and every patriot who receives this to join me in the fight to take back the Senate.

Thank you for everything you do,

Marco Rubio

name: Maggie Toulouse Oliver
subject: Hate cannot drive out hate.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Maggie, Robert Lara Treasurer
body:
Only love can do that. - Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776008/325885946/1198618133?refcode=EM_FR_220117MLK_H_MTO_30DO_5_G_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzcyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=56QyAhwrepUP_kRGb7P2mAhZGwJn_eV2-wIJGm_Gbp4=&emci=869be6e3-f96e-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=66317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=3583156)
As a young Black man in America during peak racial segregation, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was a brave pioneer, leading the civil rights movement.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776009/325885947/1198618133?refcode=EM_FR_220117MLK_H_MTO_30DO_5_G_N&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzcyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=56QyAhwrepUP_kRGb7P2mAhZGwJn_eV2-wIJGm_Gbp4=&emci=869be6e3-f96e-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=66317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=3583156)
Dr. King was a fearless and courageous champion when it came to fighting for voting rights – I am grateful to be following in his footsteps by fighting for our voting rights right here in New Mexico.
I will cherish his contribution to our democracy. His work solidified the fact that everyone in our country has the right to vote.
As we celebrate and honor his legacy today, a couple of weeks ago, we also remembered the lives lost on the January 6th insurrection in the very same month he was born. The insurrection was not only an attack on our Capitol, but a direct attack on our democracy. We must honor Dr. King’s legacy by continuing his great work in the democratic principle of voting. It is your constitutional right to be heard.
Dr. King will never be forgotten – certainly not by me. I can only hope I accomplish a teaspoon of what he did for democracy in America.
In solidarity,

Maggie Toulouse Oliver
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776010/325885948/1198618133?refcode=EM_FR_220117MLK_H_MTO_30DO_5_G_N&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzcyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=56QyAhwrepUP_kRGb7P2mAhZGwJn_eV2-wIJGm_Gbp4=&emci=869be6e3-f96e-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=66317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=3583156)
_Maggie Toulouse Oliver serves the state of New Mexico as Secretary of State, where she is dedicated to fighting for clean and fair elections devoid of dark money and outside influence.

She's up for re-election in 2022, and we need to start building our grassroots movement to keep her seat safe. **Help Maggie protect our elections system and create a stronger democracy!**_ _**______[FIND OUT MORE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776011/325885949/1198618133?refcode=EM_FR_220117MLK_H_MTO_30DO_5_G_N_BIO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzcyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjMxNzY0Ni1iZjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=56QyAhwrepUP_kRGb7P2mAhZGwJn_eV2-wIJGm_Gbp4=&emci=869be6e3-f96e-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=66317646-bf77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=3583156)**_
---

name: Right Alerts on behalf of Hillsdale College
subject: How should we elect the president?
body:
| _Sponsored by Hillsdale College_

| | [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04d64ec4e430481c977d5c488d2d523d&_e=gurUTP7VXbg68wz-aPK4Q17HbMQB5Gwlbmo5gzaWsJ0NVvfhL8ZS4bphnXCVwvYHCWS_JjQTBZPP57E61DLcf1UmaGpESPInpemlHyGXQs8GzOGuQ0u6BSgRsW6IZHrC8I68Vbs52STsbY0CML7rOc8nLdrztClTtt_09NjJq2W7aDktJtg7IS8abiJUHvr5p92RD7hEKJbXR35lBmHdzZzWfQFEIdfxR-ULzrH9y5f8fJE0PigevdbV4p6nAHHPHbhTnJZyhpyKI0RCXrAscmMasMkS481S9VmLp8Hr1_egSeraxCiSiajAvs3c1oTosgZZ32yOfwongTxGFt3a_zCS64Kg1tp9RJyqr89C3DvlWRfF3hluEs4bSzEYsQ_aVHyGDvkylXTOYr7bnP_ywcw5FFxyQuA5oF9CYe7PjP1-rfgMWrJfMOozV8ATSVFZ) Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation’s future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That’s why Hillsdale College is conducting a **National Survey on Presidential Selection.**

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** by using the link below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04d64ec4e430481c977d5c488d2d523d&_e=gurUTP7VXbg68wz-aPK4Q17HbMQB5Gwlbmo5gzaWsJ0NVvfhL8ZS4bphnXCVwvYHCWS_JjQTBZPP57E61DLcf1UmaGpESPInpemlHyGXQs8GzOGuQ0u6BSgRsW6IZHrC8I68Vbs52STsbY0CML7rOc8nLdrztClTtt_09NjJq2W7aDktJtg7IS8abiJUHvr5p92RD7hEKJbXR35lBmHdzZzWfQFEIdfxR-ULzrH9y5dxSQbi1Be7pCKXAMF2fEBw0cSg5aCEkA_Ynr8JAgiZMkZSlCSryO9hl5y2ZtIskNbtW61rwzPBh3T2E-4KU5Xb_PQoJN9e8qsxluUT4C8tonl8ECpzJsWVc6FRkqGILr9-UYKoZJBUj_yBjAiOS0ksuj0cSo3E7AsCtlkMKchQmVjgdJXgEfA0Kp9SpKAF97taEW5EzMAGF-qD92vZcZUi)**

For most of our nation’s history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

**But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.**

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America’s schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don’t understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching _millions_ of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left’s domination over most of our schools, include:

• **Teaching** millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as “Constitution 101” and “The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;”
• **Reaching** over 5.6 million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis , Hillsdale’s free digest of liberty;
• **Promoting** constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;
• **Sending** millions of pocket-size copies of America’s founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America, as well as to members of our armed forces; and
• **Restoring** excellence to K-12 education through Hillsdale’s Barney Charter School Initiative, which launches and provides guidance to classical K-12 charter schools nationwide.

Your answers to the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

**And this is especially important in 2020, a critical year for America’s future.**

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our **National Survey on Presidential Selection** and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using the secure link I’ve provided below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04d64ec4e430481c977d5c488d2d523d&_e=gurUTP7VXbg68wz-aPK4Q17HbMQB5Gwlbmo5gzaWsJ0NVvfhL8ZS4bphnXCVwvYHCWS_JjQTBZPP57E61DLcf1UmaGpESPInpemlHyGXQs8GzOGuQ0u6BSgRsW6IZHrC8I68Vbs52STsbY0CML7rOc8nLdrztClTtt_09NjJq2W7aDktJtg7IS8abiJUHvr5p92RD7hEKJbXR35lBmHdzZzWfQFEIdfxR-ULzrH9y5egj5Xn4iQijRS0frke0G5pxbIe8RoPYvlmNCPk9vTUZYmfmY0Ph50OJ4SD-0kByoOg8UtiFNb-HXJhYADs14Zw7GRZLHWuLR-g0snkwvrOAtaChnwnhfKNpIe7gfqxw-5XqBOfkH89HVxHMnP6AQSoV-EkggFP7LezrZ6lKO6xkgy7wGvnyD_ncAdcfIZ-5lLDLPtXP8qDwaT4EUOHUMjI)**

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been “pursuing truth and defending liberty.” Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts, while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

---

Hillsdale College. All rights reserved.

---
Don't want to receive any more of our emails? [Click here to unsubscribe](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_s=04d64ec4e430481c977d5c488d2d523d&_t=jZpLk1H3g3Twu8FuP1Q68MObN_nlQ7k-Zz9IKwc_gf-NYGUtde8NDOOX1P2Fou7_rGZ2xmOtb-OnZeuyU1fLlJWPa4NEZZXfGy2NOdtiO6ukKi0mrykVtMu5XEtCA9N-OLkXqNj4GOJppn_5kiJXPCU9yBh8qmYV79zYO73cjTg%3D).

body:

| Friend, please excuse how blunt I'm about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...

I've begged.
I've pleaded.
I've humbly asked.
I've even listed out the stakes - I'm the most vulnerable Republican Senator running for re-election, my race will determine control of the Senate.

_**All this - and you STILL haven 't stepped up.**_

Maybe you haven't stepped up because you think Florida is a shoo-in but that couldn't be further from the truth. Florida's last Senate election went to a recount and Republicans BARELY won. My race is virtually tied, Friend, I'm depending on you...

I'll ask one more time, can I count on you? I really **really** need this year to start off strong.

**[SEND DESPERATELY NEEDED HELP>>](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zfojrjcu7lpbn1k1f2995j7dh5o42rr7k37rf8rg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm4ob2cth2bs)**

Marco Rubio

If we let up and the Democrats win in Florida, they will KEEP control of the Senate and continue to force their dangerous agenda through Congress.

It is up to us to stop this, Friend. I need each and every patriot who receives this to join me in the fight to take back the Senate.

Marco Rubio

name: Josh Riley
subject: My statement on MLK Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Riley for Congress
body:
Martin Luther King Jr. taught us that “whatever affects one directly, affects all indirectly.” In other words: we’re all in this together. And we are.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776830/325890185/-1126184350?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTM4YmRlMC1jMjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EXL4se02oieBXdILchrP8mOyAYtqd3k53jlH2eM-gsg=&emci=1ac919b7-be77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=7e38bde0-c277-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=4588596)
---
| Peter,

Martin Luther King Jr. taught us that “whatever affects one directly, affects all indirectly.” In other words: we’re all in this together. And we are.

Our nation today often feels inextricably divided — our politics too often are dominated by folks on the extremes who are more interested in tearing people apart than bringing them together.

It’s frustrating. It’s scary, even.

And it’s why Dr. King’s words are as important as ever.

We are facing huge challenges: an ongoing public health crisis, an eroding Middle Class, a changing climate, and, yes, persistent inequalities that continue to hurt communities of color. For too long, communities of color have been left out and left behind, and it will take sustained and focused effort to remedy the inequities that have resulted. We need to revitalize the economy and create new jobs and opportunities that lift everyone up and bring everyone along.

And our politics must meet the moment. Instead of pitting people against each other and making scapegoats of minorities, it’s incumbent upon our political leaders to set aside the politics of division while remembering themselves and reminding us all that we have so much more in common than what sets us apart. Because as Dr. King said, whatever affects one directly affects us all indirectly.

Thank you,

Josh Riley
---

name: Doug Gansler
subject: Maryland's sunlight of opportunity
body:
Dr. King’s mission for true equality and justice remains unfulfilled and it’s up to us to take up the torch in his absence. | | [ ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/CMxF4nARlf6wAFa1PSfv0jlFmy0tHsxupmAVrXqus7VR8DS_OkCFqKJAu_9sMqlKHriJ_B_L8O88_5K-i6_srA_l4YD45058r09oshJzRyBKjgTf6wNASFlLf9Rk9Qk7tBOIoYgmQSnvRfLmGSf_A5CLav-cWlAZcdJN1DV96dSqG5scDJxC8xsfH7f6T78wbmpEkTUjpAeeRZRRx4AVVXeI3SOYv9t0_Wz6fJk90bimTaWmcfZqRbjdUhqosRBJR0mN68pbTIIrGdApWT-3qg/3is/pWhn3TQATPq_zktM6SEe3A/h0/-ktAwbRZYq-vKna34TeiM0Ez46KF2KB7uBI-q2nEut8)
---
Hi friends,
---
I wanted to share my reflections on Martin Luther King, Jr. Day. Dr. King and Coretta Scott King have been a huge inspiration throughout my life and time in public service. I attended the 20th Anniversary of Dr. King's _I Have a Dream_ speech in 1983 where civil and economic rights remained the theme. Today is a national holiday because Coretta Scott King fought to make it so. When I was Attorney General I honored her legacy by helping create the first memorial to Coretta Scott King, in Annapolis. Today, it's so important to reflect on our country's history and the progress still ahead of us.**Dr. King 's mission for true equality and justice remains unfulfilled and it's up to us to take up the torch in his absence. **
---
**[My racial justice plan, the"Sunlight of Opportunity Agenda,"](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/CMxF4nARlf6wAFa1PSfv0jlFmy0tHsxupmAVrXqus7VR8DS_OkCFqKJAu_9sMqlKHriJ_B_L8O88_5K-i6_srA_l4YD45058r09oshJzRyBG5B7fZrrR6Rg60GIi8QNFHmOyNONHyqFfVVxAntOfxlTVPt6cDLGhfu48RWGSJIedT2mST6q1E9inMrYUB_A7vtwRdV1fSSiwIBUCouY3QykCDxMHjjJu-eTRw3WeNHV03DyZ8luEbWETP0g1xDgOOKPUEjRCF6UEEQR1ncrbEQ/3is/pWhn3TQATPq_zktM6SEe3A/h1/dY5xt4CNQI1jnXGqy-j21aB2GgNJTFGaejnWWgpr4UU) **outlines my plans to advance equality, justice, and prosperity for people of color across our state. It's my commitment to ensure access to good jobs, good schools, equal justice, affordable housing, personal safety, and more for every person in Maryland. For far too long, Black Marylanders have been left out of the sunlight of opportunity that shines throughout our state. My plan strives to fix that.
---
[ **CHECK OUT THE PLAN HERE ->**
](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/CMxF4nARlf6wAFa1PSfv0jlFmy0tHsxupmAVrXqus7VR8DS_OkCFqKJAu_9sMqlKHriJ_B_L8O88_5K-i6_srA_l4YD45058r09oshJzRyC8AfeDV8twJNO9lppDR-3b93KeFHBRB4gyDIlCAHLqmmhNXG21t4m0fItPHaS1dXYBGeDQmpTCAoaPaJubtmD7rQ-hd5zjRYqULsZK2kE0N1Zs3yy8YvvTpADrLD_zpkamlMqkAlsRjjB3TS06UTEV4Hbmwah7gYIC2MuuSUBd7w/3is/pWhn3TQATPq_zktM6SEe3A/h2/6dYss7bmcgkRTX7Of29VplmAMj4aPtBiM5-97X3v7kQ)
---
My administration will work tirelessly to advance Dr. King's vision for peace and prosperity by prioritizing voting rights, human rights, and combating racism. **That is my promise.** As we honor MLK Day, let's remember his legacy and work together towards achieving his dream. Doug
---
By Authority: Friends of Doug Gansler, Terry Denney, Treasurer; Ana Sol Gutierrez, Chair.

---
Friends of Doug Gansler P.O. Box 31102 Bethesda, MD 20824 United States
---

name: Casey DeSantis
subject: Democrats' Disgraceful Attack
body:
| | | | [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dOpADVtIbqv2KfsWiszzVs_CTMHSTeSR94-Y8O5pcm1gUP0_jEPyeHSiYRB72Xqvp1Egc7_ZdGJkmlqlyLblclQR3RK2zifVmfuwGE8yXxn8wdyktIq_iOZT_NZVt5BeEOKdwAAYl9Lbe4UHqiXgtl1ze5LR1CTgRVsI8uruABP98-jbLgiFdNfe1xQjtFnR&c=VuCp07iKyz9935iunMcjuiEVrypjtjogNzdNYE-UJIaHvCeWwSgFnQ==&ch=BmRuZhu8RDppZkjacjKs-tMujtY8RWwsR1o9vqZjw_ikiEWZHIQv4A==)
---
| Peter,
My husband and our governor, Ron DeSantis, has been a fearless leader for Florida. Governor DeSantis is a dedicated father and loving husband. I get to see his selfless leadership up close, and he inspires me daily. Every single day he takes on liberal elites, well-funded special interests, and the corporate media to protect your rights. In our family, we understand the special opportunity we have to fight for you. It is an honor. Our governor has been so successful exposing the failed policies of the Biden-Harris-Fauci administration, Democrats are now desperate. They will stop at nothing to distract from their failed policies. When they can't win on the issues, all they can do is resort to their tactics of baseless attacks and mudslinging. Just this weekend, Florida Democrats compared Governor DeSantis to Hitler. This comparison is repulsive, offensive, and minimizes Hitler's evil crimes against humanity. But one thing this claim shows is the true feelings of our opponents and the uphill battle we are facing in 2022. While the opposition continues slinging arrows, our governor keeps forging the path ahead for Florida's bright future. He will continue fighting to make sure our children receive a high-quality, in-person education and to keep our communities safe. He will make sure our businesses stay open, our workers have the right to earn a livelihood free from government mandates, and our economy continues roaring. Our opponents' smears reek of desperation, but luckily, there is no stopping our governor - America's Governor - from doing the work because it's the right thing to do. Ron DeSantis is a great father, a great husband, a great man, and a great governor for Florida. He loves his family, loves the people of Florida, and fights like hell to protect our freedom and liberty. And I am damn proud to be alongside him for the journey. clean_body: | | | | == | Peter, My husband and our governor, Ron DeSantis, has been a fearless leader for Florida. Governor DeSantis is a dedicated father and loving husband. I get to see his selfless leadership up close, and he inspires me daily. Every single day he takes on liberal elites, well-funded special interests, and the corporate media to protect your rights. In our family, we understand the special opportunity we have to fight for you. It is an honor. Our governor has been so successful exposing the failed policies of the Biden-Harris-Fauci administration, Democrats are now desperate. They will stop at nothing to distract from their failed policies. When they can't win on the issues, all they can do is resort to their tactics of baseless attacks and mudslinging. Just this weekend, Florida Democrats compared Governor DeSantis to Hitler. This comparison is repulsive, offensive, and minimizes Hitler's evil crimes against humanity. But one thing this claim shows is the true feelings of our opponents and the uphill battle we are facing in 2022. While the opposition continues slinging arrows, our governor keeps forging the path ahead for Florida's bright future. He will continue fighting to make sure our children receive a high-quality, in-person education and to keep our communities safe. He will make sure our businesses stay open, our workers have the right to earn a livelihood free from government mandates, and our economy continues roaring. Our opponents' smears reek of desperation, but luckily, there is no stopping our governor - America's Governor - from doing the work because it's the right thing to do. Ron DeSantis is a great father, a great husband, a great man, and a great governor for Florida. He loves his family, loves the people of Florida, and fights like hell to protect our freedom and liberty. And I am damn proud to be alongside him for the journey. Thank you for your support of our mission. God bless you and God bless the great state of Florida. Sincerely, Casey DeSantis | | == | == == == | Paid by Ron DeSantis, Republican, for Governor | | | | | | | Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Our place in history
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Schiff for Congress
body:
|
#
Derek — I wanted to share some thoughts as the Senate is set to debate voting rights and the filibuster this week. A few days ago, President Biden came to a Senate Caucus meeting to urge Senators to pass voting rights and not let the filibuster get in the way. After spending decades in the Senate himself, he understood the need for a change in the Senate rules if the nation was going to move forward, not backward. But just prior to his speaking, and knowing the purpose of this personal visit by the President of the United States, one of the Democratic Senators took to the Senate floor to pledge her loyalty to the filibuster even if it meant the death of voting rights. That’s right, a member of the President’s own party would not even allow him to make the case without preemptively telling him and the country she thought it a waste of time. It is maddening, and I won’t sugarcoat what it means for the likelihood of success legislatively, in pushing back against the pernicious Republican efforts to disenfranchise people and compromise the integrity of our elections. Instead, I come back to something the Speaker often tells us, “we don’t agonize, we organize.” All around the country, we must ensure that whatever obstacle is put in our way, we will help Americans vote, and make sure every vote is counted. We will protect independent elections officials from harassment — including death threats — so that they remain in their important posts. We will use the courts to challenge any effort to turn away citizens with the right to vote, or to use these anti-democratic new processes to overturn the popular will at the polls. In short, we will save our democracy. As Americans, we have an important democratic legacy to protect. And we will do everything in our power to do so. We will do our duty to our country, and to our children and grandchildren. Thank you for doing your part. I can’t tell you how much it means to me. — Adam | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776859/325890271/1138271422?refcode=AU_ABS_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20220117_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODYxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmFkYThiNC1jMjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RR1XEuI9hldFpuMkqK9pP7jJLU3wxJjlEQf59CF61Cs=)

name: Marco Lopez
subject: raise your voice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Lopez for Governor Authorized by Marco Lopez
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776903/325890704/1742512187?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk401-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTQ2MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGM4ZjkyYy1jNDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CBLd-Ox4Z4pRQmW1oamHMzVg3g3-yjftHN5GpFSxJ1Y=)

Friend: Today, folks from around the nation will come together to celebrate the life of Martin Luther King Jr. He was a giant of the civil rights movement – and thanks to his leadership, our country took huge strides to make sure all people could fully participate in our democracy.

**But right now, our democracy is in grave danger, and Arizona has become the epicenter of the attacks against the right to vote. Far-right extremists are desperate to make it harder for working people to vote – simply because they're afraid of who the people will vote for. **
When we came together to flip the Senate _and_ White House blue, Arizona Republicans started lashing out against our electoral process. They've spent the past year unleashing some of the country's most outrageous voter suppression tactics to disenfranchise minority voters – and their worst actions are being amplified by national extremists, too. **It's an anti-democratic disgrace – and we need to use our voices NOW, before they're drowned out and silenced.**
Luckily, we have a clear way to stop these attacks nationwide. **We can call on Congress to do what it takes to pass voting rights legislation that safeguards all of our rights. But you have to be part of the push, friend.**
**[The extremist Republicans fighting to keep Arizona red are terrified of your voice – that's why they're so desperate to suppress it. So I'm asking you to _raise_ it instead, right now, in support of voting rights. But with time running out before our petition closes at 11:59 p.m. tonight, we're still missing your name. So please, add your name NOW to demand Congress pass voting rights and defend our democracy:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40776904/325890705/312301615?email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Flopez-filibuster-freedom-act&code=jtk401-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTQ2MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGM4ZjkyYy1jNDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CBLd-Ox4Z4pRQmW1oamHMzVg3g3-yjftHN5GpFSxJ1Y=&emci=8b900052-bb75-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=f0c8f92c-c477-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=6085109)**

name: First Lady Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis,
_Sponsored message_
[****](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z7rpu2t3hickhaot6k4p3dvuvt5q834j65ulj8mg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zpkgrmo9ttmp8pevrlrqk67n85clajmvug4j7oh8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z2lhp36l4g9op23mjkmvlqqcn1omoou4f1gb7t88_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-hf")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is hold those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**
[**Please rush in a contribution to help us continue to hold the line and keep Florida free. Chip in $20.22, $30, or $50 now!**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z9nloa8ucru0nj428i0shrn8i0jbpde8403jnbgo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs)
Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

[](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zuvfd276tnv0vveko6tmtssnrl3lllilic2s4c30_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs "CHIP IN NOW") [CHIP IN NOW](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zdgimcsnifo5dm2m3kdrm71l645rpvptohouq2k0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm2oj2c9h2bs)
---

Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. Platt Street, Suite 345, Tampa, FL 33606

name: Gwen Moore
subject: Help me keep his message alive
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
body:
I have a dream! My Sisters and Brothers, I still remember how moved I was watching Dr. King deliver his “I have a dream” speech as a young girl. While we take today to remember the legacy and lessons of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., today also marks a pivotal day of action in support of voting rights. Across our nation, GOP-controlled state legislatures are actively putting restrictions on our Constitutional right to vote. Join me right now in repeating Dr. King’s famous words, “a right delayed is a right denied!” Say it with me again! “A right delayed is a right denied!” Thousands are marching today in support of upholding our sacred right to vote. We will not be denied our right to make our voices heard at the ballot box! [Join me in urging the Senate to pass the John Lewis Voting Rights Act and For the People Act tomorrow!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777097/325891975/-1789690988?contactdata=q1E44qCnhZZSXwIAQkhGvKlaIx%2BBmWIvLhp5X1%2FNhnU4rsUvIRwKLkKOVZmLvgiUcz%2B8Rcp0mpEvc1x6fKy1XCYqkYR8kuI2spC3FvHe8zW5pfMlj%2FGHMNk3o%2FrrEy6JY3r%2BrM848Fo14sC%2Fmcp7dqM1hRYw3RapDjJ8cuU9OAo%3D&quick=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzczOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMmMyNGI4Mi1jNzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Nns6EMuHL-j6TB-1WRbhpUutgw9EL9OAacW8ETXccJ0=&emci=d080fe83-b977-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=b2c24b82-c777-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1486204) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777098/325891976/-1789690988?contactdata=q1E44qCnhZZSXwIAQkhGvKlaIx%2BBmWIvLhp5X1%2FNhnU4rsUvIRwKLkKOVZmLvgiUcz%2B8Rcp0mpEvc1x6fKy1XCYqkYR8kuI2spC3FvHe8zW5pfMlj%2FGHMNk3o%2FrrEy6JY3r%2BrM848Fo14sC%2Fmcp7dqM1hRYw3RapDjJ8cuU9OAo%3D&quick=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzczOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMmMyNGI4Mi1jNzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Nns6EMuHL-j6TB-1WRbhpUutgw9EL9OAacW8ETXccJ0=&emci=d080fe83-b977-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=b2c24b82-c777-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=1486204) We must keep Dr. King’s message alive. I know we will and we’ll do it together! Voting Rights are Human Rights. Together, #WeRepresent

Gwen Moore

Proud Co-Lead of the Violence Against Women Act
100% Pro-Choice

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc
body:

Hello,
After he was stricken with COVID-19, Chuck Grassley’s 27-year streak of 8,927 consecutive votes came to an end. But when you look at what many of those 8,927 votes were... it kinda makes you wish he would have been absent more often.
For over 40 years in the Senate, Chuck Grassley has voted against expanding access to health care, against combatting the world’s climate crisis, against getting money out of politics, and against even the most common sense firearm responsibility issues supported by our police and members of his own party.
Grassley has taken millions from corporate PACs and special interests, including Wall Street, Big Pharma and insurance companies, and the NRA. It should be no surprise that he remains deaf to Iowa but a good listener of billionaires and their lobbyists.
**I’m running for Senate against Chuck Grassley because after a lifetime in elected office, it’s time Iowans had someone actually representing them. You won’t find me taking money from corporate PACs.**
Control of the Senate is on the line here in Iowa, so you know Mitch McConnell and his Super PACs are going to come after me with millions of dollars of false TV ads. If we’re going to take on Chuck Grassley, turn Iowa blue, and expand our Democratic majority in the Senate, I need grassroots donors like you to chip in now and become a Founding Donor to my campaign. Give me the tools so I can fight back.
[**Please, can you chip in $5, $25, or whatever you can afford now to become a Founding Donor and help me defeat Chuck Grassley, turn Iowa blue, and defend our Democratic majority?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777227/325892903/-42398318?refcode=em20220117mid-nd&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C35%2C50%2C100%2C250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjZmM2FhYi1jNzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vzcjj6W9RIvXLkA4pbkzSrVwN1vmUBsg11Ddt38p7xI=)

clean_body: Hello, After he was stricken with COVID-19, Chuck Grassley’s 27-year streak of 8,927 consecutive votes came to an end. But when you look at what many of those 8,927 votes were... it kinda makes you wish he would have been absent more often. For over 40 years in the Senate, Chuck Grassley has voted against expanding access to health care, against combatting the world’s climate crisis, against getting money out of politics, and against even the most common sense firearm responsibility issues supported by our police and members of his own party. Grassley has taken millions from corporate PACs and special interests, including Wall Street, Big Pharma and insurance companies, and the NRA. It should be no surprise that he remains deaf to Iowa but a good listener of billionaires and their lobbyists. I’m running for Senate against Chuck Grassley because after a lifetime in elected office, it’s time Iowans had someone actually representing them. You won’t find me taking money from corporate PACs. Control of the Senate is on the line here in Iowa, so you know Mitch McConnell and his Super PACs are going to come after me with millions of dollars of false TV ads. If we’re going to take on Chuck Grassley, turn Iowa blue, and expand our Democratic majority in the Senate, I need grassroots donors like you to chip in now and become a Founding Donor to my campaign. name: Michael Bennet
subject: re: Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Colorado
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777315/325893106/721416609?refcode=header&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,500,1000&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzZmM2FhYi1jNzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=psAyra6fhOp3kAB8bnW4QmoriFFiGDA8rSbbs-tN-aA=)
In June of 1965, the _Voting Rights Act_ , which had been recently passed in the Senate, sat in the House awaiting approval. The Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King wrote an editorial praising the hard-fought near-victory, but clarified that passing such sweeping voting rights legislation was a mere step. _“We cannot rest. Laurels have not yet been earned.” - Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr._ You see, folks, passing the _Voting Rights Act_ would not automatically register and mobilize disenfranchised Black voters across the country. Dr. King prescribed mass voter registration drives to empower voters. There was still work to be done. Now, nearly 57 years later, the _Voting Rights Act_ has been gutted and we have not earned rest. I’m working tirelessly with my Senate colleagues to pass the _Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act_ in order to protect every American’s right to vote. It’s a critical step in realizing Dr. King’s vision of equality, freedom, and economic justice for all Americans, and I will continue to fight for this. We honor Dr. King’s legacy and recommit to his fight, which remains our fight today: to make sure every American can vote safely and easily. It is essential to protecting and strengthening our democracy. This is the work of our lifetimes. Thank you, Michael |
---

Denver, CO 80201
United States

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: We can't sit quietly in the presence of injustice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Peter,

This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, I hope you take a moment to remember Dr. King. Even better, I hope you are taking part in the day of service to honor Dr. King's legacy.

Dr. King famously said, "True peace is not merely the absence of tension; it is the presence of justice."

Justice is never secured by sitting on the sidelines. Dr. King's Dream is still alive, but the work is unfinished. The task for all of us is to keep working toward a more perfect union. A nation where every American can vote without facing discriminatory barriers. A nation where we embrace the principle of equal pay for equal work. A nation where everyone has affordable health care. A nation where every person can live with dignity and where every person's civil rights are protected and guarded fiercely.

Dr. King said, "We may have all come on different ships, but we're in the same boat now."

No matter who you vote for, where you live, the color of your skin, or your financial wherewithal, we are in this together, Peter. Those of us who are in a position to speak up against injustice need to. Those of us who have the power, the time, and the privilege to fight for the rights of our fellow Americans **need to.**

Today, we remember a leader who was larger than life -- whose words continue to be a guiding light in the darkest of times. Martin Luther King Jr. Day is a reminder of what's possible when we refuse to sit quietly in the presence of injustice. I will keep pushing to form that more perfect union, and I am grateful to have you by my side.

It's an honor to serve you,

Derek

name: Quaye Quartey
subject: Reflecting on his legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Quartey for Congress****
body:
“Life’s most persistent and urgent question is, ‘What are you doing for others?” – Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777579/325894401/-1689843803?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjZlYjg1YS1jODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4l1e-jhqNFqwAO8i4FajT1BPsaOGtZZzldOoHBRTPQc=&emci=bcf7e84a-2776-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=af6eb85a-c877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=14505483)

_“Life’s most persistent and urgent question is, ‘What are you doing for others?”
– Dr. Martin Luther King Jr._ Team –

I wanted to take a moment to reflect on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy, as we celebrate his life and unwavering commitment to equality. Dr. King dedicated his life to the fight for civil rights in our nation, and shaped the vision for a more inclusive America. His powerful writings and charismatic leadership unified a deeply divided country.

As the son of an immigrant father from Ghana and a mother raised in the Jim Crow South, I am no stranger to adversity. On a day like this, I am reminded of how far we have come, and how much work we still have ahead of us. I remain committed to stepping up anywhere and everywhere with the compassion necessary to welcome all.

**Today, let us honor Dr. King’s legacy in hopes that we are doing what we can every day to be better than we were yesterday.**

Sincerely,

Quaye Quartey | | We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [_**click here**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777580/325894402/2122512103?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjZlYjg1YS1jODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4l1e-jhqNFqwAO8i4FajT1BPsaOGtZZzldOoHBRTPQc=&emci=bcf7e84a-2776-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=af6eb85a-c877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=14505483).

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Remembering MLK
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Today is Martin Luther King, Jr. Day — a day we set aside to remember the important activism and work that he dedicated his life to.

As Dr. King once said “The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice.”

Even 54 years after his death, we have much to learn from his message and much to strive for as a nation. In these times, it can seem like hatred spreads more quickly than love, but when we all stand together, there is nothing we cannot conquer.

Today, as we remember the life and legacy of one of our nation's greatest civil rights leaders, we must remember Dr. King’s message of hope in a time of darkness. We must make a collective effort to live our lives guided by his activism and message. Our progress forward is not finished, and together, we can create a nation Dr. King dreamed of.

Thank you for doing your part.

Mike Thompson
Authorized & Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/61e4c80d-7375-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442/37758578-c977-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDQ0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzc1ODU3OC1jOTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xe_SrG5rL9zdU83DU4RizmC1gvqXRSPB2nlZxeINJDU=&id=103862631 Mike Thompson for Congress
PO Box 10541
Napa, CA 94581
United States

name: Pro Life Update
subject: Be one of the first
body:
Today, I’m writing to let you know that we’ve produced a limited number of DVD box sets of our “Constitution 101” course
| | | This sponsored message from Pro Life Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zen9aua9tgj52gmavhaehhhqf2n9kj28spg06dm8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6p74clh2bs) Dear Fellow American, [](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zk0j2t2jufsquhl9ohed4gq5p48k8c8vg2ms33d0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6p74clh2bs) I hope you’re having a great day so far. Today, I’m writing to let you know that we’ve produced a limited number of DVD box sets of our “Constitution 101” course, and I’d like you to have a copy. The course features Hillsdale College’s world-renowned faculty. It teaches the original design and purpose of the Constitution, the challenges it faced during the Civil War, and how it has been undermined for over a century by progressivism and liberalism. It concludes by setting forth how limited government under the Constitution might be revived. “Constitution 101” is modeled after the course that all Hillsdale students are required to take, and this DVD box set is your opportunity to experience it for yourself. I hope you take advantage of this offer, and I have included a secure link for you here: [**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/constitution-101-dvd/**](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zk6lc1t4gd3blgl5rmjq285p8khbjkq44bf3ia3g_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6p74clh2bs) As you know, Hillsdale takes no government funding, even indirectly in the form of student grants or loans. So, your support is essential to expanding our national educational outreach efforts, like free online courses, designed to restore and defend liberty. As I mentioned above, supplies of the new “Constitution 101” DVD box set are limited, so I hope you’ll reserve your copy today. Sincerely, Bill Gray Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth--Defending Liberty since 1844
---
---

|

If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for Pro Life Update, [please use this preferences page](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/prefs/9z2z0higq03jjopolm8mi3l4bo1gg6fohrpnleugjjo).
625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

[](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zemrlg1bk76r301bdqght3g9ci8fp0pv11lvu4mo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6p74clh238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

As I mentioned above, supplies of the new “Constitution 101” DVD box set are limited, so I hope you’ll reserve your copy today. Sincerely, Bill Gray Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth--Defending Liberty since 1844 | If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for Pro Life Update, . 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: Eric Gash
subject: Honoring The Legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Gash for Congress
body:
With your support, our movement continues to grow locally, and our effort to deliver compassionate, unifying leadership to North Carolina's 11th district is picking up momentum. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777827/325896032/-851526706?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjk1ZDE3Mi1jYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5eEQua-Of4AGP-KwmP10AhsAVeCA0nLQw8wNZH_s4-0=)
Friend,

This year, Martin Luther King Jr. Day has taken on a new meaning for so many of us.

As a pastor, a man of faith, and now as a candidate for Congress, the lessons and legacy Dr. King left behind resonate more poignantly than ever.

One of King’s teachings was imploring us to _"meet the forces of hate with the power of love."_ Lord knows we’ve seen the forces of hate grow more intense in recent years. We’ve seen extreme division in our communities. Last year, we endured unrelenting attacks on the same Voting Rights Act that Dr. King fought to enact in 1965.

Sadly, after 55 years, we are still fighting the same battles Dr. King did. But this is not a day to lament. **Today, let us honor Dr. King’s legacy by choosing love.** Let’s commit to protecting and strengthening our democracy by remembering the promise he fought for -- that all Americans have an equal say in the future of this country.

On this day of reflection, let's choose love, justice, and compassion. And let's recommit to building an America that represents all of us.

In service,

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777828/325896033/-525608369?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjk1ZDE3Mi1jYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5eEQua-Of4AGP-KwmP10AhsAVeCA0nLQw8wNZH_s4-0=&emci=4ebca991-c777-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=df95d172-ca77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=18459276)

name: Dr. Kermit Jones
subject: The fight for MLK’s dream continues
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kermit Jones for Congress
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777832/325896065/218260425?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220117mlk_DKJ_full_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjAxNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwY2I0YzQ5Ni1jYTc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I4NAfeHHZ0o4sg3ah_VYbG9FoJnV0t5t0zLDnh7cxf4=)

Friend, On this day that we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., I've been reflecting on how much work we have left to do to achieve his dream. Last week, Congress failed to pass the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act. Frankly, I’m beyond disappointed — I’m angry. _The voting restrictions being implemented across this country are attacks on our democracy and our constitutional right to vote._ As we grapple with the consequences of Congress’ failure to secure voting rights, many of us are feeling hopeless. But I am reminded of one of Dr. King’s sayings: **“We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope.”** We _will_ strengthen voting rights. We _will_ get dark money out of our elections. And we _will_ put a stop to racial gerrymandering. It is clearly not going to be on the timeline we had wanted or needed. But it will happen — because we don’t give up. Sincerely, Dr. Kermit | | Kermit Jones is a Democrat, doctor, and Navy veteran running for Congress in California's 3rd District.
