name: First Lady Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis,
| _Sponsored message_ |
---|---|---
[****](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zct6lapt35267q85mpvmv8muht5aqlhdlqodfjtg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z8a4el18ijlbmvaeej59uscifiajkq1jt9fnk760_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zrp2a0imldilecfj18f7abgp1864j2quhbdnmk18_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-rvs-pa?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pa_icymin&utm_tc=ICYMI-20220117")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**
[**Please rush in a contribution to help us continue to hold the line and keep Florida free. Chip in $20.22, $30, or $50 now!**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zr6u6nlp62ev1m8varngisp22a55scttu7882ts0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs)
Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zhghrufn5ngmng6368u1hrm9hpk95edqks0k5teg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs "CHIP IN NOW") [CHIP IN NOW](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z4r86db92sf87fqdsracdcimb74jebrn63l8evoo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm6o34c5h2bs)
---

Paid by Ron DeSantis, Republican, for Governor

Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. Platt Street, Suite 345, Tampa, FL 33606

If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for ICYMI News, [please use this preferences page](http://e.icyminews.com/prefs/9z2zcga2ml0lg37cfkcmls1l3f3b7rg4nk1q4mc0kdg).
625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314
[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zr82ukk0n7ff9pvi96n007m13l810rr51qqmmb0g_rp2ash2s8j2ccdkmorb4dthe8gq48bi4bh28or1c9gm2or0chgm49fg)

name: Ron Johnson
subject: DECISION MADE ✅
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ron Johnson For Senate, Inc
body:
Since I have been in office, I have become the #1 target of the Left, the liberal biased media, and Big Tech giants like Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube.
|
---
| | | Peter,
Since I have been in office, I have become the #1 target of the Left, the liberal biased media, and Big Tech giants like Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube.

I have been smeared again and again by media companies like _The New York Times_ , _Washington Post_ , and the _Milwaukee Journal Sentinel_ where they distort my comments, twist them through a liberal prism, and omit most of what I actually say.

I have been silenced, censored, and suspended by the social media oligarchs because I tell the truth, and oftentimes, it is the truth the liberal mainstream media does not want to hear. It counters their narrative, and they refuse to allow it. So they silence free speech.

Yet, despite all of this unconstitutional collusion between these far-Left parties working for political gain at the expense of the American people, I will not give up. They will not deter me. And that is why today, I am officially announcing my re-election campaign for the Wisconsin Senate seat.

I am running for a third term for the simple reason that I believe I am the best person for the job. I believe that I am the best candidate to address the national debt, work to lower inflation, secure the border, and fight for America First policies. I believe I am the best candidate to put an end to the Biden administration's constant record of misinformation and their continued lack of transparency with Congress, the press, and you, Peter. I believe I am the best candidate for Wisconsin because I tell the truth and speak for the minority, even when the majority is doing everything they can to silence me.

Now that I have announced, the Left will seize every opportunity to destroy me politically. They will continue to write manipulative hit pieces and orchestrate smear campaigns across all mediums. They will attack and distort my record of success, my record of searching for the truth, and my record of fighting for the people of Wisconsin in any way they can.

And that's why I need your help.

**[Any help you can provide will make an enormous difference and will be greatly appreciated. $5, $10, $25, or even $35 will help me respond to these hit pieces and smear campaigns. It will take all of us working together to defeat the Left and their dishonest tactics.

Can I count on you to chip in? We need to start working right now.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59273955:JiLFm-ENf:m:1:3253819041:A474387785726D7C520571EBB1109874:r "Any help you can
provide will make an enormous difference and will be greatly appreciated. $5, $10, $25, or even $35 will help me respond to these hit pieces and smear
campaigns. It will take all of us working together to defeat the Left and their dishonest tactics.Can I count on you to chip in? We need to start
working right now.")**

Ron Johnson
---
| | | [FIGHT FOR FREEDOM](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59273955:JiLFm-ENf:m:1:3253819041:A474387785726D7C520571EBB1109874:r "Fight for Freedom")
---

P.S. As the Left's number one target, they want to silence my voice. Liberal media conglomerates, talk shows, and newspapers will be pushing their viewers and readers to support liberal candidates that will work to tear down our institutions and impose their agenda by spreading their false and manipulative message. I really need your help.

---
| |
* * *
---
| | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59273956:JiLFm-ENf:m:1:3253819041:A474387785726D7C520571EBB1109874:r "RonJohnsonFooter")
---
| _A personal note from Ron Johnson:_

Thank you for standing strong with me, Peter. While the media tries to smear me, I am not afraid of standing up for the truth. I appreciate you joining me in the battle for Wisconsin against the Radical Democrats.

Recently, CNN ranked my seat #3 most likely to flip. Reports are also saying that even the THREAT of losing my seat could derail plans to take back the Senate. The thought of losing WI is terrifying -- it's just one more step towards destroying Trump's Legacy.

So I need you to step up and stand strong with me. **[Please click here to donate now](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59273956:JiLFm-ENf:m:1:3253819041:A474387785726D7C520571EBB1109874:r "Please click here to donate now")**.

Thank you for being a patriot,

Ron Johnson

name: Bridie Farrell
subject: What MLK Day means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bridie Farrell for US
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777959/325896830/-870670775?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220117mlkday_bf_full_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTU5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDNlZGFmZC1jYzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N3Bp_b1gSIO4tfAGfmp0UVA60eYjFXX6WAOHcmk6_Vw=)
---
Friend, Martin Luther King Jr. Day is a day on which we are reminded of the importance of our political process in America because every advancement Dr. King achieved came about through peaceful political advocacy and action. Listen to the recordings of President Lyndon Baines Johnson and Dr. King speaking with each other about civil rights and voting rights, and you will hear the majesty of our Democracy at work. **_What is hard on a day like today, however, is reading a tweet from Elise Stefanik honoring Dr. King just a few months after she opposed the John Lewis Voting Rights Act and days after she shot down the most recent voting rights act in Congress._** Elise is obviously free to do what she wants, but as citizens we must remind ourselves, as Dr. King did, that we have the power to fight malignancy and hypocrisy in all of its forms. There is a truth baseline that the arc of history will tell and I believe that a new patriotism can and must emerge in America where politicians will be honest with citizens and where we can again get anything done in this country. Dr. King believed in the better nature of our Democracy. I believe in that too and I believe there is something important about honesty and consistency. Just because it is easy to get away with dishonesty and mistruth today doesn’t mean it is right or that politicians should continue to lie. Dr. King urged us to be better than petty, to rise above and to seek a higher calling. That is what I pledge to you I will do in Congress and how I will honor the legacy of MLK. By being straight with you. By sticking to a new patriotism of service to country and by trying to help everyone in the North Country that I love. **_As Dr. King said, “the time is always right to do what is right.”_** Thanks, Bridie Farrell | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40777960/325896831/-870670775?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220117mlkday_bf_full_xx_btn&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTU5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDNlZGFmZC1jYzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N3Bp_b1gSIO4tfAGfmp0UVA60eYjFXX6WAOHcmk6_Vw=)
---

name: Jill Vogel
subject: Legislative Update 1-17-2022
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate 332 West Lee Hwy PMB 312 Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
|
Legislative Update January 17, 2022
Our Senate office had weeks of a challenging calendar that culminated last week with the beginning of the 2022 legislative session. We worked through a redistricting process which concluded with a failed Redistricting Commission and Supreme Court drawn maps creating new districts for every legislator in the Commonwealth. I chaired a Youngkin Transition landing team to manage work related to the Secretary of Administration and assisted with a portion of the inaugural committee's work. We did pre-session briefings with constituents, local governments, and community groups to prepare legislation and the Senate Finance Committee continued to meet to prepare for the upcoming budget year.
The Senate convened Wednesday, January 12, 2022\. Governor Ralph Northam gave his last State of the Commonwealth Address that night in advance of Inauguration of the 74th Governor of Virginia Saturday the 15th. Once in four years, the House and Senate hold a joint Saturday session of the General Assembly and proceed to the Capitol Portico to participate in the transition of leadership. Governor Glenn Youngin, Lieutenant Governor Winsome Sears, and Attorney General Jason Miyares took the oath of office followed by an inspiring ceremony and parade of proud participants from schools and communities around Virginia. Representatives of our Native American tribes offered a blessing for the Governor and the Capitol grounds.
The inaugural is historic in the Commonwealth, representing the swearing in of the first Hispanic statewide officeholder, the first Black woman to hold statewide office, and the first female to hold the office of Lieutenant Governor. After the inauguration, Governor Youngkin promptly signed 9 official executive orders and 2 executive directives. Those are:
Executive Order 1: Ends the use of "inherently divisive concepts, including Critical Race Theory." It requires removal of education policies, practices or materials "that promote or endorse divisive or inherently racist concepts."
Executive Order 2: Rescinds the statewide mask mandate for school students and states that parents may exempt their children from local school systems' mask mandates.
Executive Order 3: Replaces members of the Virginia Parole Board and directs the Secretary of Public Safety to review the Parole Board's duties and procedures.
Executive Order 4: Authorizes the Attorney General to investigate Loudoun County's public schools, stating that the School Board and administrators "withheld key details and knowingly lied to parents" when a teenage student committed sexual assaults at two schools.
Executive Order 5: Directs Chief Transformation Officer to initiate reviews of the Department of Motor Vehicles and the Virginia Employment Commission.
Executive Order 6: Directs the Safety and Health Codes Board to determine whether there is a continued need for COVID-19 standards to be imposed on employers.
Executive Order 7: Establishes a commission to prevent human trafficking and victim support.
Executive Order 8: Establishes a commission to combat antisemitism.
Executive Order 9: Initiates withdrawal from the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative.
Executive Directive 1: Directs Executive Branch entities to cut regulations by 25%.
Executive Directive 2: Rescinds the COVID-19 vaccine mandate for all state employees.
We welcome your feedback on any matters and also welcome visitors to the Capitol. Please contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached during the General Assembly session at 804-698-7527, P.O. Box 397, Richmond, VA 23218 or email at [district27@senate.virginia.gov](mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov).

---
| Sincerely,

---|---
|

Senator Jill Vogel

name: Georgette =?iso-8859-1?b?R/NtZXo=?=
subject: MLK's legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Georgette Gómez for Assembly 2022, #1443281
body:
https://georgettegomez.org/ [https://georgettegomez.org/]The night before Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was assassinated, he spoke to a group of striking Memphis sanitation workers. He urged them to keep up the strike, to keep fighting for a better tomorrow.
Martin Luther King Jr. did not live to see the end of that strike. But his work and his commitment to the ideals of justice and equality are still alive today.
Right now, sanitation workers in Chula Vista are striking for better pay and working conditions.
I stand with them, just as I stand with all frontline workers who want livable wages and dignity in their work.
Today reminded us that the fight for racial equality, worker's rights, and environmental justice is not a new one. But it is one ALWAYS worth waging. In the spirit of Dr. King's struggle, l et's make a more just California together.
In solidarity,
-- Georgette

**Houston, TX** - On Monday, Wesley Hunt, Army combat veteran and Houston native, running to represent Texas' 38th Congressional district, joined Fox and Friends to remember Dr. King's legacy and the impact he had on our country. name: Dr. Yadira Caraveo
subject: “Our feet are tired, but our souls are rested”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
The words and work of Martin Luther King Jr. are more crucial than ever. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778211/325899726/1561815853?refcode=ea.be2ea709-d077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ms=ea.be2ea709-d077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZTJlYTcwOS1kMDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wGaIMOox0Ls5rDgYvg-0gGkhq4EFtOwr72TEANgJ0b0=&emci=9a5c5591-a674-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=be2ea709-d077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12666102)
---
| | Peter,

**I’m tired.** It’s been a little over a year since the January 6th insurrection, and the radical wing of the GOP has MORE influence, not less. While my opponent Lori Saine helped bolster this movement, I was working tirelessly to expand voting rights through my multilingual ballot access bill.

**The words, actions, and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. are more necessary and more nourishing than ever this year.**

At the end of a three-day march from Selma to Montgomery, **Dr. King said, “Our feet are tired, but our souls are rested.”** I remember those words today when I look back at all the progress we have achieved, together.

Together, we defeated Trump and drove racism, hatred, and lies from the highest office in the land. Together, we elected a Congress that is passing laws that will truly help all Americans, like the Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill.

**But still, there is so much work to be done.** State by state, GOP-controlled legislatures are stripping our fellow citizens of access to the ballot box. Dr. King’s 1965 campaign in Alabama was centered around guaranteeing Black Americans the right to vote. It is exhausting that, over 60 years later, we are still fighting this same battle.

But as Dr. King said when he reached Selma after three days of walking, “The battle is in our hands... The road ahead is not altogether a smooth one. There are no broad highways that lead us easily and inevitably to quick solutions. But we must keep going... **We are not about to turn around. We are on the move now.”**

This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, let us recommit ourselves to moving forward. To marching towards racial justice and equity for every American.

Sí, se puede,

Dr. Yadira Caraveo
---
| PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
---

clean_body: The words and work of Martin Luther King Jr. are more crucial than ever. | | Peter, I’m tired. It’s been a little over a year since the January 6th insurrection, and the radical wing of the GOP has MORE influence, not less. While my opponent Lori Saine helped bolster this movement, I was working tirelessly to expand voting rights through my multilingual ballot access bill. The words, actions, and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. are more necessary and more nourishing than ever this year. At the end of a three-day march from Selma to Montgomery, Dr. King said, “Our feet are tired, but our souls are rested.” I remember those words today when I look back at all the progress we have achieved, together. Together, we defeated Trump and drove racism, hatred, and lies from the highest office in the land. Together, we elected a Congress that is passing laws that will truly help all Americans, like the Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill. But still, there is so much work to be done. State by state, GOP-controlled legislatures are stripping our fellow citizens of access to the ballot box. Dr. King’s 1965 campaign in Alabama was centered around guaranteeing Black Americans the right to vote. It is exhausting that, over 60 years later, we are still fighting this same battle. But as Dr. King said when he reached Selma after three days of walking, “The battle is in our hands... The road ahead is not altogether a smooth one. There are no broad highways that lead us easily and inevitably to quick solutions. But we must keep going... We are not about to turn around. We are on the move now.” This Martin Luther King Jr. Day, let us recommit ourselves to moving forward. To marching towards racial justice and equity for every American. Sí, se puede, Dr. Yadira Caraveo | PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS PO Box 953, Eastlake, CO 80614 If you would like to receive fewer emails, please .** If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please.**

You are invited to a Brew-Pub hosted Meet & Greet Petition Party in Lakewood this Thursday!
Erik will speak for a bit, then spend time meeting you, answering questions and talking about the upcoming campaign for CD7.
If you are a resident of CD7 (we can help find out if you're unsure) please plan to sign the petition to get Erik onto the primary ballot.

name: Kisten Engel
subject: Honoring Dr. King's legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Engel for Arizona
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778224/325899741/-2037180865?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MjcwOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTJlYTcwOS1kMDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ea3vKceiaP-uO5SWHFQq4VNPJTcHRZd8CiZaJvQX9zA=&emci=6290ab5a-b677-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=c12ea709-d077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=12898431)
---
Friend,
Dr. Martin Luther King fought for voting rights, and challenged us to fully live up to the democratic values our country was founded upon.
While the right to vote fairly and freely should not be a partisan issue, we are currently witnessing the most aggressive wave of voter restrictions in decades.
Access to the ballot box has been carefully and strategically stripped away for political gain, not only dishonoring the memory and legacy of Dr. King, but violating the constitutional rights of thousands.
[**That is why today, on Martin Luther King Day, I invite you to join me in calling on the Senate to pass the _Freedom To Vote Act_ >>**](http://actionnetwork.org/petitions/pass-the-freedom-to-vote-act-now?source=ECU_2022_FTVA_KE&referrer=group-kirsten-engel-for-arizona&redirect=https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel-for-arizona-1?refcode=X_11222_VotingRightsJA&amount=25)
[](https://actionnetwork.org/petitions/pass-the-freedom-to-vote-act-now?source=ECU_2022_FTVA_KE&referrer=group-kirsten-engel-for-arizona&redirect=https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel-for-arizona-1?refcode=X_11222_VotingRightsJA&amount=25)
---
Passing the Freedom to Vote Act is an essential first step to protecting our democracy, and would help bring an end to dark money, block partisan gerrymandering, and protect our elections from sabotage.
It is now up to us to honor Dr. King's legacy and protect the fundamental right to vote in any capacity we can. [**I hope you join me today in doing so by calling on the Senate to pass the _Freedom To Vote Act._**](http://actionnetwork.org/petitions/pass-the-freedom-to-vote-act-now?source=ECU_2022_FTVA_KE&referrer=group-kirsten-engel-for-arizona&redirect=https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel-for-arizona-1?refcode=X_11222_VotingRightsJA&amount=25)
Thank you,
Kirsten
---

name: PrimarySinema.com
subject: MLK III on Kyrsten Sinema
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHANGE FOR ARIZONA 2024 PAC**
body:
TAKE A LOOK: Martin Luther King III speaks out against Kyrsten Sinema's blocking of key voting rights legislation... | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953ylv71NjtRp9uAvapwZeG5Fwod7vf2Q19deFkSzINQB7pYO7Ed0TOwyvjuedJ39zpEnMq3REs_6qpHFEbObeQSefEuosMlyzL6pkt-qavlTwHb-DF4jjuE2cU9_8s2OZe66CPcZc2OXuueCKIvRfQggqC1NOjkFG4-DNrgTRR43M932qBrIo7yDO-bkTXVWLxLtjanAePRevGhjz_HyMh3QCa2-geZdmAJZJm7MjL09jf4JwAezkFV-sXdmIY8YrY3W13ai44vC5Ivz-hMbRhqrMbsKQWNOq8WcfMUtqredQbW249cOQElMhYtpAl9h6yM/3is/VmHoYdggSPybrh6OuCyxgw/h0/bPASJ9_pdBqg-jkubmfj2xhZg8A4OfTtWKkdn0BUKTc)
Peter, Six decades after Dr. King warned the filibuster is being used as a tool to stop Black Americans from voting, senators like Kyrsten Sinema are still standing in the way of an equitable, open democracy. **After Sinema killed the voting rights bill this week, Martin Luther King III said:**

> _“History will remember Sen. Sinema unkindly... She's siding with the legacy of Bull Connor and George Wallace instead of the legacy of my father and all those who fought to make real our democracy.”_
Today as we think back on that legacy, let's redouble our efforts to pressure misguided senators like Kyrsten Sinema to do the right thing. [**Help us raise money now to primary Kyrsten Sinema and replace her with a leader that will choose an equitable democracy over the filibuster. Join us if you can by making a $5 donation today.**](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wsEvCNw9RRN8fJ6P2o2MeS0Jz5PkW1g8wWMMDVdjZFWp1087PLga5Z_fV1p8CEkC4PgrN1Sp33SQ-YR5puPTi2tEzvv4B4mpIS_vUfbZkBG-6XXf0oeYv_acaNv2_IODZrwYAucJizORdM_50VxZlk27uZ3TQnRCsgRrNUjN2kgHuZ4iQHInVxz2RIPslRVAJmVKjZe9tznn3QD2f1El_ngYOSAGz-vDYo-xbsBdIIMcKSYVP8i-LyP1FdsyINDpC2696aZEGDn1UgAv-feNmGtnbRkdnj-iaKWKN_NDYmJCgqGdrTvgXYBxcAzfrLz0M/3is/VmHoYdggSPybrh6OuCyxgw/h1/E4LMObpwz72ZXvH6O8OLp1mJhD2CR9QsNp_c6cUbVDU) **[Contribute $5](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wsEvCNw9RRN8fJ6P2o2MeS0Jz5PkW1g8wWMMDVdjZFWsR-mPr9xpLIAGHY68f3bdcjUydIPovD8HTWK8Jrv-1fYuuLoWRLvET03_qPLIckwQSXG4aZ6Qa3NfizF-Sx8_4NJkqh-wHMYGkzuVI1VmdDgOIJpqjhVUuWB_qRhEcdVLxxjikWRWHRVuPCq-cISXFLEqD7_PYn36C_RGlnHttnWEfoocgydDfyt5wEFp8IFdHO8bzs-v2QFOSRM9yNMvnb-CEAGeSsV37ARVZo5PZ-4FvNP9jRDG_YnbLaTualqMWnNr9XKigclN9jhybDUjM/3is/VmHoYdggSPybrh6OuCyxgw/h2/YilpYbHm7eYYh_tQWVkftjUyTygKCXjsFqNQhXiTJVg)** |
**“I think the tragedy is that we have a Congress with a Senate that has a minority of misguided senators who will use the filibuster to keep the majority of people from even voting.”** _\- Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. in 1963_
---
In the face of an assault on voting rights nationwide, holding leaders accountable to the ideals espoused by Dr. King is more important than ever before.
Thanks for being part of this,
Primary Sinema Project

clean_body: TAKE A LOOK: Martin Luther King III speaks out against Kyrsten Sinema's blocking of key voting rights legislation... | | | Peter, Six decades after Dr. King warned the filibuster is being used as a tool to stop Black Americans from voting, senators like Kyrsten Sinema are still standing in the way of an equitable, open democracy. After Sinema killed the voting rights bill this week, Martin Luther King III said: “History will remember Sen. Sinema unkindly... She's siding with the legacy of Bull Connor and George Wallace instead of the legacy of my father and all those who fought to make real our democracy.” Today as we think back on that legacy, let's redouble our efforts to pressure misguided senators like Kyrsten Sinema to do the right thing. | “I think the tragedy is that we have a Congress with a Senate that has a minority of misguided senators who will use the filibuster to keep the majority of people from even voting.” \- Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. in 1963 In the face of an assault on voting rights nationwide, holding leaders accountable to the ideals espoused by Dr. King is more important than ever before. name: Madison Horn
subject: MLK’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Oklahomans for Madison
body:
| Today’s Martin Luther King Jr. Day. It is because of the work of MLK and others in the civil rights movement that we have the Voting Rights Act of 1965 — a massive law that strengthened our national voting rights.

**[But, for years now, the people elected to work _for us_ have been chipping away at the Voting Rights Act. That’s why we’ve got to act now. Please sign my petition to take a stand for voting rights and demand the passage of federal voting legislation. ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778446/325901405/614150750?source=2022FilibusterCarveOut_MH&referrer=group-oklahomans-for-madison-horn&redirect=https://secure.actblue.com/donate/madison-up-22%3Famount%3D10%26refcode%3DMH_EM_JA_1MO_220114_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_JA_1MO_220117_1_3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNzA3NzlhMy1kMDc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2Y3-8CS9A2FwMjL4lllgQRru6OIjlusqq7dRmjcnO7o=&emci=2cbb37d5-5175-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=f70779a3-d077-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=5119200)**

Voting is the key of any democracy. The U.S. holds itself as a beacon of democracy, but we haven’t been acting that way. One of the nations with the lowest voter turnout rates of any developed nation.

We can — and must — do better. Looking at the life of Martin Luther King Jr., John Lewis, or any of the other great champions of voting rights, we owe it to them to take this stand for democracy.

Let’s get to work!

Madison

Voting is the key of any democracy. The U.S. holds itself as a beacon of democracy, but we haven’t been acting that way. name: Right Alerts on behalf of Hillsdale College
subject: How should we elect the president?
body:
| _Sponsored by Hillsdale College_

| | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=4d2ffd4f5dd14012acac58ef8342dbf7&_e=ZUxsPJ1r4lK554dFcn7s2F4TKBR1QmbcN8SEv8V_t2K1-pbWUE_gfcSct_NHRuqorQJCwxTLBsmLbdd6BGAfygwIe3Ck2J5jvM9rpn8Kn3-6RR4WL8eHxbzhdT0kQzoxL-JvDkf0lLmEa-RlfYLOhaOmyL_PO6qNNTG12McfYs-9YwlN_rLhSNY21TQOI8GRmDKIt8owRT7-ds9VOhPzbI2VjP2qcb-hzVclAFlkVxe6a1WXop6jyWOIbbZd9vlxYmudZ9dKoyMK1Sg2w9H9wMist1xVVmrethMQnCNUm9tnRuJIbrDeLIx7a0j0mXPOrImA58RoXZGD7XonsRJyU0_J0U99NZYUFT8qW5x-XZI1rS5zpIaq_7_W8VrDZD5Wo-_1tPcLhvBxV33JcIVEq59De1jJDu5D8ybV06XzLr_AjEsq7rgKZJQ9EPDhI9G-) Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation’s future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That’s why Hillsdale College is conducting a **National Survey on Presidential Selection.**

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** by using the link below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=4d2ffd4f5dd14012acac58ef8342dbf7&_e=ZUxsPJ1r4lK554dFcn7s2F4TKBR1QmbcN8SEv8V_t2K1-pbWUE_gfcSct_NHRuqorQJCwxTLBsmLbdd6BGAfygwIe3Ck2J5jvM9rpn8Kn3-6RR4WL8eHxbzhdT0kQzoxL-JvDkf0lLmEa-RlfYLOhaOmyL_PO6qNNTG12McfYs-9YwlN_rLhSNY21TQOI8GRmDKIt8owRT7-ds9VOhPzbI2VjP2qcb-hzVclAFlkVxfkWb0IZ0cUBbm55iFPBqFdUJ7Djcl0F1kp6yNHBLUQJgYBtiH8iJR7hKa4HdE7RLWA5EYVjUs5RrWPQo9rTE4ZTa9F_IviBsJt_GbgK_IwhpGtNnaZCQY2XAR7Eahi4F-CCuQf7LALBeTfkuTQ_lSsMy4f7NjA8OTQyJJw-VMMii2tDmdT-yZ1Z2JHXZy18SlzqUqvxlrW3bBmGUWA271W)**

For most of our nation’s history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

**But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.**

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America’s schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don’t understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching _millions_ of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left’s domination over most of our schools, include:

• **Teaching** millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as “Constitution 101” and “The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;”
• **Reaching** over 5.6 million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis , Hillsdale’s free digest of liberty;
• **Promoting** constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;
• **Sending** millions of pocket-size copies of America’s founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America, as well as to members of our armed forces; and
• **Restoring** excellence to K-12 education through Hillsdale’s Barney Charter School Initiative, which launches and provides guidance to classical K-12 charter schools nationwide.

Your answers to the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

**And this is especially important in 2020, a critical year for America’s future.**

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our **National Survey on Presidential Selection** and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using the secure link I’ve provided below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=4d2ffd4f5dd14012acac58ef8342dbf7&_e=ZUxsPJ1r4lK554dFcn7s2F4TKBR1QmbcN8SEv8V_t2K1-pbWUE_gfcSct_NHRuqorQJCwxTLBsmLbdd6BGAfygwIe3Ck2J5jvM9rpn8Kn3-6RR4WL8eHxbzhdT0kQzoxL-JvDkf0lLmEa-RlfYLOhaOmyL_PO6qNNTG12McfYs-9YwlN_rLhSNY21TQOI8GRmDKIt8owRT7-ds9VOhPzbI2VjP2qcb-hzVclAFlkVxfwctbAmwKZmiXV9p9uyZFqx2foW6C9VpT6aJCMAh0E1JzFUCdR5FPOovyQOiy7x5w2cyVrS7LVVfz0oV3z9F5hxLuTGGBxVzFp3Sil1THLddyB__-TWKd0T0k6lw5kvOupMFUp2dEvMLU7szumkg2EVVX3dRgveX46l-D5yIPCbzbWCmQhj71SGiHJzhi5Z3b1npQGbg9uOixC6vf2kaPh)**

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been “pursuing truth and defending liberty.” Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts, while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

---

Hillsdale College. All rights reserved.

---
Don't want to receive any more of our emails? [Click here to unsubscribe](http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=4d2ffd4f5dd14012acac58ef8342dbf7&_t=nYWmhlUH1UuVW0Sd291Afoh7qhAKFtYPR4cbOHzvAez_Ai2qHqHzLvQmFTaFoqFmtZEjdrGxlK-cEHsSAR9Oav8lO5UO0dDqyKRh9D-_fJ2I9ErX6wGrMwb3TX40ZUHMHdgZQE8GXWc7gRgYWvqoEc85dqXxWyerRfaFqN5jzkU%3D).

| | Dear Patriot:

Here in Arkansas, we support our Second Amendment.

The Regime doesn't give us our freedoms.

_God does._

But our God-given right to Self-Defense is under attack like never before from the Left.

**They want to take our guns. They say it every day.

_RINOs like my opponent, Deep State Steve Womack, are helping them do it by supporting Red Flag gun confiscation._**

They're defunding the police.

They're letting drug dealers, rapists, and murderers go free. George Soros-funded District Attorneys across our country are refusing to enforce the law.

Crime is out of control, and murders are skyrocketing.

**Meanwhile, they want to make sure that normal, decent, patriotic citizens like you and I can't defend ourselves.**

They want to make sure that all we have is a criminal underclass and a totalitarian overclass.

_Look at Australia now. _The government is rounding up people and putting them in concentration camps in the name of a hoax pandemic.

How did it come to this? **Australians were forced to give up their guns long ago.**

_There has never been more reason to own a gun. You owe it to yourself and, more importantly, to your family._

Be ready to defend them against the predators that the Left is unleashing against all of us.

**I'm giving away a Radical Firearms 16" SOCOM 5.56 AR-15 Rifle.**

[](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=3e2857a9e5&e=eda539b26b)

Initially, this was a Christmas giveaway. But, after the Republican National Committee banned me from WinRed, I decided to extend it for Valentine's Day--_because there is no better expression of love than to be ready to defend your loved ones._

[_**Enter today for your chance to win!**_](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=30e70bb818&e=eda539b26b)

We'll be choosing the winner at random just before Valentine's Day, and you could end up with a brand new AR-15 at your door.

[_**Though contributing is not required in order to win, I'm hoping you'll also chip into my America First campaign**_](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=2e195f9ea0&e=eda539b26b)[.](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=06acee46f8&e=eda539b26b)

[_**I need your help to defeat a weak RINO and protect our God-given rights in Washington, so enter my gun giveaway today, and please contribute what you can**_](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=aae311834d&e=eda539b26b)[. ](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=b91e367372&e=eda539b26b)

For America,
Neil Robinson Kumar
_Republican for U.S. Congress, AR-03_

**P.S. I'm giving away an AR-15 for Valentine's Day!** [_**Enter for your chance to win, and while you're at it, chip in to support my America First congressional campaign**_](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=8f6a0e7851&e=eda539b26b)[** _._**](https://NeilforArkansas.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b07b27c744909d0ecfce3d46&id=9fa6801d01&e=eda539b26b)

name: Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis,
[View this message in your browser](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeca06dnkrlcqrnls2m1b41ckbrqueb66lth0238_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jkgqracjkmoq37d1m65qbfctjmkrncst66uqrf91ieoob9crl6uie5sj2ujh74o75elpnep1im1h6cp1gstgefcjhcsq6csb6c1pj6pjh6jgn3d9ieppm6s77q9o74p1m62p6csjk65r61o5kckoe1d9hcjj6cphlc2pm4pti65j72cljcorr1db5e8gq48bi4bh28or1c9gm2p73cpj649fg)
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeu35u1nm86ndnq21qtab6bme4spb1bpjh7mjvbo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm9or6cph2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zkq5jdp9dm8j3neq6il3s5h27u0gt31633j4ji28_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm9or6cph2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrgm1knasrgbtjn97t1096k24i3g7s1vsss0de98_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5gm9or6cph2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-hf")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is hold those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**

Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

name: Tom Perez
subject: Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day
body:
Dr. King once said that the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice. It’s up to us to put our hands on the arc and keep pulling it towards justice – together.
#

Everyone,

Today, I think of how Dr. King devoted his life to making real the promise of democracy for all Americans. As our country continues to grapple with the civil unrest over the systemic racism that still pervades our society, we see states emboldened to pass legislation that undoes the very progress for which Dr. King gave his life.

Since the U.S. Supreme Court gutted the heart and soul of the Voting Rights Act in 2013, state legislatures have seized the opportunity to further restrict the right to vote, introducing more than 400 voter suppression bills in 2021 alone. These widespread efforts to silence the voice of the people and roll back Dr. King's legacy serve as a reminder of what's at stake and what work there is left to do.

Dr. King inspired generations of civil rights activists and taught us, most importantly, to never stop pushing forward. As the Senate debated the John Lewis Voting Rights Act that would restore and strengthen parts of the Voting Rights Act, I thought of how Dr. King once said that the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice. **It is up to us to put our hands on the arc and keep pulling it towards justice – together.**

Thank you,

Tom
Authority: Tom Perez for Maryland, Stanley J. Botts, Treasurer.
Tom Perez for Maryland
PO BOX 5389
TAKOMA PARK, MD 20913-5389
United States

name: David Palmer
subject: To honor Dr. King, we must demand action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Palmer for Illinois
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778541/325902821/312490568?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg2MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYzUzNzhiZC1kMjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=821dJwb7MW6Kev-276TbJLNDdE8WSQmcd2IiKT9zYo4=) | Peter, On the day we celebrate the birth of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and all of his contributions to humanity, we must also reflect on the work that needs to be done to fully realize the dream he spoke of during his sermon at the March on Washington in 1963. **The power in Dr. King’s words have stuck with me every step of this journey. I hear him in my heart when I talk to moms and dads struggling to put food on the table. I hear him in my soul when a farmer comes to me uncertain of how they will retire because the economy has taken a turn. And I feel it in my bones when I think of the future we are creating for the next generation.** Dr. King believed that lasting change must be achieved by peaceful means. His mission required a tenacity, a long-term persistence, an inner strength that most of us cannot fathom, and which we rarely see in today’s divisive environment. **We must continue the work of Dr. King, together. We cannot achieve our goals if we don’t approach everything from a place of love and commitment to change.** As a public servant, I draw inspiration from King’s life and legacy, and I use his memory to inform me as I navigate my own path.**I hope you will join me in honoring his incredible gifts to our nation and world.** Today, we all honor King’s legacy. We recognize his struggle, we cherish his sacrifice, and we embrace his faith in equality and justice for all. Thank you, David Palmer

body:

\- Marcy Kaptur | |

| | Team,

Today we honor the legacy of the greatest American dreamer, Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Dr. King has been an inspiration to me since I was a young woman. All these years later, **I aspire to uphold his Dream in the halls of Congress.**

Dr. King was not only a gifted orator and a champion of human rights. He was also a deeply political advocate for change.

Because of his shining example, we have made progress in this nation. But, some battles of his time hold over to ours. Inspired by his perseverance, we must push forward to achieve a perfect and more equitable union.

As he poignantly said, **“Life’s most persistent and urgent question is: ‘What are you doing for others?’”**

On this day, let us come together and renew our commitment to justice, equity, and the service of all our brethren.

In service,

name: Eddie Geller
subject: Dr. King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EDDIE GELLER FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778561/325903007/-1761186797?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220117MLKday_EG_op_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODIzM2Q1Yi1kMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=76E1MrW7VG2EKyN6LYsK8YjQ97awYuTNVxw-gf-ZD6I=)
---
Hey friends.
On this day that we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., I’m reflecting in particular on how much work we have left to do to achieve Dr. King’s dream.
The struggle to pass critical voting rights legislation in Congress is deeply frustrating. And if Congress fails, it won’t just be a blow for Democrats — it’ll be a blow for our country. I’m frankly disgusted with Republicans who continue to proclaim the need for “bipartisanship” while simultaneously subverting and undermining elections at every level, causing deep damage to the very foundation of our democracy.
But if Dr. King taught us anything, it’s that real change is never easy. With vision, tenacity, and an unwavering commitment to justice, change is still possible — but we must keep fighting for it.
**And for us here in Florida’s 15th District, that starts by ensuring that Scott Franklin never gets another term in Congress.**
[Donate ➞](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778562/325903008/-1761186797?refcode=fl_email_fr_20220117MLKday_EG_op_xx_btn&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODIzM2Q1Yi1kMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=76E1MrW7VG2EKyN6LYsK8YjQ97awYuTNVxw-gf-ZD6I=)

My opponent had not one but two chances to show that he supports the right of all Americans to vote. Both times, he showed his true colors by voting no on critical voting rights legislation, putting his party’s overt power grab before the health and stability of our democracy.
**My pledge to you today is that I will _always_ fight to protect and expand the right to vote — for ALL Americans, no matter where you live or whether you’re a Democrat, Republican, independent, or anything else.**
Thanks for standing with me, and for being on our team.
Eddie

name: Dr. Jason Martin
subject: Honoring Dr. King with more than empty sentiment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Jason Martin for Tennessee
body:
| | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778577/325903161/-371068714?refcode=fl_email_pt_20220117MLKvotingrights_DJM_opntl_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTRhZmY5ZC1kMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UZcAm3rDb8UC4rCuI3LuneHfS-MEiXhGPAa2c32NLN4=)
---
Hey, Peter.
Today, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
In a way, it’s fitting that this day comes so soon after the one year anniversary of the January 6 insurrection at the Capitol. Taken together, these two days first remind us of just how much work is left to be done to achieve Dr. King’s dream, while also reaffirming what Dr. King’s life and work teaches us: **that with vision, tenacity, and an unwavering commitment to justice, real change is possible.**
But that change isn’t going to happen on its own. We must recommit to our fight to form a more perfect union, protect every American’s right to vote, and, going further, do everything possible to make voting easy and accessible.
As you probably know, Republicans like my opponent Bill Lee will stop at absolutely nothing to suppress, intimidate, and subvert their way to power. They do not care one whit about the will of the people.
The Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act is Democrats’ last best shot to protect every American’s right to vote before the midterms, and prevent the overt power grab by GOP-held state legislatures to rig electoral processes in their favor. [**I’m calling on the Senate to honor Dr. King’s legacy by passing it immediately when they return to the chamber tomorrow, and I hope you’ll join me.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778578/325903162/-1220156938?refcode=fl_email_pt_20220117MLKvotingrights_DJM_opntl_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTRhZmY5ZC1kMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UZcAm3rDb8UC4rCuI3LuneHfS-MEiXhGPAa2c32NLN4=&emci=0e963e44-6875-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=0e4aff9d-d377-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=9304149)
I try not to get too involved in national politics, because I’m focused on the people of Tennessee and what they need from their next governor. But this is just too important — the very fabric of our democracy depends on it.
**And today, I can think of no better way to honor Dr. King’s memory and everything he stood for than by calling on the Senate to pass critical voting rights legislation.**
[**Will you sign our petition to demand that the Senate protect democracy at every level of government by passing the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778579/325903163/-1220156938?refcode=fl_email_pt_20220117MLKvotingrights_DJM_opntl_xx_l2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTRhZmY5ZC1kMzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UZcAm3rDb8UC4rCuI3LuneHfS-MEiXhGPAa2c32NLN4=&emci=0e963e44-6875-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=0e4aff9d-d377-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=9304149)

clean_body: ################### | | | Team Ruiz hopes that you and your family are safe and healthy. Public health across the country is our top concern during this unprecedented time. To view the most up to date CDC COVID-19 safety recommendations, And if you have a moment, please read our message below. We’re running out of time to protect Voting Rights and END GOP voter suppression. | | | | | | | | The truth is: We’re witnessing an all out assault on voting rights. From Georgia, to Texas, to Wisconsin, the GOP is THE anti-voting rights party. We can’t allow National Republicans to continue to pass HORRIBLE voter suppression laws like they did in these states. So we need a massive outpouring of support from Democrats to fight back if we want to keep our Democratic Majority out of the hands of voter suppression Republicans. | | | | | | Thanks for stepping up! \-- Team Ruiz | PO Box 3433 Palm Desert, CA 92261 PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE Please read the following message from Dr. Raul Ruiz: I'm so thankful for your support. With President Biden and Vice President Harris in the White House, we have a clear path forward. But we can't get complacent. We have to keep fighting if we're going to undo the damage Donald Trump has done. | Together we can protect access to health care, expand Social Security, and fight for vital institutions like the Post Office. Because it's what's right. As [1] of just [3] Democratic physicians in Congress, it's more important than ever that I am able to fight for our most vulnerable communities. But I need you in this fight with me: From the emergency room to the halls of Congress, it's my duty to do as much good as I can. Can I count on you to help me keep fighting in Congress? Every dollar makes a difference: | | | | | | I believe these emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with dedicated supporters like you. the late 1970s “malaise” of Democrat Jimmy Carter’s disastrous presidency, with the same consequences for America that we’re facing now. | | [](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oL5N9M4E5YeWxFPD8Rd-rG9EK7z1bP43DRu6eYgRWYT-gE86eOWjzcQtTRBRwSVayTfhYqkDEzphM5YA_utJtPthjhH_BstnYdLQ48jxoobVu7mNSxonrVxmnkDls3iVw7xLTfJNCS5Onw87labGdqhxqWAEn2PnnwRcOty9vr5VJxW3An5HLUadsgH89VTxHqLNq9_PkvqJ0Pb8rVGGs7EQ%3D) | Peter,

I remember it well, and if you were around, you probably do, too -- the late 1970s “malaise” of Democrat **Jimmy Carter’s** disastrous presidency, with the same consequences for America that we’re facing now.

Like then, the liberal Democrat agenda was being imposed on working Americans and families who also faced skyrocketing prices from rampant inflation, a surge in violent crime levels in our communities, scarcity of basic goods like food and gasoline, and chaos overseas.

But even during that dark time for our country, when the promise of the American Dream seemed almost a thing of the past, the will to demand better **\-- and achieve it --** grew in our people, and out of that movement we elected Ronald Reagan President -- _and the rest is history._

I was there, Peter, -- serving as Assistant to the President for Political Affairs in the Reagan White House, as we began the great reversal and revived our country from the Democrats’ “malaise” of crime, inflation, and chaos. By **unshackling the American people from tax-and-spend liberalism, restoring law and order, and reasserting confidence and strength overseas, we can still save the United States.**

Peter, that’s exactly what we’re working so hard to once again achieve here at Great America PAC -- empowering the people with the truth about the catastrophic Biden Democrat control of Washington, D.C., motivating grassroots organization against it, and preparing to support Trump Republican candidates who will sweep to victory in Congress in November.

But Peter, we’ve already entered the pivotal 2022 Midterm Election year, and as the leading outside group still supporting **President Donald Trump** and candidates nationwide who are running to defeat the globalist, socialist, **Pelosi-Schumer Democrats** tearing down President Trump’s commonsense policies and our country with it -- [**we’re counting on the immediate and ongoing support of patriots like you to get it done.**](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oLw0ZVZnwXR3okQMldxCCjVyp55kSiwEmrHlfvpr5aos6ZGb62syq6HahwfW0jb52UX8fshi7kbURSc0YZfRDLVUDdXa-TRjJuUv-w-Q2jqLMtXqKK0YgzjyUYwKpIQ3drmC4-U2m1XuD-TvTAvCq8mujTR_HOIY3sA5iVLh-LNbm1sIrcg9nYhXYngi7nSKSCiqgOk71aI9z7QE3zNILPCg%3D)

And we are once again taking the leading role for the legacy of President Trump and his America First agenda _by helping to elect as many Trump Republicans to Congress as we can,_ securing new GOP majorities in the U.S. House and Senate, and forcing Joe Biden to work for the American people, not against them **\-- and pave the way for another Trump run, or America First candidate in 2024.**

While the Biden Democrats can rely on the massive funding of globalist billionaire elites, Hollywood liberals, and left-wing special interest groups, we rely on [**the patriotic generosity of grassroots leaders like you.**](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oL8g6dN4ouN7hoMv8dv68Hiz3ZtQkvcDGNBaEbu_0DviwN-ZgtzeRBKyvVxLOpbYAj8RTIZwhzCnk2nJEZy6q3jJvgeWSB9LBwu7wlW2Cx2TbuyMRtnsm6WcD7KJF_ILQY_8vDFFr8-uLvbOu6zqXeR8-LbJLi2vz3ukqmxrvHI-SLAScWnospASWvqLjd3pjThPFZyeH6unKZXs4XrJmz30%3D)

You are among our key supporters, Peter -- I'm extremely grateful for everything you do -- [**and I'm asking you to continue that support today.**](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oL1eUXINCvbUhaxRWiRxE16z_pyNzq05REXVg_3yuq0YLoLJ4mB20cl7VousUZOOjvjMBygDafMrmOWqewBuIE5bFAVhmL98ktWwP7dbmqBUl3h5uOur-wlNmi7PUaZKA9Mzq5lh9BfSuX5goe8pKJEliU6_pvD6x8sMeWt7sydWobWLfyw9l4NOtMsASYlWQegoKmr2-B1gjLHyTbQhlvrQ%3D)

The kind of resources that have made possible over 25,000 TV spots, award-winning Trump ads, 300,000 radio ads, 2.5 million pieces of mail, 30 million phone calls, 2 national bus tours and staffed field offices in the fiercest battleground states

We have been -- and still are -- what _The Wall Street Journal_ called **“the largest, most reputable, and most active Super PAC backing Donald Trump”** \-- and our fight continues!

The stakes have never been higher, and Great America PAC’s ability to mobilize for victory in 2022 has never been needed more. So please, [**, help us continue to stand and fight for President Trump, his vision for America, and the men and women who are ready to fight for it in Washington, D.C.!**](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oLxIrRE5_uTmD_zh9Ud7vzDB8x4OUUQm270zu-qR7O8UGf0S5ucokKyhpp2wa49gr28NYmZHr2ZMIjHxzCHbdTokKhU6A3y7VuELL23BF4XyAzxLrH3F_0piuKSF6iw8HdnEyXjTV9GcjS0Ubs1RaAvV8CNJ5hJli71JA4ni4t9aSqdT2vYe3Ff6VwwEHg74bMXhdHV0CIEnuW3mRSKPCzWY%3D)

_Thank you,_ Peter -- [**I hope to hear from you today!**](http://links.action.greatamerica4usa.com/u/click?_t=b93111d5faf54ad99e89d66d6b494312&_m=a20d71bc75c842c89d64dd91bf5387fb&_e=4rKELNeqYdAbYDw-k0A37AHsODiYkJQs90FdNzS3sW5jWXUt10QL3DiTPaQ_WOYandEQK4TPI4Zw1vwSr4GtBz3QZMzUUYkA5btWS21BtggMnpDkEqGh8QPmpXR3varp-cUYn_PfMe4ILynXIbeGwJ_4fCkd4x6864edQpriNp7LpTWvzV4_d1mmNG-FeTrnq_AovPmi2o4PdO_sctU-uNY4guMJ5_Sm5mIxmfI1jTCgej1sRTR6yhjjzQ15wmApvijIaC6W4N2tHFI0Ht7oLw12p6L3O9v1pk0DBsvW3kW5g7tgPawkDUCbQRjsqcNlckI2cp5DH-TkpNoUm4PbiLfaM3--ONzsmELerRRhjAPxBJX--yxcpkv2I4HzXcF8F3fewdVN5C87pZoXXCCcfcRoYo75uMAR62X7OIk3OVCRljmOZmTTXTOFprzj8eR0LCEevhZtiqTu5i0Rw9bcGw0emNE8eLHRdqlU8rM6Ipg%3D)

Ed Rollins

subject: Meet Derrick Gates California's next Rep to Congress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DERRICK GATES FOR CONGRESS
body:
MEET DERRICK GATES Hello Friends, My name is Derrick Gates, Republican candidate for Congress in the now 36th District. Many people may not know yet
| | [](https://sable.madmimi.com/c/416378?id=10031.46.1.70ec0f8b04115e005a054209de58ebbc)
---
[](https://sable.madmimi.com/c/416378?id=10031.47.1.bd03afdc2cf0becc2a45b544a41ea036) |
---
__
# MEET DERRICK GATES
__ Hello Friends, My name is Derrick Gates, Republican candidate for Congress in the now 36th District. Many people may not know yet, but the 33rd has now changed to 36th. While the district number has changed, the objective has not - to beat Ted Lieu. I would like to introduce myself to you and talk about why our campaign represents the best shot we have had to beat Lieu since he was first elected. I’m a husband, father, Christian, American patriot, small business owner, minister, and community leader. I am far from a perfect man - I have no hesitation admitting that. I tackled my flaws head-on, had an intimate run-in with Jesus Christ, and turned my life around. Since the moment I met Jesus, I have dedicated my life to the ministry where I counsel those who are
struggling, sit beside those who have lost their loved ones, help the less fortunate, and even kick down doors to physically pull people out of crack houses. My wife and I feed the hungry, house the homeless, turn former prisoners into productive members of society, and help the addicted get clean. We do all of these things while being forthright in our faith and belief in limited government, conservative principles, and pro-family values. We help our community, but we also defend the Constitutional rights of Americans, advocate for the unborn, fight for medical freedom, and are vocally supportive of the restoration of personal freedom and parental rights. My life experience and consistent defense of our shared values, combined with the political environment of this midterm election, provides us with a unique opportunity. Make no mistake about it - we can do what Republicans did in New Jersey and Virginia this past November. 2022 is undoubtedly going to be a wave year - so why not us? But I can’t win this race alone. We all know that campaigns are a costly endeavor, and with my family’s financial resources dedicated to our ministry, I need the help of patriots like you. Can you contribute $25, $50, $100, $250, or any amount you can afford today? To contribute, [click here](https://sable.madmimi.com/c/416378?id=10031.48.1.26ee1f7bb30e114c095440f8db8b71a7). Together, we can show Los Angeles that America First means People First.
Thank you and God Bless, Derrick Gates P.S. If you would like to discuss my campaign, give me a call at 424-216-6893. Learn more about Derrick and see his endorsements so far below.
[www.officialderrickgates.com](https://sable.madmimi.com/c/416378?id=10031.49.1.3fc44088fb58e45cc5de3596e5608390).
---

name: Trish Gunby
subject: Reflecting on Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trish Gunby for Congress [unsubscribe](https://email
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40778803/325904496/368400405?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjAzMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODYwYTBkZi1kNzc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3_lbsSQyJL11J2jIWjZ8yFCZ12y_P1TF3PEOLu-UQzc=&emci=cf4ec4ce-cb77-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=0860a0df-d777-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=14943357) Peter, Today is a day when many of us will be thinking about Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. and the legacy of the Civil Rights Movement. Progress is messy, and seemingly never comes in a straight line...an idea that is more true today than we may want to admit. But a valuable lesson we can learn from the struggle of Dr. King and thousands of others stretched across our complicated history is that _we cannot give up_ —**we must choose hope, not despair; and we must love, and not hate**. As we reflect today, we should also keep in mind that many politicians, media personalities, and shameless opportunists will be inappropriately using Dr. King—taking his words, his memory, and his message out of context—to provide an expedient and "acceptable" cover for actions and beliefs that are wildly incongruous with what the man and the movement stood for. These charlatans dishonor the legacy of MLK. But it is on us to fight back—with words, yes, including the words of Dr. King himself, but more importantly _**with action**_. Today, to honor the man and the legacy, I pledge myself to act: for racial, economic, and social justice. And I hope you will do the same. clean_body: Peter, Today is a day when many of us will be thinking about Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. and the legacy of the Civil Rights Movement. Progress is messy, and seemingly never comes in a straight line...an idea that is more true today than we may want to admit. But a valuable lesson we can learn from the struggle of Dr. King and thousands of others stretched across our complicated history is that we cannot give up —we must choose hope, not despair; and we must love, and not hate. As we reflect today, we should also keep in mind that many politicians, media personalities, and shameless opportunists will be inappropriately using Dr. King—taking his words, his memory, and his message out of context—to provide an expedient and "acceptable" cover for actions and beliefs that are wildly incongruous with what the man and the movement stood for. These charlatans dishonor the legacy of MLK. But it is on us to fight back—with words, yes, including the words of Dr. King himself, but more importantly with action. Today, to honor the man and the legacy, I pledge myself to act: for racial, economic, and social justice. And I hope you will do the same. Sincerely, Trish Gunby for Congress PO Box 430114 Saint Louis, MO 63143 United States Paid for by Trish Gunby for Congress

name: Team AOC
subject: "I’ve been to the mountaintop"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Please read an excerpt from Dr. King’s speech, “I’ve Been to the Mountaintop”:
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20220117-2738-header&t=1&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)
This Martin Luther King Day, we encourage you to read an excerpt from Dr. King’s speech, “I’ve Been to the Mountaintop” below and reflect on the many lessons shared by Dr. King that still ring true today.
_On April 3, 1968, the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. was in Memphis to support striking sanitation workers when he was asked at the last minute to speak at the Mason Temple church because a crowd of a few thousand people wanted to see him. It would be his final speech._
_This is an excerpt. To read the full annotated version,**[click here](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/10370?t=2&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)**._
“Something is happening in Memphis; something is happening in our world. And you know, if I were standing at the beginning of time, with the possibility of taking a kind of general and panoramic view of the whole of human history up to now, and the Almighty said to me, “Martin Luther King, which age would you like to live in?
[...]
Strangely enough, I would turn to the Almighty, and say, “If you allow me to live just a few years in the second half of the 20th century, I will be happy.”
Now that’s a strange statement to make, because the world is all messed up. The nation is sick. Trouble is in the land; confusion all around. That’s a strange statement. But I know, somehow, that only when it is dark enough can you see the stars. And I see God working in this period of the twentieth century in a way that men, in some strange way, are responding. Something is happening in our world. The masses of people are rising up. And wherever they are assembled today, whether they are in Johannesburg, South Africa; Nairobi, Kenya; Accra, Ghana; New York City; Atlanta, Georgia; Jackson, Mississippi; or Memphis, Tennessee—the cry is always the same: “We want to be free.”
And another reason that I’m happy to live in this period is that we have been forced to a point where we are going to have to grapple with the problems that men have been trying to grapple with through history, but the demands didn’t force them to do it. Survival demands that we grapple with them. Men, for years now, have been talking about war and peace. But now, no longer can they just talk about it. It is no longer a choice between violence and nonviolence in this world; it’s nonviolence or nonexistence. That is where we are today.
And also in the human rights revolution, if something isn’t done, and done in a hurry, to bring the colored peoples of the world out of their long years of poverty, their long years of hurt and neglect, the whole world is doomed. Now, I’m just happy that God has allowed me to live in this period to see what is unfolding. And I’m happy that He’s allowed me to be in Memphis.
[...]
And that’s all this whole thing is about. We aren’t engaged in any negative protest and in any negative arguments with anybody. We are saying that we are determined to be men. We are determined to be people. We are saying—We are saying that we are God’s children. And that we are God’s children, we don’t have to live like we are forced to live.
Now, what does all of this mean in this great period of history? It means that we’ve got to stay together. We’ve got to stay together and maintain unity. You know, whenever Pharaoh wanted to prolong the period of slavery in Egypt, he had a favorite, favorite formula for doing it. What was that? He kept the slaves fighting among themselves. But whenever the slaves get together, something happens in Pharaoh’s court, and he cannot hold the slaves in slavery. When the slaves get together, that’s the beginning of getting out of slavery. Now let us maintain unity.”
_To read the rest of the speech,_**[_click here_](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/10370?t=3&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)** _._
In our world today, there is confusion, there is trouble, and the nation is sick (quite literally). But as Dr. King says, “only when it is dark enough can you see the stars”.
The masses of people are rising up and we are being forced to grapple with the problems of the last century because it’s imperative to our survival. To keep fighting and achieving the change that we need, we must do it together, unified as one.
_“_ _I’ve seen the Promised Land. I may not get there with you. But I want you to know tonight, that we, as a people, will get to the promised land!” -Dr. Martin Luther King Jr._
Thank you.
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ocasio-campaign?refcode=em20220117-2738-P_ENG_EXP_GRM&t=4&refcode2=2738_2526796_VY8b4O&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20220117-2738-P_ENG_EXP_GRM&t=5&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)**

[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=6&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=7&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=8&akid=2738%2E2526796%2EVY8b4O)

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
