name: Kina Collins
subject: Today, we honor Dr. King
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KINA COLLINS FOR CONGRESS** ](https://u1584542
body:
We are at another inflection point in American history. | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yOQozAy4249GfohatM8XLCP7Gyf6bzrkjN8sXh_JjXvDm21fvmGLhqUe-quR3_x0bPZYqqRmKw-ebpnqlyWdsbb4WUxyyDpjjPMxiSxHH8s-Ujdl1CjdB-S4OsCqbTBI30Rma8CwONmCF4nNzvMfSxLF8lQpHgZM5S4cp3ThPoQNTPPDR3T3BednpkDxPWL6khbc9gFzcMUEaf_yS3EUNvDyQRg_RHiveg97DX2mWFMNR2JbleKanZtIp6hgZwnuBTHukJFQO4jFM7uDdmouC-psInuvYjIcPfHTPeTRmnLLPg866hO19oBU05ATHh1oMgmVsuH6MvAbQfD0SzSup9gUW32rzcrI8MEDcudteucA/3is/QTHOFMKtSdWveGGxMKDJyw/h0/yidOCMoj1t2BhC8I6nqSjHGuZXg3hHN4Am4jpcQtf5I) Dear team, Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day - a day we honor the legacy of a giant whose shoulders we still stand on, whose fight we’re still fighting. Dr. King was a visionary - someone who knew that racial and economic justice were inextricably linked in this country - and someone who was dismissed as a young radical while he was alive. Across the country, as we build a movement to combat our most urgent crises, it is young people with bold solutions and uncompromising values that are leading us towards a better future. Dr. King was just 26 years old when he led the Montgomery Bus Boycott, John Lewis was 25 when he marched across the Edmund Pettus Bridge, and the Little Rock Nine were all less than 18 years old. Just like now, the moment called for action, and Dr. King and countless other young, Black leaders stepped up and answered the call, by fighting against injustice and achieving real progress for the Black community. **As we mark 54 years since the assasination of Dr. King, I wrote an****[op-ed for the Chicago Defender](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/Mqag7sW8TIW-nixyBdulQkjlZ-RhBfRqwEgfVjtIDQ6PdrwYLwBLchWzsLbhqe7tB27dL4JSRqpckLB9-WLicht7fXHGq7uc-WjxsQyWstQ2YH9Tk1kk4aNlgRjBJEtjs7_OHhYekIHTU8UOl4g0pN4Uu2-YVKan8tsuV2bjaBy4odEBWEf-JKhGEPe3y3u4kgTk13Pcw_lomcKmuvPclXTOQse7dqvVoc0-aPRXknAdiGWmCNssdYneuIba538LX3YLUwns8kGtLDkScSRuPVGAXnfg5LyoKxkXaKObwHoZfAioiiLG_inNRn35s5zs0vsx967W1gYySDddCu8u2NK_cn_FI32Dvb4MOuoQLEThAVqHrBnb6K6pHhp4NTV6yYx9-1wZWU6BYtuBeRtGCDoP-_QUddJCnvsy2FLBwGc/3is/QTHOFMKtSdWveGGxMKDJyw/h1/cwuUnxvZe04bIuI-ULN4B64F0phmBvpY8kXO_MjsgtU)****–one of Chicago’s oldest Black newspapers–calling for a new generation of leaders.** The urgency of this moment requires urgency from our leaders, but too many here in Chicago and in D.C. have failed to step up. **We are at another inflection point in American history. Racial justice is on the line. Economic justice is on the line. Our democracy is on the line. Climate justice, the future of our planet is on the line.** From Chicago’s Good Kids Mad City to the Sunrise Movement and United We Dream, young people are fighting these battles because they have seen that no one else will — and because they can’t afford not to. For the elders, whose shoulders we stand on, it’s time to step up by stepping back, and help a new generation lead the fight against injustice everywhere. In this critical moment, there is an invaluable lesson to be learned from Dr. King’s legacy, the civil rights movement, and the other fighters for justice that came before us. **Now more than ever, young people belong at the front of our movement.** In solidarity, Kina Collins P.S.: Dr. King [opposed ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/dSCQfi9FLISmU3ZE3bfPhhiCNB_cGud18X8RGbb_P7A9aXJWD8hPEJ4pApWDn2SSm1bzXmTrGsmd2VJhtgn97ZiNKebSpL_syrDy51ajVAzQ_GqfaGpAriw9cqrJKnSG6QPHMVn50YROvzEgEDoULGl1xBDtLuc_CKuLKWpNm1cZA4AuoRklAYWjbDVmLgRtTAGrES6g4z-5dzhAjViMb5vIgnmmBeeLW91ZyVUYQZO4PTP-odbyl_jthktwJgRaJzkJ0c8XFbsDEIHu7DVTnTBD6QjMyxaDT0Ru_LZh7rWgnBu7gfgPG-X3HDG-imbK--tkgysm5VAB0K6OUJ0Dd1EPhEwcyKVd_c2KmBaX6Ug/3is/QTHOFMKtSdWveGGxMKDJyw/h2/TrBZJtXf2lFi_7lY3a0eCTAGEnNRtTeXGQr6ZtOCTv4)the filibuster.

[Donate »](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yOQozAy4249GfohatM8XLCP7Gyf6bzrkjN8sXh_JjXvCmYnnW-6I0oaLoxnMYHNBtfCMc8MElj_R9J3sgrgmO6ozuaTzo_rQLzV_a7waH3Pi54pS06M5IbP4b42iT0hruBsXPRqglRE7Z80ra3ldZHKki5o320skrpfLnsXtSTpSpt66vtMkwZ09nFm45tZnySmN_Bnpb75sN50sS3OEVgt72BOthxg5Y1W_VgU73AWnJsIJ7xVHrWZ72588waOBFzUGIrsDm5WBoOGyImAuyKngVRUd6UN-LphMUX5CmxdRd9eREHjpMtTw4X5uegqMBdbwKDGM-91lGR_88fXmLO3pXNY1dtAZhqJXKi7GI3pA/3is/QTHOFMKtSdWveGGxMKDJyw/h3/AjgCRrgpxtbhLPlZpQ6F9nIg-3DtViGMK1KylsGkWvM) | To give by check:

name: First Lady Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis,
[View this message in your browser](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zoqcetf908muhgrciis3s3o1a469grn64qq3brl0_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jkgqracjkmoq37c9l6cqj7djl6eqjetjjkorrbdt469r31d5jekrq9onicbqe2d1lmor3ac35msr36tdim4qr4dtmc5gm2ttnmli6ecbmmdonbd76clqe1d9nmv970sj46oob4pjiegonco70mhij1o5l65iecpj66lgb6oj0e8pmcd70mdi3fc5lclp238h1e8he4933c5h62pb6c9h6aoh5u)
[****](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z4s40ol96uiap437lsd02tfi3nb5dn76a4llu61g_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zum65q0hu1lfpt5mi7aea2gplmkirhasde1n7gt0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2znh54u88a4ag765crt69g5p15frcaa3456o1ektg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-rvs-pa?utm_source=prospecting&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pa_adn&utm_tc=ADN-20220117")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**
[**Please rush in a contribution to help us continue to hold the line and keep Florida free. Chip in $20.22, $30, or $50 now!**](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zfthbahmhujhmaiefrnuursbcgu7vmpap9cdhf1g_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs)
Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

[](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z8ivhe64ef72bshr1443r10af28la6umna487vqo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs "CHIP IN NOW") [CHIP IN NOW](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zuec6nvp02nfo24ta6vvq0j940un9375dpao4oqo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imcoj2clh2bs)

Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable. He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude. Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems. Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become. Get ready, Florida - Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you. Sincerely, Casey DeSantis "CHIP IN NOW" Paid by Ron DeSantis, Republican, for Governor Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. Platt Street, Suite 345, Tampa, FL 33606 If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for American Defense News, . 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: Bobby Jindal
subject: Washington Examiner: Ending the COVID-19 emergency
body:
[](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=f1447e008afe48feada96676e19f02ea&_e=tgaF_Iagy8SkKf2TGPIbh03opsgALJ5rL1eciGwl54jkZPlncqkw_vFQlfKbvmybJ8-D4JACluQ94S4yvY8psFofhAcgYF9yfvWlHlSFOwSTmWsRgumvJBv7HVw9YT9JezsohQpNt3-qDB0HjmQ5-mvkR-__9MvS74bYFfDkdzONll_eHDAeTFe_tIDuOg2kOSRQQMJr6yhIZXMINzbBB_EOs1IlxekuB_VEe2qWie3KrJgv0shbCymhemkovG0kn9Z572lVqZUveFwVQn8vVg%3D%3D) Ending the COVID-19 emergency By Bobby Jindal & Heidi Overton | |
---
"When will the emergency end?" Justice Amy Coney Barrett asked during the Supreme Court’s recent vaccine mandates hearing.

The nation is asking the same question.

This is a pivotal moment in the two-year pandemic. Omicron has created record-high daily cases, but severe outcomes are fewer,ushering in a new phase — the likely transition to endemic status. Emergency order expiration dates are looming. Unless renewed, the public health emergency order should end on Sunday, and the national emergency declaration expires on March 1. Policymakers need to relinquish emergency powers at the end of the surge and plot a sustainable path forward.

Dr. Monica Gandhi, a University of California, San Francisco infectious disease expert, predicted months ago that COVID-19 would become endemic, perhaps like the flu, always circulating with seasonal peaks. She outlined that the transition would occur when the disease severity decreased and the immunity from vaccines and prior infections increased...
_Read more_**[HERE](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=f1447e008afe48feada96676e19f02ea&_e=tgaF_Iagy8SkKf2TGPIbh50ek-W-wdKGL-izls7da3O-Zx3u1MFAfbpshdJVFi1pFMzI7zhvWOdNpUQy6y_1InVGGc2nLJZsYaW2rpNXwICsTskgj3OE0d_MzB2YFtrjEm86Hr6LH0pP8YdZy3lc2nbBYLPTE20sPFFB7j3w3rbNowaIzcTtOigPFmrdJ6TPTs4X9Fj38pt3rbZFDP4yXLAJuh7bE7A9qPXMUAAK_4TH51H0-1GnUHxeh5_5Z-fcqWd2jk3LMw6bckLa0MGyx9sht1TFF3cImMCwptijHfDeAF92wAU14HW84XIG61XGRKEuBe-4QJ47oaVog5KWJQ%3D%3D)**.

subject: continuing the fight

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40779134/325905969/1254848285?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjMwYzRjZS1kODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TqH8BLamwHj8hU86riwHYeRXyZ2WBzDpfbl3QnIDxmU=&emci=b3412f44-d177-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&emdi=8230c4ce-d877-ec11-94f6-c896650d4442&ceid=23594672) | |
---
Peter, on this day we reflect on the tireless work spearheaded by Martin Luther King Jr.

While we recognize the amazing accomplishments he brought forth, we also need to understand the failures that we, as a country, have endured since then.

Since 2014, we have seen an increase in claims of “voter fraud”, an alarming growth in restrictive voting laws, and a movement to deprive the people of resources and voting locations. **This is unacceptable.** Not only does this disproportionately impact minority communities, but it also impacts poor and rural communities like my own.

This is exactly why my campaign has prioritized voter registration since **DAY ONE.** We are the only campaign currently registering voters in Northwest Georgia. Making sure that our communities are heard and supported is **how we are going to win this race against Marjorie Taylor Greene. **

We have identified **15,000 likely Democratic voters** in District that are unregistered. As money has come in from our grassroots outreach, we have put that money to use by mailing out our initial round of over 5,000 voter registration packets earlier this month. **[Today, we’re asking you to chip in to support our rural, minority, and disenfranchised communities](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40779135/325905970/-804235033?refcode=em0117&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjMwYzRjZS1kODc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TqH8BLamwHj8hU86riwHYeRXyZ2WBzDpfbl3QnIDxmU=)[. name: Greg Stanton
subject: Honoring Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
body:
Friend,

Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. dedicated his life to building a more just, equitable America. Our country is better today because of him.

Now, more than ever, we must work to carry on his legacy.

One of Martin Luther King Jr.'s most important objectives was to expand and protect voting rights across the country. The Voting Rights Act of 1965 was a result of his extraordinary work as part of the Civil Rights Movement to ensure all Americans have the opportunity to cast their vote at the ballot box.

The mantle has fallen on us now to continue the fight for voting rights. That's why I'm committed to passing the Freedom To Vote Act and John Lewis Voting Rights Act as soon as possible and continuing Martin Luther King Jr.'s work for a fairer, more just democracy.

I hope you'll join me in that effort as we celebrate the life of Dr. King and carry on his dream of freedom and equality for all people.

In solidarity,

--Greg

------------------------------------------
| Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
------------------------------------------

Stanton for Congress
4340 E Indian School Rd.
Suite 21-518
Phoenix AZ 85018 United States

name: Dr. Neil Shah For Governor
subject: Meet Me This Week + Fox Business Interview
body:
| Peter -- With overwhelming evidence that Omicron is 90% less harmful than other variants, and that vaccines are less effective with each passing day, what do the career politicians in Saint Paul do? Instead of admitting they were wrong, they continue stunting your children’s development by moving to remote learning, and they continue turning our state into an economic dead zone with Vaxports. Check out my latest Fox Business interview on my commitment to kick these criminals out of office, restore science, and deliver school choice to the state of Minnesota. [](https://electneil.nationbuilder.com/r?u=iUaD1gacZHU3dYzSqkV0rE6mdqyyFRZww70W8qFtwynagOodvsvpmUNJDsnNuEtXlQ9IWTT7BTPpfqLqUXilY1Htj8nnFAbzZPoADkQ58CH4nRiL-z_X4xUjwF9kpeLVMHFS-gczHhsyn4bsMJRUpQ&e=d39b8e369eb2bbdd6f36e913aaadeb34&utm_source=electneil&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fox_business&n=1) **If you have had enough like I have - Please Get Involved.** Go to caucus on February 1st, sign up to be a delegate, and vote for me in the straw poll. Use Caucusfinder.com to find your caucus location and attend! **If you Want to have a say in who will be the next governor of our great state, you MUST be a delegate.** Sign up at your local precinct caucus Here are some opportunities to meet me and compare the candidates this week: Let's #SaveSota! Dr. Neil Shah Conservative Candidate for Governor electneil.com

name: Mia McLeod
subject: Dr. King's legacy demands courageous leadership
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mia for South Carolina
body:
Dr. King's dream is not fully realized. | | | As we pause to remember Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. I’m reminded of his powerful legacy and the questions he posed to all of us when he said: _“Cowardice asks the question, is it safe? Expediency asks the question, is it politic? Vanity asks the question, is it popular? But conscience asks the question, is it right? And there comes a time when one must take a position that is neither safe, nor politic, nor popular, but one must take it because it is right.”_ Never before in our lifetimes have the questions Dr. King posed been more pivotal or more resonant. **Dr. King's dream is not fully realized — and this work is not done. Today, one of the ideals he fought so hard for — equal access to the ballot box — is at risk, friend.** Republican-led states, like South Carolina, are passing a wave of laws suppressing the right to vote — especially making it more difficult for Black voters to cast their ballot and make their voice heard in their government. We are being set back decades by harsh voter suppression laws, partisan gerrymandering and inflammatory rhetoric. **South Carolina’s Republican leadership clearly wants to be a part of the problem. And while I’ll proudly be a part of the solution as your governor, the truth is we can’t afford to wait.** **[So to honor Dr. King’s legacy, I’m using my voice to support the advancement of federal voting rights legislation before it’s too late. If you’re with me, add your name to my petition demanding Congress pass voting rights legislation without delay.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnlK8ctzsQ7z0kCcDcOVXIhUPmDht4Pine1SAmSjmMEsKfUVD5rQbS223FunOZdcEEXdqGubpqf_Jc_kDD5m9a7MJvgLtmPC9lnZ0pK9aQC75IkxSvlg2VOfCge5FnFd6PSi74qMUrYLPfFtq4bBhgbFddmPPD6jYDoxuh1mEHs6SmXtyjhOigtbRHrCn4ut_m0tqffYfitFfwQvfIiD-6dGkBpi3SovS40BsUUpE8up53CVM7FTPeFTjvTwKRzips5NxZFKHcydl2VYzEGnkJLcTKlRAvtEdvVfJNKGPnZZVpJoQbZnb0w7pQBOzf063cDodb9IdDvvH-dyU82hB9xWagaeb14HiLTILl_Gj3gOGEdUmSmbFzmzftEER177vM0/3is/W-2i0zdwRSiGAoC5omzu0Q/h0/nZ6y8WuSm3FSMdAj89KXh1w9DNoC7-IIbp4aA8i4A7I)** | | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnlK8ctzsQ7z0kCcDcOVXIhUPmDht4Pine1SAmSjmMEsKfUVD5rQbS223FunOZdcEEXdqGubpqf_Jc_kDD5m9a7MsITssMe-O8HVSwunxDAn4ruNyiDqpHG9QLiYZym7vY0GhlFrtO7pX_KvGCSw0bUOSPPr_q3Vt9Krsh2NUUBYo2i29UJo5dBguBSGs_lEwYdOBFQOVE25Sjrra_f-FqUw7Py3kNEqxpwetiAbysic_h4_BjLAhASAGS1ukYsxEZgMsYjj_qyr5PY2L_fgz6-tWdw4yiadgBRQPp6f4JTm99AHDB08QhX3jyxamkwoz55PtOtDXhiVEhgDVCPkwuQc0gU2YMPcndCCteKsjSbZosSZbpKNiCPRERyK__AFEvQ/3is/W-2i0zdwRSiGAoC5omzu0Q/h1/66DYcLE-8ytIpDpNwgZE3NM05xxkJGV_P9JavKB8LhY)

Dr. King believed that no act of service was too small.
The time is now. And what we’re fighting for is right and just. Together, we can finish strong and make Dr. King’s Dream a reality for ALL of us.
In solidarity,

clean_body: Dr. King's dream is not fully realized. | | | As we pause to remember Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. I’m reminded of his powerful legacy and the questions he posed to all of us when he said: “Cowardice asks the question, is it safe? Expediency asks the question, is it politic? Vanity asks the question, is it popular? But conscience asks the question, is it right? And there comes a time when one must take a position that is neither safe, nor politic, nor popular, but one must take it because it is right.” Never before in our lifetimes have the questions Dr. King posed been more pivotal or more resonant. Dr. King's dream is not fully realized — and this work is not done. Today, one of the ideals he fought so hard for — equal access to the ballot box — is at risk, friend. Republican-led states, like South Carolina, are passing a wave of laws suppressing the right to vote — especially making it more difficult for Black voters to cast their ballot and make their voice heard in their government. We are being set back decades by harsh voter suppression laws, partisan gerrymandering and inflammatory rhetoric. South Carolina’s Republican leadership clearly wants to be a part of the problem. And while I’ll proudly be a part of the solution as your governor, the truth is we can’t afford to wait. | | Dr. King believed that no act of service was too small. The time is now. And what we’re fighting for is right and just. Together, we can finish strong and make Dr. King’s Dream a reality for ALL of us. In solidarity, — Mia Please, disregard if you are a registered lobbyist. | | | Paid for by Mia for South Carolina | | Sent via . name: Cheri Beasley
subject: Honoring Dr. King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
body:
In this moment, where the threats to our democracy seem so great, I am reminded of Dr. King’s words: “The time is always right to do what’s right.”
#

Friend -
Today, we recognize a man who is remembered for his commitment to civil rights, human rights, voting rights, fair wages, access to health care and the dignity of work.
While Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. is remembered for his unwavering commitment, he indeed sacrificed his life for our democracy.
Dr. King knew that a country is not truly free or democratic when not all people enjoy freedom or democracy.
He knew we cannot celebrate a strong economy when people work two and three jobs to make ends meet and corporations profit as people struggle.
He knew that we cannot say our elected leaders represent the will of the people when voters are disenfranchised and excluded.
Yet here we are in 2022, still fighting for jobs. We’re still fighting for freedom. And we are still fighting for the right to vote.
Forty-nine states across the nation – including North Carolina — are attacking our right to vote in a desperate attempt to cling to political power. Meanwhile, we’re seeing Washington fail us by refusing to pass common-sense legislation that would protect the right to vote and help secure our elections.
Let’s be clear: the right to vote is about more than ballots being counted.
It’s about reasonably accessing the ballot box and ensuring our voices are heard.
The right to vote is about the dignity of a day’s work and the morality of a decent wage.
The right to vote is about making sure small business owners get a fair shot to grow, thrive and create jobs.
The right to vote is about your right to live in a world where you feel safe.
It’s about holding politicians accountable when they make it more difficult for you to take care of your families, earn a living wage, pay your student loans, afford your health care, or protect your planet.
We all know that when our votes are counted, our voices are heard, and change will come. And that is exactly why they are trying to stop us.
They aren’t just afraid we’ll vote – they are afraid we will win.
But make no mistake about it, we are going to win this fight.
In this moment, where the threats to our democracy seem so great, I am reminded of Dr. King’s words: “The time is always right to do what’s right.”
We cannot give up. We must assure that America keeps her promise.
Today, let us go forward with grace and determination to live up to the values of Dr. King and his call to each and every one of us.
Let us turn the spirit and energy of this day into the action and purpose we take into the days that follow.
Thank you,
Cheri
**[SIGN UP TO VOLUNTEER](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/323?source=em220117-1-ma&t=1&akid=270%2E357359%2EcGkYKZ)**

Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
PO Box 28778
Raleigh, NC 27611
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you want to receive fewer emails, you may [submit this form to get only our most important updates](https://act.cheribeasley.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=270%2E357359%2EcGkYKZ). Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts, but if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.cheribeasley.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=270%2E357359%2EcGkYKZ). If you would like to support Cheri's campaign for the U.S. Senate in North Carolina, please consider [making a donation](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beasley-em-2021?refcode=em-footer&t=4&refcode2=270_357359_cGkYKZ&akid=270%2E357359%2EcGkYKZ) today to help us make history.

name: First Lady Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, Florida is the freest state in the United States.
[View this message in your browser](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zfjj1a7l6q4ngeusnkd229plhjq5hak2p2illpd0_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jk5p7atdge5p78d5jmeqjetjjkorrbdt469r31d5jekrq9onicbo69pj0s8qbepj1e5im8dblmtim2s5lm6r7ccj1eeom4o3ks9g69d9mmjin1pjgcv970sj46oob4pjiegonco70mhij1o5l65iecpj66lgb6oj0e8qmcd34mdgefc5lclp238h1e8he4933c5h62pb5c1i68oh5u)
[****](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zjb12cfcgc68hrfssjusa1i6hbde4ed2388uptq0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z4hb8rse60m53jslh7esc2qfqoobed4rpvl9ktl0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zl2jtifevtrtq8mpbvh0ik6ahi4do4pvbdo5s7f8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-hf")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**
[**Please rush in a contribution to help us continue to hold the line and keep Florida free. Chip in $20.22, $30, or $50 now!**](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2ztqigfo1tsd213rqeg01c27gluhevnhnhmgnd78g_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs)
Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

[](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zrv8203qg7gnrs09p40e3osi7oq5t9v3kucsnir0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs "CHIP IN NOW") [CHIP IN NOW](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z8ggeqmitp2i71ol9g093npucd0ghe7k3pc87rqo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5imao34chh2bs)
---

Paid by Ron DeSantis, Republican, for Governor

Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. Platt Street, Suite 345, Tampa, FL 33606

If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for Trumptrainnews, [please use this preferences page](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/prefs/9z2zo8kpltvkq5ip1k96t51hmifnc7q13i07ksa4qco).

name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: MLK Day, CRT & What I Will Do On Day 1 In DC
body:
MLK Day & CRT It's Martin Luther King Jr. Day! Dr. King led the way in the 20th Century of fighting for equality in America. Unfortunately, in the 2
| | [](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1817.1.882ba30a28488ecc2827b3fc9c33cb11) |
---
## MLK Day & CRT
---
It's **Martin Luther King Jr. Day**! Dr. King led the way in the 20th Century of fighting for _equality_ in America. Unfortunately, in the 21st Century _segregation_ has crept its' way back into our society with **COVID19 Vaccine passports**. _Critical Race Theory_ is proof that the fight against **racism** is still one we must engage in today. We need to carry on Dr. King’s legacy of not judging an individual by the color of their skin (or _vaccine status_) but by the **content of their character**.
---
| [ ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1818.1.cd0dc3314e9013dbc68217994c782b11) https://twitter.com/JacksonLahmeyer/status/1466444006502580231?s=20
---
As a father of 5 amazing kids, every decision I make filters through that paradigm.
When deciding what to do first when elected to the US Senate it was a _quite easy decision_. **I believe that parents should raise their kids not the government.**
Therefore, on day one **I will sponsor legislation to ban all federal funds to any public school that teaches Critical Race Theory** in the classroom. Our classrooms should equip students to learn to read, write and do math instead of teaching one another to hate based on skin color.
I will work with a member of the house to sponsor similar legislation to expediate this important move to protect our children.
Also, if you live in Oklahoma and would be willing to host me at your home or business with 10+ friends for a meet & greet in 2022 that would be incredibly helpful. If you live outside of Oklahoma but would be willing to host a fundraiser that would be great. To arrange a meet & greet or a fundraiser please email caitlin@jacksonlahmeyer.com The legendary, Roger Stone, who is leading the #LahmeyerForSenate campaign will be with me for a round of private meet & greets throughout Tulsa & Oklahoma City between January 29 - February 2. If you would like more details on attending one of those events email caitlin@jacksonlahmeyer.com Thank you,
Jackson Lahmeyer Follow me on [Facebook](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1819.1.f664fba3a732ba10a1fb7e4f6a4f1d6c), [Instagram](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1820.1.e1220215b8261e0d5beb259aafc76897), [Twitter](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1821.1.c0c06a18100f6473188057c915c36185), [Telegram](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1822.1.00a1b1826186fbb58d717620f8ecfee6), GETTR & [YouTube](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1823.1.9ea84ffc882e48c4d21e48754a7f1742)).
---
| | [ Click Here To Fight To Save America ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1824.1.44c15527b7b23e4b4bf2b6961b9a598e)
---
---
## Updates:
---
| [ ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1825.1.7dc0e180ee3941e5970145db4da9eba4)
---
I spent this weekend in _Phoenix, Arizona_ at the **Re-Awaken America Tour** event and I had some incredible conversations with many great heroes such as **Eric Trump** , **General Michael Flynn** and **Mike Lindell**.
_Mike Lindell_ wanted me to specifically let you know how grateful he is for the support great Americans like you have shown him and [MyPillow](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1826.1.660342911c42188068eae3de946f8de9) over the last year in this fight to #SaveAmerica.
Right now, the Patriot movement is under attack, and we must stand **UNITED**! We have to recognize this is a fight between good and evil.
**Mike Lindell** has just found out that his bank, Minnesota Bank & Trust, has **cancelled the accounts of his business and his charities**.
This is UNBELIEVABLE!
We need to **support Mike in this fight**.
We must use our **3 Ts** to fight back:
1\. **Time** \- Attend events with other like-minded Patriots such as the Re-Awaken Tour (Ohio in February, San Diego in March. Get more info at www.timetofreeamerica.com )
2\. **Talent** \- Use the gifts and abilities that God has given you to contribute to saving of America. _America needs you_!
3\. **Treasure** \- We must stop funding Deep State companies and start funing America First companies. Every dollar we spend is a bullet in this war.
**If we will fight, we will win.**
It is very important that we continue to support **Mike Lindell** in this battle. His business, [MyPillow](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1827.1.c7f6c7ee027b6b00dfc2086028643204), has been cancelled by all the big box stores and now his bank because of his boldness about the 2020 election and it's up to us to make the decision to not FUND Deep State Companies but to FUND America First Companies such as [MyPillow](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1828.1.58a5f96e3b244386bca8a693da1cd1ae).
I want to encourage you to visit [www.MyPillow.com](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1829.1.288c124f4905f74036a91e00485335ae) and look at all of the great America First products available (much more than just great pillows).
We are at war right now. Spend wisely. Spend America First.
Use the Promo Code **JACKSON** at checkout to save up to 66% while shopping at [www.MyPillow.com](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1830.1.d7e3b83412dfce5df286c681f23e7ee3)
Kendra and I love all of our [MyPillow](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1831.1.7174972e68961969f7539989b83a1cb6) products and we TOTALLY support Mike Lindell! I hope you will too...
| [ ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/375205?id=2866266.1832.1.63137d5c2b2135180ba1e6c8a3b03314)

| | Today, we honor the legacy of the great Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. The Civil Rights leader fought to end racial inequality, end segregation, and usher in a brighter future for our nation with his unwavering commitment to justice. His powerful work led us out of a dark chapter in our nation’s history and continues to resonate today as we fight for our nation’s promise of “liberty and justice for all.” I’m reflecting on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s significant contributions to racial equity and civil rights as well as how each of us can carry the torch of his legacy by championing equality and challenging injustice at every turn. Let’s keep up the fight, Steve

name: Jack Ballard
subject: MLK Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JACK BALLARD FOR MONTANA
body:
Folks, I’m writing to you all on a very special day: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day. Dr. King was an exceptional American who preached equality, civi | | | [](http://links.ballardformontana.com/u/click?_t=01481013522b4e28a72d84f828a757ef&_m=0b955d9cd4414405903dd643d793a158&_e=SyiTAKhD1Iw84VBQBVegxSDu0Y4PEh2SqTEDCps_R8m8iUdQSvAyWPAD9CDhPry3M_vpKXA2B4Gir0oFa5LPdj2gK1Ja0XCIsSxD7PJqyUYmxJYH08jqs0TPa_20v4HB69U6QIJ3RILvCxJqd92g-bg-2Ga_DAArg0okaIhUaRzclU1TlsDLZcCksskTO8_idNnX7jfBdGctecHgH8qkTxR2mJvXxDv5Tlyy4JWnPsPFfu0Ga6Mme0E2TIkcTTeSG503lGAK4LJvarVWA_icEA%3D%3D) Folks, I’m writing to you all on a very special day: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day. Dr. King was an exceptional American who preached equality, civil rights, and justice and it’s incredibly important that we take a moment today to honor his life, legacy, and accomplishments. Although we still have a long way to go before we achieve the truly equal and just America that Dr. King envisioned, we can honor him every day by trying to work toward that goal. I encourage you to take a moment today to reflect on Dr. King’s contribution to this country, and to use the day as a chance to love someone, see the good in someone, or help someone in need. After all, that’s what today is all about. Very best, Jack

PAID FOR BY JACK BALLARD FOR MONTANA.

| Team, the Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. had a profound impact on our country.

One of the causes he fought so passionately for was voting rights. It is because of Dr. King’s efforts that the landmark Voting Rights Act of 1965 was signed into law.

Unfortunately, Dr. King’s voting rights work still continues to this day, decades later, as communities of color continue to be disenfranchised at the polls.

**Today, on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we honor Dr. King’s memory by continuing his efforts to make voting accessible for everyone.**

Through year-round organizing, we are reaching all of Arizona’s communities – Black, white, Native, and more – and encouraging voter registration and participation.

Hopefully, one day, we can say that the fight for equal voting rights is over. Until then, we will continue Dr. King’s work.

Sincerely,

Arizona Democrats

| The Arizona Democratic Party is working around the clock to ensure Democratic candidates are elected up and down the ballot — candidates who will fight for what YOU value most, like affordable healthcare for every Arizonan, economic stability and job opportunities for working families, and representation for the most marginalized among us. **Help us continue to build a party that is fighting for all Arizonans. Become a monthly donor.**

name: Marco Rubio
subject: Please forgive me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for Senate
body:
Friend, please excuse how blunt I’m about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...
[View this message in your browser](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zlnoap9vopckc7ikrjfd3sbukrh3tik7e95vn3q8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im1or2cjh238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kkpqedtge1p7adjkm4qj7cbkmgor79hnmmrq8cjm62qb6t9nkjhf4onie1jm4phieeoncd9jelpn5cb6mcqb4t9ie3hr6d9gc6q66p5j6uo6ct9jmeo7fduie1p64pjgm9j71p5jcdjr4cj66libbd37m9p6cclgm9j61o5lcdge1d9h66qj3dbi4no)
Friend, please excuse how blunt I’m about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...
|
---
| |
| Friend, please excuse how blunt I'm about to be, but desperate times call for desperate measures. Listen...

I've begged.
I've pleaded.
I've humbly asked.
I've even listed out the stakes - I'm the most vulnerable Republican Senator running for re-election, my race will determine control of the Senate.

_**All this - and you STILL haven 't stepped up.**_

Maybe you haven't stepped up because you think Florida is a shoo-in but that couldn't be further from the truth. Florida's last Senate election went to a recount and Republicans BARELY won. My race is virtually tied, Friend, I'm depending on you...

I'll ask one more time, can I count on you? I really **really** need this year to start off strong.

**[SEND DESPERATELY NEEDED HELP>>](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zrinkh2dnvlpqb78c9ldtf727tc8fe6a5fpnmsf8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im1or2cjh2bs)**

Thank you for your support,

Marco Rubio
---
| |
* * *
---
| | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zv56d8spfjcme70ibjdcqucbgtlhr0peb443em30_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im1or2cjh2bs)
---
| _A personal note from Marco Rubio_ :

Friend, did you see what my former Trump Impeachment Manager opponent said about our race?

Demings is a do-nothing Democrat with a laughable record. Schumer and his cronies are propping her up with their multi-million dollar warchests all because they know she'll be a RUBBER STAMP for their radical agenda. If we let up and the Democrats win in Florida, they will KEEP control of the Senate and continue to force their dangerous agenda through Congress.

It is up to us to stop this, Friend. I need each and every patriot who receives this to join me in the fight to take back the Senate.

If you stand with me, **[please click here to donate now.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zr5jcb7rpk3uqsk5gdg37e032h7ogln7lql86nm0_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im1or2cjh2bs)**

Thank you for everything you do,

Marco Rubio

name: Casey DeSantis
subject: Freedom is Prevailing in Florida
body:
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis,
[****](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2zt3tshhvm4t8s8lrldj5kp4huk2gjja378dn57l8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs)
Friend,
I am grateful every single day to live in Florida. Thanks to the leadership of our great governor and my husband, Ron DeSantis, **Florida is the freest state in the United States.**
[**CHIP IN $20.22 TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS CONTINUE THE FIGHT FOR FLORIDA >>>**](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2zknbs8auvp4g0do3q6p0560bil1jmq5ec0ep682o_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs)
But across the country, that freedom has come under assault as politicians have cast aside their citizens' liberty. Thankfully, Florida has a governor who stands courageously in the face of sharp opposition to protect our freedom. Thanks to his leadership, Floridians have a right to earn a living without heavy-handed mandates and kids have an opportunity to thrive.
Over the last three years, Governor DeSantis led Florida to record-breaking economic success, which has become the envy of America. Our tax burden is the lowest per capita in the nation, our job growth far outpaces the national average, we are leading the nation in business formations, and our tourism industry is soaring even beyond its pre-pandemic levels. Despite this, federal policies are creating negative headwinds. To that end, Governor DeSantis has proposed a $1 billion gas tax holiday to help Floridians combat rising inflation and gas prices.
With politicians in Democrat-controlled cities and states AGAIN making the disastrous decision to close schools, our governor continues to stand strong for parents and students to keep schools open! We have a governor who is committed to protecting parental rights -- he believes parents must have a say in their child's education, from the curriculum to the decision to vaccinate their child against COVID.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP GOVERNOR DESANTIS KEEP FLORIDA FREE!**](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2zahnfiktuconmgagfsnf55ok74m98j7f6ejpsmg8_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs "https://secure.winred.com/rondesantis/freedom-is-prevailing-fl-hf")
Governor DeSantis has banned Critical Race Theory in our schools. He believes that if a child is subjected to any hateful or discriminating propaganda in school, parents should have legal recourse.
As a husband and father, he knows the importance of keeping our communities safe, which is why he has made it clear that Florida stands for law and order. In addition to the $1,000 bonus checks he awarded to first responders last year, he's calling on the legislature to not only issue another round of bonuses this year but to increase pay for state law enforcement by up to 25% and to provide $5,000 bonuses for new officers, including those already serving in other states who want to relocate to Florida.
Keeping our communities safe also requires enforcing our border laws. With the federal government's indifference towards enforcing the rule of law, our governor is taking action. Governor DeSantis banned sanctuary cities and was the first governor to send reinforcements to Texas to help combat the surge taking place at our southern border. He's fought to stop clandestine flights that drop off illegal immigrants in Florida. He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable.
He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude.
Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems.
Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become.
Get ready, Florida - ** _Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you._**
[**Please rush in a contribution to help us continue to hold the line and keep Florida free. Chip in $20.22, $30, or $50 now!**](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2z537kba79ud4f6pjssa9uu22f3qcei4q7ms7ijpg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs)
Sincerely,
Casey DeSantis

[](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2zdb0pv0d5j4bkrdv19behdqnms45ssgl53hbrl08_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs "CHIP IN NOW") [CHIP IN NOW](http://e.selfreliancecentral.com/rd/9z2zea24i74leu4va0bnmfg8dm45ue3el669gk0m268_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pj4c5h2bs)

He is also introducing proposals to stop these relocation efforts and is holding those who help facilitate such underhanded policies accountable. He understands that enforcing a strong border means protecting our communities by keeping drugs off our streets and out of our children's hands, and he refuses to allow the federal government's human smuggling operation to go on unchallenged. Florida's citizens shouldn't be forced to bear the burden of the federal government's immigration ineptitude. Florida counted over 11 million ballots on Election Day. Other states took days, if not weeks, to return ballots, ultimately casting doubt on the integrity of our electoral process. To ensure Florida continues to lead, Governor DeSantis banned "Zuckerbucks," ballot harvesting, the mass sending of mail-in ballots and he strengthened our voter ID laws. He's prepared to take additional measures this legislative session to further ensure Floridians have confidence in our election systems. Florida is in a better place today because of our governor, but his work isn't finished. With an election coming up this November, I know that with your support, together we can continue building upon the rock of freedom Florida has become. Get ready, Florida - Governor DeSantis has only just begun to fight for you. Sincerely, Casey DeSantis "CHIP IN NOW" Paid by Ron DeSantis, Republican, for Governor Ron DeSantis for Governor | 301 W. subject: Studying his accomplishments is needed
body:
You can now own one of Hillsdale College’s most popular online courses, “Winston Churchill and Statesmanship,” on DVD.
| _Sponsored message_ |
---|---|---
This sponsored message from American Action News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z3ikaacqv1qmobetl38m4957lbh01d5jacfhk8ig_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pb1c1h2bs) Dear Fellow American, You can now own one of Hillsdale College’s most popular online courses, **“Winston Churchill and Statesmanship,”** on DVD. Thousands of people have enjoyed this in-depth course online since its release, _**and now you can add it to your personal library or give it as a gift.**_ In recent years Winston Churchill, who is best known for his leadership during World War II, has been portrayed in numerous films and television shows, proving without a doubt that Americans find the “British Bulldog” captivating. The reasons for this sustained interest in Churchill are profound. No statesman has better diagnosed the threat of modern technology; no statesman has spoken more effectively in opposition to socialism; and, no statesman has displayed more courage in the face of tyranny. It should come as little surprise that so many “academics” and activists are working to smear his legacy, which makes studying a true account of Churchill’s accomplishments more important than ever. **For precisely this reason, I am so excited to offer you this powerful DVD set:** **_[https://secure.hillsdale.edu/winston-churchill-dvd-offer/](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zg301bt0l6aojv9k98gfifate5a5m8eg0t7c73mo_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pb1c1h2bs)_** As a student of Dr. Arnn myself, I know that his lectures on Churchill will teach you more about this subject than any of the many books and videos available to you, and they will reveal profound lessons that can be applied to the urgent problems of today. [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zejshlsmesu1k9stodg4il43dgnin65d1auvchpg_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pb1c1h2bs) Only a limited number of these DVD sets are available, so I hope you’ll take this opportunity to add it to your personal library or give it as a gift—before they’re gone. Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of **“Winston Churchill and Statesmanship** ” on DVD: _[**https://secure.hillsdale.edu/winston-churchill-dvd-offer/**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zp8mtkc02jbd5u7a52murb635asm1ugen93hdjho_rp22sh2s8i66ob2c5im9pb1c1h2bs)_ You’ll undoubtedly enjoy this close study of Churchill’s words and deeds.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, ‘09 Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Abel=20Mulugheta?=
subject: Honoring Dr. King Today
body:
We must honor Dr. King today, not through words, but with actions. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/abelfortexas.com/honoring-dr-king-today?e=2dc92facb0)
---
|
---
Peter, today I join millions of Americans in celebrating the life of Dr. Martin Luther King. While we celebrate him, it’s important that we not lose sight of what Dr. King was fighting for decades ago. He was fighting against segregation and injustice, a fight that continues to this day. There is no better evidence of this fight than the voter suppression legislation we have seen that seeks to disenfranchise black and brown people.We must not just celebrate today through words, but through actions that honor the legacy and work of Dr. King. At the federal level we have the opportunity to act and guarantee the right to vote for millions of Americans, but too many in Washington are unwilling to confront the urgency of this situation and take action. I take umbrage with these politicians who will be honoring Dr. King today with press releases and tweets, but not with action. We may be experiencing a setback today, but our faith and commitment to voting rights and equality must not waver.Let’s remember Dr. King today through acts of service that honor his legacy and uplift our communities, and by vowing to never quit on our march towards securing the right to vote for all Americans.Your friend,
| | | | | |
---|---|---
| | |
---

name: Matt Brown and Cynthia Mendes
subject: Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Matt Brown for Governor and Friends of Cynthia
body:
In 1963, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. wrote a letter from a Birmingham,
Alabama jail cell after his arrest while fighting to end segregation. The
letter came in response to criticism that Dr. King would have the audacity
to break the law in protest.

But Dr. King rejected that idea. In his letter, he pleaded for direct
action against racist and unjust laws. He also denounced the “white
moderate, who is more devoted to ‘order’ than to justice; who prefers a
negative peace which is the absence of tension to a positive peace which
is the presence of justice.”

Those words still ring true, all these years later.

Dr. King’s commitment to racial and economic justice continues to be a
guiding light. And it’s why we must keep fighting.

We’re fighting for a government that can build a truly just
state.

We must fight back against the legacy of injustice — decades of neglect
and economic divestment from Black communities, a criminal justice system
that disproportionately punishes Black Rhode Islanders, a climate crisis
and pandemic that hits Black communities hardest, and so much more.

We all have a duty to help realize Dr. King’s dream for true racial and
economic justice. To do that, we must keep fighting for a multi-racial
government that can bring dignity and justice to all of us.

We hope you have a restful and reflective holiday, and come tomorrow, we’ll
be right here, ready to fight alongside anyone who is up to the challenge
of changing the system for the better.

With gratitude and solidarity,

Matt Brown and Cynthia Mendes

---

Today we recognize, remember, and celebrate the life of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Dr. King was a trailblazer for the advancements of civil rights for Black Americans. As time has gone on, his record has been white-washed to fit the narratives of too many who would rather be comfortable with their own version of who they think Dr. King was — and they’ve gotten away without taking any meaningful action to actually honor the work he did. **People cannot quote or praise Dr. King in the same breath that they defend their opposition to expanding voting rights and passing voting rights legislation.** As his daughter Bernice King has said — “enact policies that reflect your birthday sentiments”. **That is why we must pass the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act.** After the Supreme Court gutted crucial parts of the Voting Rights Act, many states have gone to extremes to make sure they can suppress the vote as much as possible — whether it's been by enacting voter ID laws, purging voters from their rolls, gerrymandering, outright voter intimidation, or other ways in which they’ve made voting even more difficult than it already is. These bills will help end racial gerrymandering, secure the right to vote for millions of people, and work to end voter suppression across the country. The House has passed the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Amendment Act. Now, two Democrats and all of the Republicans in the Senate are standing in the way by arguing to preserve an arcane rule that has been used to STOP civil rights legislation from passing. We must abolish the filibuster and pass this voting rights legislation. Without it, we will continue to see an undoing of what voting rights we DO have and further erosion of our democracy. Abolishing the filibuster and passing these two bills is just one of the ways we can truly honor the legacy of Dr. King and take real action. In solidarity, Bruno

name: Jessica Mason
subject: The third Monday of every January
body:
Dr. King’s legacy and beyond.

| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40780399/325913366/-1288899752?refcode=220117_CLTV_mlkday_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTM0OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYWRlZDY5MS1lYjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vHSPdldCzyM7JpsjOdjiCJLB5S9xdtUfG3-pExx84dU=)
---
Hello Team New Deal!

I hope that you had a relaxing, long weekend. But as we close out the day, let’s take a moment to remember why this third Monday of every January is a national holiday to begin with:

Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was an iconic legend, best known for his achievements in advancing the civil rights movement. Thanks to Dr. King’s work, we have the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and the Voting Rights Act of 1965. _**But his legacy goes far beyond policy.**_

MLK Jr. remains an honored public figure decades later due to his powerful speeches and sermons that championed peace, non-violence, and humanity. That was the root of his dedication to equality, and it’s what made him stand out as a national treasure.

Growing up as a little Black girl in a poor neighborhood, Dr. King was one of the first historic African Americans we learned about in school, and I continue to be inspired by his work today. He preached the notion that every single human has the _**same inherit value**_ regardless of skin color, race, and individual identity. Income, status, job: **none of that changes our worth.** He opened our minds and our hearts to the hopeful vision of a world where discrimination no longer exists — a fight that persists in our current societal and political atmosphere.

Today, I attended the NAACP Martin Luther King Day Parade in his honor, and it was such a sincere joy to see all of the people who carry on this great man's memory today.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40780400/325913367/-1288899752?refcode=220117_CLTV_mlkday_IMG1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTM0OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYWRlZDY5MS1lYjc3LWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDQ0NDIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vHSPdldCzyM7JpsjOdjiCJLB5S9xdtUfG3-pExx84dU=) Let’s never forget the important lessons that Dr. King taught us or lose sight of possibility that change _is_ possible when we use our collective voice. clean_body: Dr. King’s legacy and beyond. | | | = Hello Team New Deal! I hope that you had a relaxing, long weekend. But as we close out the day, let’s take a moment to remember why this third Monday of every January is a national holiday to begin with: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was an iconic legend, best known for his achievements in advancing the civil rights movement. Thanks to Dr. King’s work, we have the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and the Voting Rights Act of 1965. But his legacy goes far beyond policy. MLK Jr. remains an honored public figure decades later due to his powerful speeches and sermons that championed peace, non-violence, and humanity. That was the root of his dedication to equality, and it’s what made him stand out as a national treasure. Growing up as a little Black girl in a poor neighborhood, Dr. King was one of the first historic African Americans we learned about in school, and I continue to be inspired by his work today. He preached the notion that every single human has the same inherit value regardless of skin color, race, and individual identity. Income, status, job: none of that changes our worth. He opened our minds and our hearts to the hopeful vision of a world where discrimination no longer exists — a fight that persists in our current societal and political atmosphere. Today, I attended the NAACP Martin Luther King Day Parade in his honor, and it was such a sincere joy to see all of the people who carry on this great man's memory today. = Let’s never forget the important lessons that Dr. King taught us or lose sight of possibility that change is possible when we use our collective voice. Let's commemorate the achievements and lasting contributions of an outstanding man today (and every day) and continue to honor his legacy by fighting for justice with everything we've got. In solidarity, Jessica = ******************EXPRESS DONATE to Jessica Mason for Congress****************** ******************If you've stored payment info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:****************** | ******************= "="****************** | ******************= "="****************** | ******************= "="****************** | ******************= "="****************** ---|---|---|--- Jessica Mason was born and raised in Dallas and has experienced firsthand the socioeconomic barriers and systemic inequities that impede the success of Texas working families. As a former Navy medic, Jessica received her Master's Degree in Public Administration so that she could pursue her passion for public service. She's running for Congress to represent Texas' 30th District because she's tired of seeing everyday people left behind. TX-30 deserves policies that are designed for the success of the people who live here. That starts with new leadership that sees our humanness and understands our struggles. And that starts right here, with Jessica Mason. = ____= | || || ** Jessica Mason for Congress PO Box 271183 Dallas, TX 75227 United States | |

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: [Add your name] Fight for voting rights on MLK Jr. Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
This week is critical, Peter.
#
Peter,
On this Martin Luther King Jr. Day, I’m hoping that you’ll join me in the fight for voting rights legislation. A great way to honor the work of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. is recommitting to the effort to secure voting rights so everyone can have their voice heard and vote counted.
[**This week is an especially critical time to redouble our resolve for action. So, can you add your name to our petition calling on Congress to immediately pass historic voting rights legislation?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/voting-rights-2022/?source=em20220117-966&t=1&akid=966%2E420207%2ENktOV_)
**[Add your name](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/voting-rights-2022/?source=em20220117-966&t=2&akid=966%2E420207%2ENktOV_)**
The right to vote is a fundamental part of our democracy. But throughout America’s history, different groups of people have been barred from voting based on their race, gender, and economic status.
Today, Republican voter suppression laws and outdated voting practices continue to prevent people from voting — particularly Black and brown folks in states with Republican legislatures. If we are going to succeed in our fight for free and fair elections, we must succeed in guaranteeing the voting rights of BIPOC folks in Georgia, Texas, and every state in the country.
Whether it’s voter suppression, shutting down polling places, or taking people off of the voting rolls, people across the country are having their voting rights taken away. We can do something about that right now.
[**Will you join me and add your name to the call for voting rights legislation today? We must guarantee that everyone can have their vote counted, and that we have free and fair elections.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/voting-rights-2022/?source=em20220117-966&t=3&akid=966%2E420207%2ENktOV_)
Thank you for taking action today on such an important issue and at such a critical moment. Let’s protect our democracy today and always.
In solidarity,
Pramila

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20220117-966-footer&t=4&refcode2=966_420207_NktOV_&akid=966%2E420207%2ENktOV_)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

| Dear Patriot, They knew the truth all along, and they lied to you. **In February of 2020, the top scientists in the U.S. all agreed that the COVID-19 virus was almost certainly leaked from a lab in Wuhan, China.** The latest set of emails that have been released publicly make that clear. They knew this came from a lab. But somewhere along the way, something changed. It had nothing to do with science, as the emails make clear. But within months, these same scientists were calling the lab-leak hypothesis a "conspiracy theory" and working to discredit it. **I can only guess what might have taken place behind the scenes to cause them to change their tune.** Emails just a short amount of time later give clues as to what might have been behind this change. Ron Fouchier, a Dutch scientist, wrote that the lab-leak hypothesis might "do unnecessary harm to science in general and science in China in particular." clean_body: Did you miss my survey? 🚨 🚨 Peter, Our records indicate that you did NOT send in your response to my team’s new year survey. I’m ready to fight for Alabama and America, but I need to know what’s important to YOU before I can do that in the Senate. My team’s extending the deadline for our 2022 Priority Survey by just ⏰ 24 HOURS ⏰ WHAT ISSUE IS MOST IMPORTANT TO YOU IN 2022? This survey will EXPIRE in 24 hours. Taking just 15 seconds to send in your answer will shape the FUTURE of the Senate. Thank you, Katie Britt Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc.

name: Harmeet Dhillon
subject: We're suing Twitter and we need your help
body:
[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) Dear Peter, I'm not going to beat around the bush, we are living in a dystopian, tyrannical state. My team at the Center for American Liberty has recently uncovered evidence of powerful elected politicians colluding with Big Tech corporations like Facebook and Twitter to de-platform and silence conservatives like you and me. **In other words, we've caught big government using tax-payer funding to silence their political opponents.** And if someone does not immediately stand up against this, the freedoms enshrined in our Constitution will become nothing more than meaningless words on a piece of paper. That's why we launched a lawsuit against Twitter, U.S. Senator Alex Padilla, and a handful of liberal elected officials for their state-sponsored plot to eliminate our First Amendment liberties, _and we need your help to win._ **[Will you join us in our lawsuit against Twitter and Sen. Padilla and help defend the First Amendment?](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r)** Let me explain. Like millions of Americans after the 2020 presidential election, conservative activist Rogan O'Handley took to Twitter to express his concerns about the dubious election procedures and skepticism of the results. [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) Shortly after the election, many of Rogan's tweets were removed by Twitter and his account was permanently suspended for "violating the government's rules about election integrity." _Hopefully, I don't need to explain to you that censoring or de-platforming ANY American for their political beliefs or opinions is a gross violation of the First Amendment._ But it gets much worse. When Rogan tried to figure out why he was censored, he found that bureaucrats working for then-California Secretary of State, Alex Padilla, had explicitly instructed officials at Twitter to silence him. You see, Alex Padilla was being considered to fill Kamala Harris' vacated seat in the U.S. Senate, and he was afraid that Rogan's tweets would hurt his image and chances of being appointed. **So, the office of Alex Padilla immediately instructed his press secretary to contact Twitter and shut Rogan up.** **From:** California Secretary of State Communications Department **To:** Twitter **Date:** Tuesday, November 17th, 2020 at 12:31 PM Hi, We wanted to flag this Twitter post: [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) From user @DC_Draino. In this post user claims California of being a culprit of voter fraud, and ignores the fact that we do audit votes. This is a blatant disregard to how our voting process works and creates disinformation and distrust among the general public. Twitter then flagged the post as misinformation, deleted it, and -- a few weeks later -- permanently banned Rogan from the their site. [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) That's why my team and I at the Center for America Liberty are suing Twitter, now-Senator Alex Padilla, California Secretary of State Shirly Weber, and a handful of other liberals for their involvement in this blatant assault on the First Amendment. But the only way we can take on the deep pockets and deep connections of these tyrants is with the help of patriotic Americans like you. [**Will you help us fund this critical lawsuit by sending a generous, tax-deductible contribution of just $25, $35, $50 or more to the Center for American Liberty today?**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) My name is Harmeet Dhillon. Two years ago, I founded the Center for American Liberty with the purpose of protecting our First Amendment freedoms and civil liberties from being destroyed by today's left. We've taken on Governor Gavin Newsom in California when he unconstitutionally shut down small businesses, banned people from going to church, and kept millions of children locked up in their homes, out of school, during the pandemic. We've taken on Antifa for viciously assaulting and nearly killing journalist Ando Ngo who exposed Antifa's vile, anarchic plot to destroy the United States and turn it into a socialist utopia. **And now, we're taking on Twitter, Sen. Alex Padilla, and others for colluding to eliminate Rogan O'Handley's freedom of speech.** But as a non-profit organization, everything we do to defend our civil liberties is with the generous help of like-minded conservative patriots like you. And this massive lawsuit is expected to cost us over $1,000,000. _After all, we are taking a multi-billion-dollar company, the state of California, and a handful of well-connected politicians._ [**So, can I count on you to help us stand up to Twitter and Senator Alex Padilla by making a tax-deductible contribution of $25, $35, $50 or more to the Center for American Liberty today?**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) I'm not exaggerating when I say that this is the legal battle that will define our generation, and the future of our country. Will we sit back and allow elitist politicians, billion-dollar corporations, and faceless government agencies to continue to trample our constitutional freedoms? Or will we prove that WE, the American people, are still in control of our own lives? [**Please join us in this critical fight to defend the First Amendment by contributing $25, $35, $50 or more to the Center for American Liberty today.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) Sincerely, Harmeet K. Dhillon P.S. For four years, leftist politicians and mainstream media figures would not let go of the bogus and unproven accusation that President Trump colluded with Russia to "steal" the 2016 election. But now that there is clear evidence that a California elected official colluded with big tech to censor conservatives who wanted to comment on the 2020 election those same leftists are dead-silent. I am absolutely fed up with left's hypocrisy, double-standards, and blatantly unconstitutional behavior. [**That's why I'm urgently asking you to contribute $25, $35, $50 or more to the Center for American Liberty to help us fund our lawsuit against Twitter and Senator Alex Padilla. **](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) [**DONATE**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) [Please consider supporting our efforts to defend our Constitution with a donation today.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274791:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) The Center For American Liberty is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization created to fight against growing anti-free-speech and anti-civil liberties trends.
The Center for American Liberty | [www.libertycenter.org](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/59274792:6a8Vo-ENP:m:1:3253819041:B2E1F4C6297784764F1192C493905A82:r) PO Box 2510, Leesburg, VA 20177

name: =?utf-8?Q?Kathleen=20For=20Congress?=
subject: How to best honor Dr. King.
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/cc783f6f1fa3/how-to-best-honor-dr-king?e=4712f71bea)
---
|
---
[](https://kathleenfortx.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=09fca73837c97c9ddd5e5e266&id=40306732d9&e=4712f71bea)
|
---
I’m sure you’ll see a lot of emails today from people like me - Democrats and progressives running for elected office - and a lot of them will sound more or less the same: **We value Dr. King’s moving words, his tireless dedication to equality, and his love of country.** Just because you read words like that often today doesn’t make them any less true. **They are.** Dr. King was a uniquely American leader unwaveringly dedicated to **bending the moral arc of history towards justice.** But neither can we deny that too many in our country - _**today**_ \- are trying to bend the arc of history away towards inequality and oppression. While a few Republicans like **Liz Cheney, Adam Kinzinger** and others deeply care about the ongoing threats to American democracy, too many - **including** **my opponent Ronny Jackson** \- are in Donald Trump’s anti-democratic authoritarian death-grip.The only way out is to organize and elect more _small-d_ democrats, among whom I count myself a proud member. And while this is the point of the email when I usually ask for a small donation, that’s for tomorrow.Today, I think Dr. King’s legacy is best honored if I ask for your strength, your time, and your talent **to volunteer for my campaign in service of American democracy.** So please click below to make phone calls, knock on doors, or organize others.Thank you,
_Kathleen Brown_ _Candidate for Congress (D),__Texas 13th district._
| [Volunteer. Get Involved.](https://kathleenfortx.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=09fca73837c97c9ddd5e5e266&id=b5bcc36745&e=4712f71bea)
